eras.label: In the Beginning
eras.worldContext: Before anything existed, God was already there.

eras.label: The Age of the Patriarchs
eras.worldContext: The world's first great cities rose up. And God chose one family to change everything.

eras.label: The Exodus and Wilderness
eras.worldContext: Egypt was the most powerful country in the world. God was about to show everyone who was really in charge.

eras.label: The Promised Land
eras.worldContext: Israel was about to enter a new land. The big question was whether they would trust God.

eras.label: The Kingdom Era
eras.worldContext: Israel got the kings they asked for. Some were good. Some were not.

eras.label: Exile and Return
eras.worldContext: God's people were taken far from home. But God never left them.

eras.label: The Life of Jesus
eras.worldContext: Rome controlled the world. And into that world, God was born as a baby.

eras.label: Death, Resurrection and the Early Church
eras.worldContext: Jesus rose from the dead and told his friends to go tell the whole world. They did.

stories.title: In the Beginning
stories.hook: Before anything existed at all, God was already there. Then He spoke, and everything began.
story: Before anything existed, God was already there. There was no sky, no ground, no sun, no animals, and no people. Just God. And then God started making things.
He said, "Light!" And bright, beautiful light appeared. God looked at the light and loved it. He called the light Day and the darkness Night. That was the first day.
On the second day, God made the sky. On the third day, He made dry ground and oceans. He made every kind of plant and tree, with seeds and fruit and flowers. He looked at everything and loved it.

On the fourth day, God put the sun in the sky to shine during the day and the moon and stars to shine at night. On the fifth day, He filled the oceans with fish and the sky with birds. He loved everything He made and told them to have babies and fill the earth.

On the sixth day, God made animals. Every kind you can think of! Lions and elephants, dogs and ladybugs. Then God said something special that He had never said before. He said, "Let us make people, someone like us."

So God made a man and a woman. He looked at them and loved them very much. He told them, "Take care of everything I made. You are in charge." Then He looked at everything He had made and said it was very good. Not just good. Very good!
On the seventh day, God rested. He had finished everything He wanted to make, so He rested and called that day special.

Then God did something amazing with the man. He bent down close and breathed right into the man's nose. And the man came alive! God planted a beautiful garden called Eden and put the man there to take care of it.

God said, "It is not good for the man to be alone." So while the man was sleeping, God made a woman from one of his ribs. When the man woke up and saw her, he was so happy. The two of them were perfectly happy together, with God right there with them.

devotional.bigIdea: God made everything, including you, on purpose. You are the best thing He made, and He loves you very much.
questions: God made the whole world and said it was good. But when He made people, He said it was very good. Why do you think people are so special to God?
God breathed His own breath into the man to make him alive. What does that tell you about how much God cares about people?
God rested on the seventh day because His work was all done. What is your favorite thing to do when you rest?

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Jesus was there when God made everything. John says Jesus is the Word that created the whole world.

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: The same Spirit who was there at creation helped bring Jesus into the world.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: Paul says when someone follows Jesus they become a new creation, just like God made everything new in the beginning.

quiz.question: What were the very first words God said to make the world?
options: Let there be light
Let there be animals
Let there be people
Let there be water
quiz.explanation: God said "Light!" and bright, beautiful light appeared. That was the very first thing God made.

quiz.question: How did God make the man come alive?
options: He spoke his name out loud
He breathed into his nose
He touched him with fire
He sprinkled water on him
quiz.explanation: God bent down close and breathed right into the man's nose. That is how the man came alive.

quiz.question: What did God say about everything He made on the sixth day?
options: It was finished
It was pretty good
It was very good
It was enough
quiz.explanation: After making people, God looked at everything He had made and said it was very good. Not just good, but very good!

stories.title: The First Bad Choice
stories.hook: Adam and Eve lived in a perfect garden. God gave them one rule. Then a sneaky snake showed up.
story: Adam and Eve lived in a beautiful garden called Eden. God walked with them there like a close friend. They had everything they needed. Every kind of food, every kind of animal, and God right there with them.
God had given them one rule. He said, "You can eat from any tree in the garden except one. Do not eat from the tree in the middle of the garden. If you do, something very bad will happen."
One day, a sneaky snake came to Eve. The snake was tricky. He asked, "Did God really say you cannot eat from any tree?" He twisted the words to make God's rule sound mean and unfair.

Eve said they could eat from any tree except the one in the middle. The snake said, "You will not get in trouble! God knows that if you eat it, you will become very smart, just like Him." The snake wanted Eve to think God was hiding something good from her.
Eve looked at the fruit. It looked yummy. She wanted to be smart. So she took some and ate it. Then she gave some to Adam, who was standing right there, and he ate it too.

Right away, everything felt different. But not in a good way. They felt bad inside, like they wanted to hide. They made clothes out of leaves to cover themselves. And when they heard God coming, instead of running to Him like always, they hid in the trees.

God called out, "Where are you?" Adam came out and said he was hiding because he was scared. God asked, "Did you eat from the tree I told you not to eat from?" Adam blamed Eve. Eve blamed the snake. Nobody said sorry.
Because of what they did, things got harder. Life was not as easy as it had been. And they had to leave the beautiful garden.

But before they left, God made something kind for them. He made real clothes for them so they would not be cold. And God made a promise. One day, someone in Eve's family would fix everything the snake had broken. The rescue was already on its way.

devotional.bigIdea: When we make bad choices, we might want to hide. But God always comes looking for us. He loves us even when we mess up.
questions: Adam and Eve hid from God after they made a bad choice. Have you ever felt like hiding when you did something wrong? What did you do?
God made Adam and Eve warm clothes even after they disobeyed Him. Can you think of a time someone was still kind to you after you made a mistake?
When God gives us rules, do you think He is trying to help us or hurt us? Why?

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Adam's bad choice brought sin into the world. Jesus came to fix what went wrong in the garden.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Just like the son in that story ran away and then came back, Adam and Eve turned away from God, but God made a way to come back to Him.

connections.title: In the Beginning
connections.why: God promised someone from Eve's family would one day beat the snake. That was the first hint about Jesus.

quiz.question: What was the one rule God gave Adam and Eve?
options: Do not talk to animals
Do not leave the garden
Do not go out at night
Do not eat from the special tree in the middle
quiz.explanation: God said they could eat from any tree except the one in the middle of the garden. That was the only rule.

quiz.question: What did Adam and Eve do when they heard God coming after they ate the fruit?
options: They hid in the trees because they were scared
They ran to meet Him like always
They went to sleep
They kept eating the fruit
quiz.explanation: Adam and Eve hid in the trees. They felt bad inside and were scared because they had broken God's rule.

quiz.question: What did God make for Adam and Eve before they left the garden?
options: A boat to travel in
Real clothes to keep them warm
A new garden to live in
A bag of food for the journey
quiz.explanation: God made real clothes for them. Even after they disobeyed, God still took care of them.

stories.title: Two Brothers
stories.hook: Cain and Abel were the very first brothers. They both wanted to give something to God. But only one of them gave his very best.
story: Adam and Eve had two sons. The first was named Cain, who grew food from the ground. The second was named Abel, who took care of sheep.
One day both brothers decided to bring a special gift for God. Abel brought the very best of his baby lambs, the finest ones he had. Cain brought some of his crops, but not his best ones.
God was happy with Abel's gift. But He was not happy with Cain's gift. Cain got very angry. His face fell and he looked upset.

God came to talk to Cain. He said, "Why are you angry? If you do what is right, I will be happy with your gift too. But be careful. Something bad wants to get control of you. You have to choose to do what is right." God was warning Cain and giving him a chance to change.

But Cain did not listen. He said to Abel, "Let us go out to the field." And when they got there, Cain hurt Abel very badly, and Abel died. It was the very first time someone had died, and it happened because of anger and jealousy.
God came to Cain and asked, "Where is your brother Abel?" Cain said, "I do not know. Am I supposed to watch my brother?" But God knew what had happened.

Because of what Cain did, he had to go away and could not go back home. He was sad and scared. But even then, God put a special mark on Cain to keep him safe so no one would hurt him. Even Cain was not forgotten by God.
This sad story asks us a question we should never forget: should we look out for each other? Yes. God wants us to love the people around us, especially our family.

devotional.bigIdea: God warned Cain before things went wrong. When we feel angry or jealous, we can talk to God about it. He helps us make good choices before we do something we cannot take back.
questions: Cain felt jealous because God liked Abel's gift better. Have you ever wanted something that someone else had? How did it make you feel?
God warned Cain before anything bad happened. Can you think of a time a grown-up warned you to stop before you did something wrong?
Even after Cain did something terrible, God still kept him safe. Does that surprise you? What does that tell you about God?

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus taught that angry feelings in our heart are dangerous, not just the bad things we do with them. Cain's story shows why.

connections.title: The First Bad Choice
connections.why: The trouble in Cain's heart started because of what went wrong in the garden. Bad choices lead to more bad choices.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: The Bible uses Cain as an example of what happens when we let jealousy grow. The opposite of Cain is love.

quiz.question: What did Abel bring as a gift to God?
options: Some vegetables from his garden
A pile of rocks he had collected
The very best of his baby lambs
Some bread he had baked
quiz.explanation: Abel brought the very best of his baby lambs. He gave God his best, and God was happy with his gift.

quiz.question: What did God tell Cain when Cain was angry?
options: That he should go away and not come back
That Abel was better than him
That he should try again tomorrow
That if he did what was right, his gift would be accepted too
quiz.explanation: God told Cain that if he did what was right, his gift would be accepted. God gave Cain a chance to change before anything bad happened.

quiz.question: Even after Cain did something very wrong, what did God do for him?
options: God put a special mark on him to keep him safe
God forgave him right away and forgot it happened
God gave him a new family
God let him go back to his old home
quiz.explanation: God put a mark on Cain so that nobody would hurt him. Even Cain was still cared for by God.

stories.title: The Ark and the Rainbow
stories.hook: The world had forgotten about God. But one man still loved Him. And God had a very big job for that man.
story: A long time after Adam and Eve, the world had gotten very bad. People had stopped caring about God. They were hurting each other. God looked at the world He had made and was very sad.
But there was one man named Noah who still loved God and tried to do what was right. God saw Noah and decided to talk to him.

God told Noah that He was going to send a big flood to cover the whole earth. But God wanted to save Noah and his family. He told Noah to build a huge boat called an ark. It was not just a little boat. It was enormous, like a floating house. God told Noah exactly how to build it.
God also told Noah to bring two of every kind of animal onto the ark, one boy animal and one girl animal. That way, the animals would be safe too.

Noah did everything God told him to do. It took a very long time to build such a big ark. His neighbors probably thought he was very strange, building a huge boat when there was no water nearby. But Noah kept building because he trusted God.
When the ark was ready, the animals came, two by two. Every kind of animal you can think of. Then Noah and his wife and his sons and their wives all went in. And God closed the door.
Then it started to rain. It rained and rained for forty days. The water rose higher and higher until everything was covered. But the ark floated safely on top of the water.

After a long time, the water started going down. The ark landed on a mountain. Noah sent out a little dove to see if there was dry land. The third time the dove flew out, she did not come back. Noah knew it was time to come out.

The very first thing Noah did when he got out of the ark was thank God. He built a place to worship and said thank you to God for keeping them safe. God was so pleased. Then God made Noah a promise. He said, "I will never flood the whole earth again." And He put a rainbow in the sky as a sign of that promise.
Every rainbow you see is a reminder that God keeps every promise He makes. Every single one.

devotional.bigIdea: Noah built a huge boat because he trusted God, even when it did not make sense. Every rainbow is God showing us He keeps His promises.
questions: Noah built a giant boat far from any water because God told him to. Have you ever done something hard because a grown-up you trusted asked you to?
God closed the door of the ark Himself. Noah did not have to figure out when to go in. How does it feel to know that God takes care of things for us?
Next time you see a rainbow, what will you remember about God?

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: Peter says the ark and the flood pointed forward to baptism, being carried safely through water into a new life.

connections.title: A Promise as Big as the Stars
connections.why: The rainbow promise with Noah is the first of God's big promises. The same God would later make big promises to Abraham and to us.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus talked about the days of Noah to help people understand that they should always be ready for Him.

quiz.question: Why did God decide to save Noah and his family?
options: Because Noah was the tallest man alive
Because Noah still loved God and tried to do what was right
Because Noah had the most animals
Because Noah lived far away from the flood
quiz.explanation: In a world where everyone had forgotten about God, Noah still loved God and tried to do what was right. God saw Noah's heart.

quiz.question: What was the very first thing Noah did when he got out of the ark?
options: He looked for food for the animals
He checked that everything was dry
He thanked God and worshipped Him
He started building a house
quiz.explanation: Right away, Noah built a place to worship and said thank you to God. Saying thank you to God was the most important thing on his mind.

quiz.question: What did God put in the sky as a sign of His promise to never flood the whole earth again?
options: A bright star
A pillar of fire
A big cloud shaped like a boat
A rainbow
quiz.explanation: God put a rainbow in the sky as a sign of His promise. Every rainbow we see is a reminder that God keeps His word.

stories.title: The Tower of Babel
stories.hook: Long ago, everyone on earth spoke the same language. Then they decided to build a tower to make themselves famous. God had something to say about that.
story: After Noah and his family, people spread out across the earth and had lots and lots of babies, until there were people everywhere. For a while, everyone in the whole world spoke the same language. Everyone could talk to everyone else.
A big group of people settled on a flat, open plain. They figured out how to make bricks and build big things. They were good builders, and they had a big idea.

They said to each other, "Let us build a huge city with a tower so tall it reaches all the way up to heaven! We will make ourselves famous. And we will all stay right here together." The problem was that God had told people to spread out and fill the earth. They were doing the exact opposite.
God looked down at what they were building. These people were only thinking about making themselves look great. They were not thinking about God at all.

So God did something surprising. He changed their language! All of a sudden, nobody could understand what anyone else was saying. Workers who had been talking to each other all morning suddenly heard strange sounds when their friends opened their mouths.

Everything got very confusing. People who could understand each other stayed together. People who could not understand each other went in different directions. They stopped building and spread out all over the earth, which is exactly what God had wanted them to do all along.
The city was called Babel, which means mixed up. They had wanted to build something to reach up to God. Instead, God came down and mixed everything up.

But here is the good news at the end of the story. Many, many years later, God sent His Holy Spirit, and something wonderful happened. People from every country and every language could suddenly understand God's good news in their own language. What happened at Babel was fixed by God's Spirit. God always finishes what He starts.

devotional.bigIdea: The people at Babel wanted to do things their own way instead of God's way. God wants us to trust His plans, not just make up our own.
questions: Have you ever wanted to show off or be the most important person? How did that feel?
God asked people to spread out and they said no. Have you ever not wanted to do something a grown-up asked you to do? What happened?
At the end of the story, God fixed the Babel problem with His Spirit. What does that tell you about God? Does He give up?

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: At Pentecost, God's Spirit let everyone hear the good news in their own language. That undid what happened at Babel.

connections.title: A Promise as Big as the Stars
connections.why: Right after Babel, God called Abraham and started His plan to bless every nation on earth.

connections.title: The First Bad Choice
connections.why: The snake told Adam and Eve they could be like God on their own. The people at Babel tried the same thing. It did not work either time.

quiz.question: Why did the people want to build such a tall tower?
options: To make themselves famous and stay together
To see the animals from far away
To get closer to God and worship Him
To protect themselves from rain
quiz.explanation: The people wanted to make themselves famous and stay in one place. They were not thinking about what God wanted.

quiz.question: How did God stop the people from finishing their tower?
options: He sent rain to knock it down
He mixed up their language so they could not understand each other
He made them all fall asleep
He sent an angel to tell them to stop
quiz.explanation: God mixed up their language so nobody could understand what anyone else was saying. Building became impossible when no one could talk to each other.

quiz.question: What does the name Babel mean?
options: High tower
Big city
Mixed up
Famous place
quiz.explanation: Babel means mixed up. The city got that name because God mixed up the language of all the people there.

stories.title: A Promise as Big as the Stars
stories.hook: God told a man to leave his home and go somewhere new. He did not even know where he was going yet. But he went.
story: A man named Abram had a good life. He had a nice home, a wife named Sarai, and lots of animals and helpers. He was happy where he was. He was seventy-five years old.
Then God spoke to him. God said, "Leave your home and your family. Go to a new land that I will show you." God did not give him a map. He did not tell him how far it was. He just said go.

God also made Abram a big promise. He said, "I will make your family into a great nation. I will bless you, and through your family, every person on earth will be blessed." That was a very big promise for one man.
Abram went. He packed everything up and left the only home he had ever known. He did not know exactly where he was going. He just knew that God was asking him to go. So he went.
Years went by. Then more years. Abram and Sarai had no children, and they were getting old. How could God make a great nation out of them if they had no children? It seemed impossible.

One night, God took Abram outside and said, "Look up at the sky. Can you count all the stars?" There were so many stars that Abram could not even begin to count them. God said, "That is how many people will be in your family one day." Abram looked up at all those stars, and he believed God.

God changed Abram's name to Abraham, which means father of many nations. And He changed Sarai's name to Sarah. Then God told them something that made Sarah laugh right out loud. He said they were going to have a baby. Sarah was ninety years old! God heard her laughing and said, "Is anything too hard for me?"
One year later, Abraham and Sarah held their baby boy. They named him Isaac, which means he laughs. God had kept His promise.

Then God asked Abraham to do the hardest thing he had ever been asked to do. He asked Abraham to give Isaac back to Him on a mountain. Abraham was very sad. But he trusted God. He took Isaac up the mountain.

At the last moment, God called out, "Stop! I can see that you trust me." Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught in the bushes. He used the ram instead. God had provided everything Abraham needed. Abraham named that place The Lord Will Provide. And it has been called that ever since.

devotional.bigIdea: Abraham did not know where he was going. He just knew who was asking him to go. That is what it means to trust God.
questions: Abraham left his home without even knowing where he was going. Have you ever had to do something scary because someone you trusted asked you to?
God asked Abraham to wait a very long time for his baby boy. Waiting is hard! What is something you have had to wait a long time for?
God said to Abraham, "Is anything too hard for me?" What does that make you think? Is there anything too hard for God?

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: God gave His own Son, just like He asked Abraham to give his. But this time, no angel stopped it.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: Abraham believed God could do the impossible. That same faith points straight to the resurrection.

connections.title: The Ark and the Rainbow
connections.why: God made a big promise to Noah with a rainbow. The promise to Abraham is the next big promise in God's plan.

quiz.question: How old was Abram when God told him to leave his home and go?
options: Twenty-five years old
Forty years old
Ten years old
Seventy-five years old
quiz.explanation: Abram was seventy-five years old, not young at all, when he trusted God and left everything he knew.

quiz.question: What did God use to show Abraham how big his family would be one day?
options: The stars in the night sky
Grains of sand on a beach
Drops of water in a river
Blades of grass in a field
quiz.explanation: God took Abraham outside at night and said count the stars. So shall your family be. Abraham believed Him.

quiz.question: What did God provide at the last moment when Abraham was on the mountain?
options: A lamb sent down from heaven
A ram caught in the bushes
A dove with a message
An angel with a new plan
quiz.explanation: A ram was caught in the bushes nearby. Abraham used it instead of Isaac. He named the place The Lord Will Provide.

stories.title: The Stolen Blessing
stories.hook: Esau was born first, so the big blessing belonged to him. But his brother Jacob wanted it, and he came up with a sneaky plan to get it.
story: Isaac and Rebekah had twin boys. The first one came out red and hairy, and they named him Esau. The second one came out holding on to his brother's heel, and they named him Jacob. Esau loved to hunt outside. Jacob liked to stay near home. They were brothers, but they were very different.
Because Esau was born first, he had something special called the birthright. It meant that one day he would get the biggest gift and the special blessing from his father.

One day Esau came home very hungry. Jacob was cooking a pot of red stew. "Please give me some!" said Esau. Jacob said, "First give me your birthright." Esau was so hungry that he said yes, just for a bowl of soup. He gave away his special gift without even thinking about it.
Many years later, their father Isaac was very old, and he could not see anymore. He wanted to give his big blessing to Esau. But Rebekah wanted Jacob to have it instead. So she made a plan.

She cooked Isaac's favorite food. She put goat skins on Jacob's smooth arms so they would feel hairy like Esau's. Then Jacob went to his father and said, "I am Esau." Isaac could not see, so he believed him, and he gave Jacob the big blessing.
When Esau found out what had happened, he was very sad and very angry. He was so angry that Jacob had to run far away to keep safe.

Jacob did a wrong thing when he tricked his father, and it made his whole family sad. But God was not finished with Jacob. Even when we make a mess, God still keeps his promises and never gives up on us.

devotional.bigIdea: Jacob tricked his father to get the blessing, and it made his family very sad. But God did not give up on Jacob. God keeps his promises even when we make mistakes.
questions: Esau gave away something special just because he was hungry right now. What is something good that is worth waiting for?
How do you think Esau felt when he found out about the trick?
Even when we do something wrong, God still loves us. How does that make you feel?

connections.title: A Promise as Big as the Stars
connections.why: The blessing Jacob fought for was the very promise God first made to his grandfather Abraham, handed down through the family.

connections.title: Jacob's Ladder
connections.why: Running for his life, Jacob meets God in the wilderness, who promises to stay with the very schemer who just lied to his father.

connections.title: The Dreamer's Long Road
connections.why: Jacob grew up in a home with a favorite son, and sadly he repeated it with his own boys, loving Joseph best of all.

quiz.question: Esau traded his special birthright for what?
options: A bowl of stew
A new bow
A baby goat

quiz.question: Isaac could not see Jacob's trick because he was?
options: Sleeping
Old and could not see
Too busy eating

quiz.question: After he tricked his father, Jacob had to do what?
options: Say he was sorry
Run far away
Go hunting

stories.title: Jacob's Ladder
stories.hook: Jacob was all alone, far from home, with a stone for a pillow. Then he had a dream that showed him heaven was closer than he ever imagined.
story: Jacob had to run away from home, because his brother Esau was very angry with him.
He walked a long, long way, all by himself. He had no friends with him and no cozy place to sleep.
When the sun went down, Jacob was out in the wild, and there was nowhere to rest. There were no beds and no soft pillows anywhere at all.
So Jacob found a smooth stone, put his head on it like a pillow, and lay down on the hard ground. He felt lonely and very far from home.
But while Jacob was sleeping, God gave him an amazing dream.
In his dream, Jacob saw a giant stairway. It started on the ground and went up, up, up, all the way into heaven!
And on the stairway were the angels of God, going up and coming down, up and down between heaven and earth.
At the very top of the stairway stood God himself. And God spoke kindly to Jacob.
"I am with you," God said. "I will watch over you everywhere you go, and I will bring you back home again one day. I will never, ever leave you."
When Jacob woke up, he was filled with wonder. "God is right here in this place," he said, "and I did not even know it!"
In the morning, Jacob stood his stone pillow up tall to help him always remember this special day. He named the place Bethel, which means the house of God.
Jacob had set out so lonely and so afraid. But now he knew a wonderful secret: with God watching over him, he was never really alone at all.
Jacob had felt all alone in the dark. But God had been with him the whole time. And God is always with you too, even when you cannot see him.

devotional.bigIdea: Jacob felt all alone, but God was right there with him, and God promised to never leave him. God is with you too, even when you cannot see him.
questions: When is a time you have felt alone? How does it help to know that God is with you?
What was the most amazing part of Jacob's dream to you?
Jacob set up a stone to remember God. What is something that helps you remember God loves you?

connections.title: The Stolen Blessing
connections.why: Jacob is on the run because of the blessing he stole, and God meets him anyway with grace he never earned.

connections.title: A New Name
connections.why: Twenty years later God meets Jacob again, this time face to face, and gives the schemer a brand new name.

connections.title: A Promise as Big as the Stars
connections.why: God repeats to Jacob the very promise he made to Abraham, that through this family every nation on earth would be blessed.

quiz.question: What did Jacob use for a pillow?
options: A stone
A pillow of feathers
His shoes

quiz.question: In his dream, Jacob saw a stairway going up to where?
options: A mountain
Heaven
A castle

quiz.question: What did God promise Jacob?
options: He would be rich
He would always be with him
He would win every race

stories.title: A New Name
stories.hook: Jacob was going home at last, but he was scared to see his brother Esau. Alone in the dark, a mysterious man began to wrestle with him all night long.
story: After many years away, Jacob was finally going back home. But he was very, very scared.
Jacob was scared because he had to see his brother Esau again. The last time they were together, Esau had been so angry with him.
The night before they would meet, Jacob sent his whole family across a river to keep them safe. Then he stayed behind, all by himself, in the dark.
Suddenly, a man came out of the dark and started to wrestle with Jacob.
They wrestled and wrestled, back and forth, all night long. Neither one of them would give up!
When morning was almost there, the man said, "Let me go now."
But Jacob held on tight. "I will not let you go," he said, "until you give me a blessing."
The man asked him, "What is your name?" "My name is Jacob," he answered.
Then the man said, "Your new name will be Israel, because you have held on to God and would not give up."
Suddenly Jacob understood something amazing. He had been wrestling with God the whole time!
Jacob named that special place Peniel. "I have seen God face to face," he said, with wonder in his heart.
All through that long, dark night, Jacob had held on with all his might, and he would not let go until God blessed him. And in the morning, God gave him a brand new name and a fresh new start.
And from that day on, even though he walked with a limp, Jacob would always remember the special night when he met God face to face.
When the sun came up, Jacob walked toward his brother, and he was limping a little. Jacob used to grab for everything he wanted. But that night, he learned to hold on tight to God instead.

devotional.bigIdea: Jacob held on to God all night and would not let go until God blessed him. God gave him a new name and a new start. God can change us, even when life is a struggle.
questions: Jacob held on tight to God. What are some ways you can hold on to God when you are scared?
How do you think Jacob felt when God gave him a brand new name?
God gave Jacob a fresh start. Is there something you would like a fresh start with?

connections.title: Jacob's Ladder
connections.why: Years earlier God promised at Bethel to bring Jacob home safely, and now Jacob holds God to that very promise on the way back.

connections.title: The Stolen Blessing
connections.why: The brother Jacob cheated is finally right in front of him, and the cheater has to face the past he ran away from.

connections.title: The Dreamer's Long Road
connections.why: Jacob, now named Israel, becomes the father of the sons whose families grow into the twelve tribes of Israel.

quiz.question: Jacob was scared to go home because he had to see whom?
options: A lion
His brother Esau
A king

quiz.question: Who did Jacob wrestle with all night?
options: A man who was really God
His brother
A friend

quiz.question: What new name did God give him?
options: Israel
Adam
David

stories.title: The Dreamer's Long Road
stories.hook: Joseph had big dreams. His brothers did not like that. But God had a plan that nobody could have guessed.
story: Jacob had twelve sons, and Joseph was his favorite. Jacob gave Joseph a beautiful coat to show how much he loved him. Joseph's brothers saw the coat every day and it made them jealous.

Joseph also had two special dreams. In one dream, his brothers' bundles of wheat bowed down to his. In another dream, the sun and moon and eleven stars all bowed to him. When Joseph told his brothers about the dreams, they got very angry. Were they supposed to bow down to their little brother? No way.

One day, far from home, the brothers saw their chance. They grabbed Joseph, took his beautiful coat, and threw him into a deep empty pit. Then some traders came by going to Egypt. The brothers sold Joseph to the traders for twenty pieces of silver. They dipped his coat in goat's blood and told their father that a wild animal must have eaten him. Jacob cried for a long, long time.

In Egypt, Joseph was sold to work in a man's house. He worked hard and did everything right. But then something unfair happened and Joseph was thrown into prison. He had done nothing wrong. He was in prison anyway.

Even in prison, God was with Joseph. Two men who worked for the king had strange dreams one night. Joseph listened carefully and told each man what his dream meant. Everything he said came true exactly.
One of the men went back to work for the king and forgot all about Joseph. Two whole years went by. Then the king himself had two very strange dreams. Nobody could figure out what they meant.

The man who had forgotten about Joseph finally remembered. He told the king about the young man in prison. Joseph was brought out of prison, cleaned up, and stood before the most powerful man in the whole country.

Joseph listened to the king's dreams and said, "Seven good years of food are coming. Then seven years of no food at all. You need to save up food now while you can." The king looked at Joseph and said, "There is no one as wise as you. You will be in charge of all Egypt." Joseph went from prison to being the second most powerful man in the country in one single day.
When the years of no food came, Joseph's brothers traveled to Egypt to buy food. They stood before the man in charge and bowed down. They had no idea it was Joseph.

Joseph tested his brothers to see if their hearts had changed. When he saw that they had, he could not hold back his feelings any longer. He started crying so loudly that everyone in the building could hear him. He said, "I am Joseph, your brother." Then he said words that nobody expected: "You meant to hurt me. But God meant it for good."

devotional.bigIdea: Joseph had a very hard life for a long time. But God was with him every step of the way, even when it did not feel like it.
questions: Joseph's brothers were mean to him because they were jealous. Have you ever been left out or treated unfairly? How did that feel?
Joseph was in prison for something he did not do. That is very unfair! Do you think God was still with him even there? How do you know?
Joseph said to his brothers, "You meant to hurt me, but God meant it for good." What do you think that means?

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Joseph hugged his brothers and forgave them, just like the father ran to his son in that story.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Joseph was sold by his brothers and then lifted up to save many lives. His story points to Jesus.

connections.title: The Baby in the River
connections.why: God used Joseph's time in Egypt to get His people there. Then He used Moses to bring them home.

quiz.question: Why were Joseph's brothers so jealous of him?
options: Because Joseph was the fastest runner
Because Joseph had the most animals
Because their father loved Joseph best and gave him a beautiful coat
Because Joseph was the tallest brother
quiz.explanation: Jacob gave Joseph a beautiful coat to show how much he loved him. The brothers saw it every day and it made them very jealous.

quiz.question: What happened to Joseph after his brothers threw him in the pit?
options: He climbed out and ran home
He was rescued by his father Jacob
He stayed in the pit until it rained
His brothers sold him to traders going to Egypt
quiz.explanation: Some traders came by on their way to Egypt. The brothers sold Joseph to them for twenty pieces of silver.

quiz.question: What did Joseph say to his brothers when he finally told them who he was?
options: You meant to hurt me, but God meant it for good
I have been looking for you all this time
You will pay for what you did to me
I never want to see you again
quiz.explanation: Joseph said you meant to hurt me, but God meant it for good. It is one of the most amazing things anyone ever said.

stories.title: The Baby in the River
stories.hook: A brave mother hid her baby for as long as she could. When she could not hide him anymore, she did something very brave.
story: More than four hundred years had passed since Joseph. The family of Israel had grown into a huge people. There were so many of them that the new king of Egypt got scared. He made all of the Israelite people into slaves.

The slaves had to work very hard every single day in the hot sun, making bricks and building things for the king. The king kept making their work harder, but the more he pushed them down, the more they grew. So the king did something terrible. He said that every baby boy born to the Israelite people had to be thrown into the river.
Into this scary world, a woman had a baby boy. She took one look at him and knew she had to protect him. She hid him for three whole months. Every time he made a sound, her heart must have jumped.

When she could not hide him any longer, she made a little boat out of reeds, the tall grasses that grow by the river. She made it waterproof and put her baby inside. Then she carried him to the river and set the little boat in the water among the tall reeds. His big sister Miriam watched from a little way away to see what would happen.

The king's own daughter came down to the river to wash. She saw the little boat floating in the reeds. She opened it, and the baby cried. Her heart went soft right away. She said, "This must be one of the Israelite babies." But she did not throw him in the river. She kept him.
Right then, Miriam stepped out from her hiding place. She asked, "Should I go find an Israelite woman to feed the baby for you?" Yes, said the princess. Miriam ran and got their own mother.
The mother who had been so scared and so sad got to hold her own baby boy and take care of him, and the king's own daughter paid her to do it.

The baby grew up in the king's palace. The princess named him Moses, which means pulled out of the water. God had used the very river the king meant for harm to carry Moses to safety. God was already writing the next part of the story.

devotional.bigIdea: Moses's mother was very brave. She trusted God with her baby when she could not protect him herself. God was watching over Moses the whole time.
questions: Moses's mother made a little boat and put her baby in the river because she wanted to save him. That must have been so hard! Have you ever had to let go of something you loved?
God used the king's own daughter to save a baby the king wanted to hurt. Does that surprise you? Can you think of other times God uses surprising people to do good things?
Moses's sister Miriam was watching and stepped forward at exactly the right moment. Have you ever been brave at just the right time?

connections.title: The Ark and the Rainbow
connections.why: The word for Moses's little boat is the same word used for Noah's big ark. Both were boats that God used to rescue someone.

connections.title: The Burning Bush
connections.why: Growing up in the palace gave Moses the education God would use to prepare him for his big job.

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: Jesus was also a baby whose life was in danger from a king. His family had to run to keep Him safe, just like Moses.

quiz.question: What did Moses's mother make to keep her baby safe?
options: A strong wooden box with a lock
A little boat made of river reeds
A hiding place under the floor
A tent in the forest
quiz.explanation: Moses's mother made a little boat out of reeds from the river and made it waterproof so her baby would float safely.

quiz.question: Who found the baby Moses in the river?
options: A fisherman who was fishing nearby
Moses's sister Miriam
The king's own daughter
A kind old woman from the village
quiz.explanation: The king's daughter came down to the river and found the little boat. Even though she knew he was an Israelite baby, she kept him and raised him.

quiz.question: What does the name Moses mean?
options: Born in a river
Son of a king
Strong and brave
Pulled out of the water
quiz.explanation: The princess named him Moses, which means pulled out of the water, because she pulled him out of the Nile River.

stories.title: The Burning Bush
stories.hook: Moses was watching sheep in the desert when he saw something very strange. A bush was on fire, but it was not burning up. Then God called his name.
story: Moses had grown up in the palace, but things had gone wrong and he had run away to the desert. He had been living there for forty years, taking care of sheep for his father-in-law. He was eighty years old. His big exciting life in the palace felt like a long time ago.
One ordinary day, Moses was walking with the sheep near a mountain called Horeb. He saw something that stopped him right in his tracks. A bush was on fire.
Bushes catching fire in the hot desert was not unusual. But this bush kept burning and burning and was not burning up at all. The leaves stayed green. Moses walked over to look more closely.

The moment Moses turned to look, God called out from inside the burning bush. "Moses! Moses!" Moses said, "Here I am!" God said, "Take off your shoes. The ground where you are standing is special because I am here." Moses took off his shoes.

Then God said, "I have seen how My people are suffering in Egypt. I have heard them crying. I know how much they are hurting. I am going to rescue them. And I am sending you to go tell the king to let them go."
Moses was surprised. He said, "Who am I? Why would the king listen to me?" God said, "I will be with you. That is all you need."
Moses tried again. He said, "What if people ask me Your name? What should I tell them?" God said, "Tell them I AM sent you. I am always here, always present, always the same."

Moses kept finding reasons why he could not do it. "What if they do not believe me? I am not a good speaker. Please send someone else!" Each time Moses worried, God answered. He gave Moses signs to show people. He gave Moses his brother Aaron to help him speak.

Moses had five worries. God had an answer for every single one. Finally Moses stopped arguing and went. He was eighty years old. He had a brother and a staff and a God who promised to be with him. It was enough.

devotional.bigIdea: Every single worry Moses had, God had an answer for. God does not just tell us not to be scared. He stays right beside us.
questions: Moses said to God, "Who am I? I am not important enough for this job." Have you ever felt like you were not good enough to do something? What happened?
God told Moses, "I will be with you. That is all you need." Do you believe that? What does it feel like when you know someone is with you?
Moses turned aside to look at the strange burning bush. What might he have missed if he had been too busy to stop and look? What do you think God wants you to pay attention to?

connections.title: The Baby in the River
connections.why: God says I have seen and I have heard. The same God who protected Moses as a baby was now sending him back to help His people.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Jesus said before Abraham was, I AM. He used the same name God spoke from the burning bush.

connections.title: Three Hundred Men and Torches
connections.why: God called Gideon the same way He called Moses. Both men felt completely unqualified. Both went anyway.

quiz.question: What was so strange about the burning bush that made Moses stop and look?
options: It was on fire but it was not burning up
It was glowing every color of the rainbow
It was floating above the ground
It was singing a song
quiz.explanation: Bushes catching fire in the hot desert happened sometimes. But this bush kept burning and burning and never burned up. That was what stopped Moses.

quiz.question: What did God tell Moses to do before coming closer to the burning bush?
options: Bring a gift from his flock
Take off his shoes because the ground was special
Cover his eyes so he would not be scared
Kneel down and be very quiet
quiz.explanation: God told Moses to take off his shoes because the ground was special. God's presence makes ordinary places into something amazing.

quiz.question: What was Moses's first worry when God asked him to go talk to the king?
options: He said he was too old
He said the desert was too far to walk
He said, "Who am I? Why would the king listen to me?"
He said he did not speak Egyptian
quiz.explanation: Moses said who am I. He felt too small for such a big job. God's answer was not to list Moses's good qualities. He just said I will be with you.

stories.title: The Ten Plagues
stories.hook: God told Pharaoh to let his people go free. But Pharaoh kept saying no, so God showed him who was really in charge.
story: God's people, the Israelites, had been slaves in the land of Egypt for a very, very long time. They worked hard all day long, and they cried out to God to please save them.
So God sent a man named Moses to the king of Egypt, who was called Pharaoh. Moses stood before the king and said, "God says, let my people go!"
But Pharaoh did not want to lose all his workers. He folded his arms and said, "No! I will not let them go."
So God sent troubles on Egypt called plagues. First, Moses held his hand over the river, and all the water turned to blood.
Then frogs came hopping up out of the river, thousands and thousands of them, into the houses and the beds and even the cooking pots!
After the frogs came tiny biting bugs, and then big, buzzing clouds of flies all over Egypt.
Next, the farm animals got sick, and the people got itchy, sore skin. Then big chunks of hail came crashing down from the sky, and hungry locusts ate up every green plant that was left.
Last of all, a thick darkness covered Egypt for three whole days. It was so dark that no one could even see their own hand in front of their face.
But here is the wonderful part. Where God's people lived, it stayed bright and sunny the whole time. God kept his people safe through every single plague.
Every time a plague came, Moses said, "Let God's people go." And every time, Pharaoh was stubborn and said no.
God was showing Pharaoh, and everyone in Egypt, that he is stronger than any king in the whole world.
And one more plague was still coming, the very last one, the one that would finally set God's people free.

devotional.bigIdea: Pharaoh thought he was the strongest. But God showed that he alone is God, and that he never forgets his people. Nothing is stronger than God.
questions: Pharaoh kept saying no to God. Why do you think he was so stubborn?
God kept his people safe through all the plagues. How does it feel to know God keeps you safe?
What was the most surprising plague to you?

connections.title: The Burning Bush
connections.why: At the bush God promised to rescue his people, and now he sends Moses back to Pharaoh to make good on that promise.

connections.title: The First Passover
connections.why: Nine plagues could not move Pharaoh's heart. The tenth and final one is coming, and it changes everything.

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: Even after the plagues, Pharaoh chases the people to the sea, where God wins the very last word.

quiz.question: What did Moses keep telling Pharaoh?
options: Let God's people go
Give me a crown
Build a boat

quiz.question: What did the river turn into?
options: Blood
Milk
Sand

quiz.question: Did God keep his own people safe during the plagues?
options: Yes, he protected them
No, he forgot them
Only sometimes

stories.title: The First Passover
stories.hook: On the night God set his people free, every family painted lamb's blood on their door, and the blood kept everyone inside safe.
story: God said that one last plague would finally set his people free from Egypt. But first, he gave them a special way to stay safe that night.
God told each family to pick a perfect little lamb, one with nothing wrong with it at all.
That evening, the families would gather together, and they would paint some of the lamb's blood on the sides and the top of their front door.
Then they cooked the lamb and ate it with flat bread. They put on their coats and their shoes and held their walking sticks, all ready to leave the very moment God said go.
God told them, "When I come through Egypt tonight and I see the blood on your door, I will pass right over your house. No harm will come to anyone inside."
So the families waited inside their homes, trusting God. And in the middle of the night, God passed through all of Egypt.
Every single house that had the blood on the door was kept safe, and everyone inside was protected. The blood showed that the people inside belonged to God.
That very night, Pharaoh finally called for Moses. "Go!" he cried. "Take your families, take your animals, take everything, and leave!"
So God's people walked out of Egypt as free people at last, after such a very long time.
God told them to remember this special night every single year, with a special meal. They called it the Passover, because God had passed over them and kept them safe.
And there was a wonderful secret hidden in that night. Long, long after, a man named John would point to Jesus and say, "Look, the Lamb of God!"
Jesus is the Lamb who keeps us safe forever. The very first Passover was pointing to him all along.

devotional.bigIdea: God's people were kept safe by the blood of the lamb on their door. That first Passover was a picture of Jesus, the Lamb of God, who keeps us safe.
questions: The families had to trust God and paint the blood before anything happened. When is it hard for you to trust?
God told them to remember this night every year. What is a special thing your family likes to remember?
How do you think the families felt when morning came and they were safe?

connections.title: The Ten Plagues
connections.why: Nine plagues failed to move Pharaoh. The tenth, on the night of the Passover, finally sets the people free.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: A spotless lamb died so death would pass over God's people, pointing ahead to Jesus, the Lamb of God who died to rescue us.

connections.title: The Night Before
connections.why: Centuries later Jesus shared a Passover meal with his friends the night before he died, and gave it a brand new meaning.

quiz.question: What did the families paint on their doors?
options: A lamb's blood
Gold stars
Their names

quiz.question: What did God promise when he saw the blood?
options: He would pass over the house
He would send snow
He would knock

quiz.question: What special meal did God tell them to remember each year?
options: The Passover
Breakfast
A birthday

stories.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
stories.hook: God's people were free at last! But then the king changed his mind and came after them with his whole army. They were trapped between the army and the sea.
story: After many amazing and terrifying things happened in Egypt, the king finally let God's people go. Hundreds of thousands of people walked out of Egypt, free for the very first time. God went ahead of them as a tall cloud during the day and a pillar of fire at night so they could always see where He was.

But then the king changed his mind. He took six hundred of his best chariots and his whole army and chased after the Israelites. Chariots are like fast carts pulled by horses, and they were very scary.

The people looked up and saw the army coming behind them. Then they looked ahead and saw the sea in front of them. There was nowhere to go. They were very scared and started complaining to Moses. They said, "Did you bring us out here to die?"

Moses said something very important. He said, "Do not be afraid. Stand still and watch what God is going to do today. You will never see this army again. God will fight for you. You just need to be still."

Then God told Moses to hold out his staff over the sea. All that night, a strong wind blew. And the sea split open. The water piled up on both sides like two great walls, and the ground in the middle went completely dry. The people walked through the sea on dry ground with walls of water on both sides.

The Egyptian army followed them in. But then God looked down at the army and made the wheels of their chariots come off. They could not move properly. The soldiers said to each other, "God is fighting for Israel! Let us get out of here!" But it was too late.
When all of God's people were safely on the other side, Moses held out his staff again, and the water came rushing back. The whole army was gone. Not one soldier was left.

The people stood on the other side and looked back at the sea. They were safe. God had done something nobody had ever seen before. Then Moses sang a song, and his sister Miriam grabbed a tambourine and all the women danced and sang together. They had been slaves. Now they were free. And the God who had been quiet for four hundred years had made Himself impossible to ignore.

devotional.bigIdea: When you are stuck and scared with nowhere to go, that is often exactly when God does His most amazing work. He just asks you to trust Him and watch.
questions: The people were stuck between the army and the sea and they were very scared. Have you ever felt really stuck and not known what to do? What happened?
Moses said, "Stand still and watch what God is going to do." That is very hard when you are scared! Why do you think being still and trusting God is sometimes the bravest thing you can do?
After they crossed safely, Miriam grabbed her tambourine and everyone danced and sang. Have you ever been so happy and relieved that you just had to celebrate?

connections.title: The Ark and the Rainbow
connections.why: Paul says crossing the sea was like baptism for Israel, being carried safely through water into a new free life.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: God says I am doing something new. The crossing of the sea pointed forward to an even bigger rescue.

connections.title: The Burning Bush
connections.why: After the crossing, the people believed in God and in Moses, the faith God had been building since the burning bush.

quiz.question: What was in front of the people and what was behind them when they were trapped?
options: A desert in front and a mountain behind
A forest in front and a river behind
A city in front and a storm behind
The sea in front and the Egyptian army behind
quiz.explanation: The sea was in front of them and the Egyptian army was coming behind them. There was nowhere to go.

quiz.question: What did God use to make a dry path through the sea?
options: A strong wind that blew all night
A big wave that pushed the water aside
Angels who held the water back
Moses jumping into the water
quiz.explanation: God sent a strong wind that blew all night and pushed the water into two walls, leaving dry ground in the middle.

quiz.question: What did Miriam do when the people reached the other side safely?
options: She sat down and cried with happiness
She grabbed a tambourine and led everyone in dancing and singing
She built a fire to warm everyone up
She went straight to sleep because she was so tired
quiz.explanation: Miriam grabbed her tambourine and led all the women in dancing and singing. It was one of the happiest moments in the whole Bible.

stories.title: Bread from Heaven
stories.hook: There was no food in the desert, and the people were hungry and scared. So every single morning, God sent bread down from heaven.
story: Out in the hot, dry desert, the people of Israel ran out of food. They were hungry and afraid, and they began to grumble.
"We wish we were back in Egypt," they complained, "where we had plenty to eat! Did you bring us out here just to let us starve?"
But God was very kind to them. He told Moses, "I will send bread down from heaven for you, every single day."
That evening, a big flock of birds called quail came right into the camp, so the people had meat to eat for dinner.
And the next morning, when the dew dried up, there were little white flakes all over the ground, like frost. The people had never seen anything like it before.
They asked each other, "What is it? What is it?" So they gave it that very name: manna, which means, what is it?
The manna was white like a tiny seed, and it tasted sweet, a little like honey. The people gathered it up and ate it, and it was good.
God told them to gather just enough for one day. But some people did not listen, and they kept extra overnight. In the morning, it was full of worms and smelled terrible!
On the sixth day, God told them to gather twice as much, because the seventh day was a special day to rest. On that day, no manna would fall, and the extra they saved stayed fresh and good.
God did not send manna for just a few days. He sent it every single morning for forty years, until his people finally reached the land he had promised them.
God gave his people exactly what they needed, one day at a time. He always took care of them.
And God still takes care of us, giving us just what we need, one day at a time. We can always trust him.

devotional.bigIdea: God gave his people just enough food for each day, so they would trust him every morning. God still gives us what we need, one day at a time.
questions: The people forgot how big God was and started to grumble. What helps you remember the good things God does?
God gave them food one day at a time. What is something you can trust God for today?
How would you feel if you woke up and found breakfast on the ground from God?

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: Soon after God split the sea to save them, the people forgot, and grumbled that they would rather be back in Egypt.

connections.title: Water From the Rock
connections.why: Just as God sent bread when they were hungry, he sent water from a rock when they were thirsty, day after day.

connections.title: Five Loaves and Two Fish
connections.why: God fed a whole nation with bread from heaven, pointing ahead to Jesus, who said, I am the bread of life.

quiz.question: What did God send down every morning?
options: Bread called manna
Rain
Snow

quiz.question: How much were the people supposed to gather each day?
options: Just enough for that day
As much as possible
None at all

quiz.question: How long did God feed them with manna?
options: Forty years
One day
One week

stories.title: The Ten Commandments
stories.hook: The whole mountain shook and smoked and rumbled. Then God spoke out loud so everyone could hear.
story: Three months after walking out of Egypt, the Israelites set up camp at the bottom of a big mountain called Sinai. God told Moses to tell the people to get ready. In three days, God Himself was going to come down to speak to them.

On the morning of the third day, something amazing and terrifying happened. Thunder boomed and lightning flashed all over the mountain. A thick cloud covered the top. A loud trumpet blast rang out, so loud that the whole camp of people shook with fear. Then the whole mountain started to shake and smoke, because God came down on it in fire.

The people were told to stay back from the mountain. God was coming down, and His presence was not something to treat as ordinary. This was the same God who had split the sea and sent amazing signs in Egypt. And now He was going to speak.

God spoke ten rules that He wanted His people to live by. The first four rules were about God Himself. He said to have no other gods, to not make statues to worship, to treat His name with care, and to rest one day every week the way He had rested.

The last six rules were about people. He said to honor your mum and dad. Do not hurt people. Be faithful to your husband or wife. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others. Do not wish that you had things that belong to someone else.

The people were so scared by the thunder and fire and smoke that they stepped back and said to Moses, "You talk to God for us and then tell us what He said. If God keeps speaking directly to us, we might not survive!" Moses went up into the cloud where God was.
God wrote these ten rules on two flat stones with His own finger. Moses brought them back down the mountain.

Here is something important to remember. God gave these rules after He had already rescued His people from Egypt. He did not say, follow these rules and then I will save you. He saved them first. Then He said, now that you are free, here is how to live well. That is what kind of God He is.

devotional.bigIdea: God's rules are not meant to make life boring. They are meant to help us live well together and stay close to God.
questions: God gave His rules after He had already rescued His people. He did not make them earn their rescue. How does it feel to know that God helps us before He asks things of us?
Which of the ten rules do you think would be hardest to follow? Why?
Jesus said all the rules can be made simple: love God with everything you have and love other people. Can you think of one way to do that today?

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus said He came to fulfill the law, not get rid of it. He showed that the rules were always about the heart.

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: God opens the Ten Commandments by saying I am the one who brought you out of Egypt. The rescue always comes before the rules.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: What the law pointed to but could not do in us, Jesus did by coming to live among us.

quiz.question: What happened on the mountain when God came down to speak?
options: It turned green with flowers and fruit
It became quiet and peaceful
It shook and smoked and there was thunder and lightning
A big rainbow appeared over the top
quiz.explanation: The mountain shook and smoked and there was thunder and lightning and a loud trumpet sound. God's presence is powerful and amazing.

quiz.question: What did God write the ten rules on?
options: A big piece of wood
A long piece of cloth
The side of the mountain
Two flat stones
quiz.explanation: God wrote the ten rules on two flat stones with His own finger. Moses carried them down the mountain.

quiz.question: When did God give the people His rules?
options: After He had already rescued them and they were free
Before He rescued them from Egypt so they could earn their freedom
While they were still slaves in Egypt
A long time after they had settled in the new land
quiz.explanation: God rescued His people first and then gave them the rules. He did not make them earn their rescue. He helped them first.

stories.title: The Golden Calf
stories.hook: While Moses was up on the mountain talking with God, the people grew tired of waiting and made a pretend god out of gold.
story: Moses went up a tall mountain to meet with God and to get his rules for living. He was gone for a long, long time, forty days and forty nights.
Down at the bottom of the mountain, the people got tired of waiting. They went to Moses' brother Aaron with a bad idea.
"We do not know what happened to Moses," they said. "Make us a god that we can see!"
And instead of saying no, Aaron gave in. "Bring me your gold earrings," he said.
So Aaron took all their gold, melted it in the fire, and shaped it into a shiny golden calf. The people bowed down low to it and had a big, wild party.
But this was very, very wrong. They were forgetting the real God, the one who had just saved them from Egypt with his mighty power.
Way up on the mountain, God told Moses what the people were doing. So Moses hurried back down, carrying two flat stones with God's words written on them.
When Moses saw the golden calf and the wild party, his heart filled with sadness and anger.
He was so upset that he dropped the two stones, and they smashed into pieces on the ground.
Moses melted the golden calf in the fire until there was nothing left of it. Then he asked Aaron, "Why did you let the people do such a terrible thing?"
The next day, Moses went back up the mountain. "Please forgive your people," he begged God. He loved them so much.
And God, who is slow to get angry and full of love, forgave the people and stayed with them. They had been quick to forget God, but God would never give up on them.

devotional.bigIdea: The people forgot God and made a pretend god of gold. But when Moses asked God to forgive them, God did. God is patient and loving, even when we mess up.
questions: The people made a god they could see. Why is it better to trust the real God we cannot see?
Moses asked God to forgive his friends. Who prays for you?
How does it feel to know God forgives us when we are sorry?

connections.title: The Ten Commandments
connections.why: The people break the very first command, to worship God alone, before Moses even gets down the mountain with the stone tablets.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Like the father who runs to his lost son, God shows himself to be slow to anger and full of love, forgiving a people who turned away.

connections.title: Water From the Rock
connections.why: Moses keeps standing between God and a stubborn people, pleading for them again and again on the long desert road.

quiz.question: Why did the people make a golden calf?
options: They were tired of waiting for Moses
God asked them to
They were hungry

quiz.question: What did Moses do when he saw the calf?
options: He dropped and broke the stone tablets
He danced too
He hid

quiz.question: What did God do when Moses asked him to forgive the people?
options: He forgave them
He left them
He got a new people

stories.title: Water From the Rock
stories.hook: The people were very thirsty and there was no water anywhere. Then God told Moses to hit a rock with his stick.
story: The Israelites were travelling through the desert and they stopped at a place called Rephidim. There was no water there. Not a little water. No water at all.

The people were very thirsty and very grumpy. They turned on Moses and said, "Why did you bring us out of Egypt? To make us and our children and our animals die of thirst?" Moses was in real trouble. The people were so angry they wanted to throw rocks at him.
Moses called out to God. "What should I do? These people are about to throw rocks at me!" God told Moses exactly what to do.

God said, "Take some of the leaders with you and walk ahead to a rock at Horeb. Take your staff, the same stick you used at the Red Sea. Hit the rock with it. Water will come out and the people will have something to drink."
Moses did exactly what God said. He walked to the rock with the leaders watching. He lifted his staff and hit the rock.

Water poured out of the rock. Enough water for all the people and all the animals in the desert. Clean, fresh water pouring out of a hard rock in the middle of the desert, because Moses hit it and God made it happen.
Moses called the place by two names that meant testing and arguing, because the people had tested God by asking, "Is God actually here with us or not?" The water answered that question.

Many years later, a man named Paul wrote something surprising. He said that the rock in the desert was pointing to Jesus. Jesus is the one who gives living water that never runs out, not just water for thirsty bodies but water for thirsty hearts. Jesus stood up one day and called out, "If anyone is thirsty, come to me and drink."

devotional.bigIdea: The people forgot what God had done the moment things got hard again. God gave them water from a rock. He keeps taking care of people who keep forgetting He takes care of them.
questions: The people forgot about the Red Sea as soon as they were thirsty. Have you ever forgotten something good that happened because something hard came along? What was it?
Moses called out to God when he was worried instead of just giving up. What do you do when something feels really hard or scary?
Jesus said, "If anyone is thirsty, come to me." What kind of thirst do you think He was talking about?

connections.title: The Burning Bush
connections.why: The same staff Moses used at the burning bush and the Red Sea was the one God told him to use on the rock.

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: Paul says the rock was pointing to Christ, connecting the water in the wilderness to Jesus who offers living water.

connections.title: The Woman at the Well
connections.why: Jesus offered living water that would never run dry, the real thing the desert rock was only pointing toward.

quiz.question: Why were the people so angry with Moses at Rephidim?
options: Because Moses had gotten them lost in the desert
Because there was no water and they were afraid of dying of thirst
Because Moses had taken their food
Because Moses would not let them rest
quiz.explanation: There was no water at all. The people were so thirsty and scared that they turned on Moses and started arguing.

quiz.question: What did God tell Moses to use to get water from the rock?
options: His hands pressed flat against the stone
A prayer spoken out loud to the rock
His staff, the same one he used at the Red Sea
A bowl of water poured over it
quiz.explanation: God told Moses to use his staff and hit the rock. The same staff used at the Red Sea was chosen again here.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say that connects to this story?
options: I am the bread of life
I am the light of the world
Come to me and I will give you rest
If anyone is thirsty, come to me and drink
quiz.explanation: Jesus stood up one day and said if anyone is thirsty, come to me and drink. He is the living water the rock in the desert was pointing toward.

stories.title: The Twelve Spies
stories.hook: God's people came right up to the edge of the wonderful new land God promised them. Twelve men went to explore it, but only two of them trusted God.
story: After many years in the desert, God's people finally came right up to the edge of the new land that God had promised to give them.
God told Moses to choose twelve men and send them ahead to explore the land and see what it was like.
For forty days, the twelve men walked all around the land. It was beautiful, just like God had said, and it was full of good things to eat.
The men even found a bunch of grapes so big and so heavy that it took two men to carry it on a pole between them!
But when the twelve men came back, ten of them were very scared. "There are giants in that land," they said, "and huge cities with walls as tall as the sky! We can never go in there."
When the people heard this, they got very afraid. They cried all night long, and they forgot how big and strong their God was.
But two of the men, Caleb and Joshua, were brave and full of faith. "Do not be afraid!" they said. "God is with us. He will help us go in and live there."
Sadly, the people listened to their fear instead of trusting God.
Because they would not trust him, the people had to wait out in the desert for a very long time before they could go into the good land.
But Caleb and Joshua had trusted God. And one day, they got to go into the wonderful land God promised.
The giants were big, that was true. But God was even bigger than any giant.
Caleb and Joshua had seen the very same giants that everyone else had seen. They had simply remembered that their God was even bigger.
And whenever we feel scared, we can remember that our God is bigger than anything that frightens us.

devotional.bigIdea: Everyone saw the same giants. But Caleb and Joshua remembered that God was even bigger than the giants. We can trust that God is bigger than anything that scares us.
questions: Why do you think the ten men were so scared?
Caleb and Joshua were brave because they trusted God. What is something scary that God can help you with?
How do you remember that God is with you when you feel afraid?

connections.title: The Walls Come Down
connections.why: Only Caleb and Joshua trusted God here, and forty years later Joshua is the one who finally leads Israel into the land.

connections.title: Crossing the Jordan River
connections.why: The fearful generation never entered the land, but their children, led by Joshua, cross the river and go in.

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: God had already split the sea for these people, yet they still would not trust him with the giants ahead.

quiz.question: What did the men bring back to show the land was good?
options: A giant bunch of grapes
A pile of rocks
A baby lion

quiz.question: Which two men trusted God and were brave?
options: Caleb and Joshua
Moses and Aaron
Adam and Eve

quiz.question: What should we remember when we are scared?
options: God is bigger than anything
We should run away
Giants always win

stories.title: The Bronze Snake
stories.hook: When snakes came into the camp, God gave the people a surprising way to be saved. All they had to do was look up.
story: The people of Israel had been traveling through the desert for a long, long time, and they were getting grumpy and tired.
They complained against God again, even about the good food that he sent them fresh every single day.
Because of all their grumbling, snakes came slithering into the camp. Many of the people got hurt, and everyone was very scared.
The people ran to Moses and said, "We are so sorry! We should not have complained against God. Please ask him to help us."
So Moses prayed to God for the people. And God gave Moses a surprising answer.
God said, "Make a snake out of shiny bronze, and put it up high on a tall pole."
Then God made a promise. "Anyone who has been hurt can look up at the bronze snake, and they will get better."
So Moses made the bronze snake and lifted it up high on the pole, right in the middle of the camp where everyone could see it.
There was no medicine to take and nothing hard to do. The people who were hurt just looked up at the snake, and they got well again!
All they had to bring was their trust. Looking up was their way of saying, "God, I believe that you can save me."
Long, long after this, Jesus told this very story. He said that he would be lifted up high too, on a cross.
It was such a simple thing to do, just to look up at the pole and trust God. But every single person who looked was saved.
And just like the people who looked at the snake and lived, everyone who looks to Jesus and trusts him will be saved. When we need help, we can always look to Jesus.

devotional.bigIdea: All the people had to do was look up and trust God, and they were saved. We are saved the same way, by looking to Jesus.
questions: The people complained even about good things. What is something good God has given you to be thankful for?
God's cure was just to look up. Why is it good that God makes the way simple?
What does it mean to you to look to Jesus when you are scared?

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Jesus said that just as Moses lifted the snake on a pole, he would be lifted up on the cross, so that all who look to him in trust would live.

connections.title: Water From the Rock
connections.why: Once again the people grumble on the hard desert road, and once again God answers their complaining with mercy.

connections.title: Bread from Heaven
connections.why: The people complain about the very manna God sends them each day, forgetting that it is a daily gift from heaven.

quiz.question: What came into the camp when the people grumbled?
options: Snakes
Bears
Birds

quiz.question: What did God tell Moses to put up on a pole?
options: A bronze snake
A flag
A lamp

quiz.question: What did the people do to get better?
options: Looked up at the snake
Ran away fast
Took medicine

stories.title: Balaam's Donkey
stories.hook: A king wanted a man named Balaam to say bad words against God's people. But God had a very surprising helper to stop him: a donkey that could talk!
story: God's people set up their camp near a land called Moab. The king of Moab was very afraid of them, because there were so very many of them.
So the king sent for a man named Balaam. He offered Balaam lots and lots of money to come and say bad, cursing words against God's people.
But God told Balaam, "Do not curse them. They are my people, and they are blessed."
Still, Balaam really wanted that money. So he got on his donkey and started off to go anyway.
God was not happy about this. So an angel with a shiny sword came and stood right in the middle of the road. But only the donkey could see the angel, not Balaam!
To stay safe, the donkey kept turning away from the angel. But Balaam could not see why, so he hit his donkey to make her keep going.
