This happened three times. The poor donkey was just trying to keep Balaam safe!
Then God did something truly amazing. He let the donkey talk! "Why are you hitting me?" she asked Balaam.
Then God opened Balaam's eyes, and at last he could see the angel standing in the road. Balaam was very sorry.
His donkey had been protecting him the whole time, and he had not even known it.
After that, every single time Balaam opened his mouth to say bad words about God's people, only good and happy words came out instead!
Try as he might, the king could not get even one bad word said against God's people. God's blessing on them was far too strong to be broken.
No one in the whole world can curse the people God has blessed. God was taking care of his people, even when they did not know it.

devotional.bigIdea: A king with lots of money could not hurt God's people, because God was protecting them. God even used a talking donkey to keep Balaam safe. God watches over us all the time.
questions: Only the donkey could see the angel. Has anyone smaller than you ever noticed something you missed?
God protected his people from something they did not even know about. How does it feel that God is watching over you?
What was the most surprising part of this story to you?

connections.title: A Promise as Big as the Stars
connections.why: God told Abraham he would bless those who blessed his family and curse those who cursed them, and now no one can curse what God has blessed.

connections.title: The Burning Bush
connections.why: Just as God once spoke from a bush that would not burn up, here he speaks through the mouth of a donkey, using the unexpected to get our attention.

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: The same God who fought for his people at the sea now protects them from a curse they never even knew was coming.

quiz.question: What did the king want Balaam to do?
options: Say bad words against God's people
Build a boat
Sing a song

quiz.question: Who could see the angel in the road?
options: The donkey
Balaam
The king

quiz.question: What surprising thing did God let the donkey do?
options: Talk
Fly
Dance

stories.title: Crossing the Jordan River
stories.hook: God's people came to a big river they had to cross to reach their new home. The river was deep and wide, but God made a dry path right through the middle.
story: After many years of traveling, God's people finally came to a big river called the Jordan. They needed to cross it to reach the new home God had promised them.
But the river was very deep and wide and rushing fast, and there was no bridge and no boats anywhere.
Their brave new leader was a man named Joshua. God told him exactly what to do.
"Have the priests carry the special golden chest, the ark, into the river first," God said.
So the priests lifted up the ark and carried it down to the edge of the rushing water. Then they stepped right in.
And the very moment their feet touched the river, the water stopped flowing! It piled up high, far away, and the ground in the middle of the river became dry.
Then all of God's people walked across the river on the dry ground. They stayed perfectly safe and perfectly dry!
God told Joshua to have twelve men each pick up a big stone from the middle of the river.
They piled the stones up on the other side, to help everyone remember this amazing day forever.
"In the future," said Joshua, "when your children ask, what are these stones for, you can tell them how God stopped the river."
Long ago, God had stopped the sea so the people's parents could escape from Egypt. And now God stopped the river so the children could go into the promised land.
What an amazing sight it must have been, a whole nation of people walking across a riverbed that had been deep, rushing water just moments before!
God was showing his people that he would always be with them and always take care of them, no matter what.

devotional.bigIdea: God stopped a whole river so his people could cross safely. He gave them stones to help them remember, so they would never forget how good he is.
questions: How do you think the people felt walking through the middle of a river on dry ground?
The stones helped them remember God's help. What helps you remember the good things God does?
God took care of his people again and again. How has God taken care of you?

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: Forty years earlier God split the sea for their parents, and now he stops a flooding river so the children can cross on dry ground.

connections.title: The Walls Come Down
connections.why: The first thing waiting on the far side of the river is the great walled city of Jericho, Israel's first test in the new land.

connections.title: The Twelve Spies
connections.why: This is the generation the fearful spies never believed in, the children who grew up and finally trusted God enough to go in.

quiz.question: What did the people need to cross to reach their new home?
options: A big river
A tall mountain
A wide desert

quiz.question: What happened when the priests stepped into the water?
options: The river stopped flowing
It started to rain
They swam across

quiz.question: Why did they pile up twelve stones?
options: To help everyone remember
To build a house
To play a game

stories.title: The Scarlet Thread
stories.hook: Two spies were hiding in a city that Israel needed to take. A woman with a complicated past hid them. Then she tied a red rope in her window.
story: After forty years in the desert, it was finally time. Moses had died, and Joshua was the new leader. The Israelites were camped right across the river from the land God had promised them. The first city in their way was Jericho, a city with very thick, very tall walls.
Joshua sent two spies across the river to have a look around. The spies went into the city and came to the house of a woman named Rahab. Her house was actually built right into the city wall.

But the king of Jericho found out that spies were in the city. He sent soldiers to Rahab's house. Rahab had already hidden the spies under piles of grain drying on her roof. She told the soldiers the men had already left and if they hurried they could probably catch them. The soldiers ran off. The spies were safe.

Then Rahab went up to the roof and talked to the spies. She told them something surprising. She said everyone in the city was scared. They had all heard about God drying up the Red Sea. They had heard what God did to the armies that stood against Israel. She said, "Your God is the real God. He is God in heaven above and on earth below. I know it."
She had never met God. She had only heard the stories. But she believed them. And she had hidden the spies at great risk to herself.

She asked only one thing. "When you come back to take this city, please keep me and my family safe." The spies agreed. They gave her a sign. Tie a red rope in your window. Anyone inside your house when we come will be kept safe.
Rahab lowered them by a rope out of the window and they escaped. Then she tied the red rope in the window and waited.

When Jericho fell, Joshua kept the promise. Rahab and her whole family were brought out safely. The woman with the red rope in her window walked out alive. And the Bible tells us that Rahab became part of the family of Jesus Himself.

devotional.bigIdea: Rahab had only heard stories about God. But she believed them and acted on them. God does not need a perfect past to write someone into His story.
questions: Rahab believed in God just from hearing stories about what He had done. Have you ever believed something because someone told you about it even though you did not see it yourself?
The red rope in Rahab's window kept her family safe. What do you think that red rope was pointing toward?
Rahab risked her life to help the spies because she trusted God. When have you been brave for someone else?

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Rahab is in the Bible's list of great faith heroes. The red rope points to the blood of Jesus.

connections.title: The Walls Come Down
connections.why: Rahab's faith came before the walls fell. She believed before she saw God act.

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: Rahab is named in the family line of Jesus in Matthew's Gospel. God chose her on purpose.

quiz.question: Where did Rahab hide the two spies?
options: Under piles of grain drying on her roof
In a cave outside the city walls
In a room under the floor
In a barrel of water
quiz.explanation: Rahab hid the two spies under piles of grain that were drying on her flat roof, then sent the soldiers in the wrong direction.

quiz.question: What sign did the spies give Rahab to keep her family safe?
options: A white stone placed above her door
A red rope tied in her window
A lit candle left burning
Her name written on the wall
quiz.explanation: The spies told Rahab to tie a red rope in her window. Anyone inside that house would be kept safe when Israel came.

quiz.question: How did Rahab know that God was real even before she met any of God's people?
options: She had a dream about God one night
She saw a rainbow over the Israelite camp
She had heard the stories about what God did at the Red Sea
An angel appeared to her
quiz.explanation: Rahab said everyone in Jericho had heard what God did at the Red Sea and they were all scared. She heard the stories and believed them.

stories.title: The Walls Come Down
stories.hook: God's battle plan for Jericho was this: walk around the city, blow some trumpets, and shout. The people did it. The walls fell flat.
story: Jericho had its gates shut tight. Nobody was going in and nobody was going out. The whole city was scared because of what they had heard about God and His people.

God gave Joshua the strangest battle plan anyone had ever heard. He said, "March around the city once a day for six days. Seven priests carrying trumpets made from rams' horns walk in front of the ark. On the seventh day, march around seven times with the priests blowing their trumpets. When they blow a long loud blast, have all the people shout as loud as they can. The wall will fall down."

No ladders to climb over. No battering rams to smash the gates. No arrows. Just walking and trumpets and a shout. If anyone had explained this plan to a general, the general would have laughed. But Joshua told the people what God said and they obeyed.

For six days they marched around the city once each day. The priests blew their trumpets. The ark went with them. The people did not say a single word. Just footsteps and trumpets going round and round. Six days.

On the seventh day, they got up before the sun was even up and marched around the city seven times. The seventh time, when the priests blew the long blast, Joshua called out to everyone: "Shout! God has given you this city!" And the people shouted with everything they had.

The walls fell down flat. Every part of them. The armies of Israel ran straight in. Joshua remembered his promise to Rahab. The two spies went straight to her house, the one with the red rope in the window, and brought her and her whole family out safely.

The walls did not fall because Israel had a great army. They fell because God said they would. And because the people trusted what God said enough to shout about it before they could even see it happening.

devotional.bigIdea: Israel shouted before the walls fell, not after. They trusted what God said before they could see it come true. That is what faith looks like.
questions: The people marched around the walls for six whole days and nothing happened. Can you imagine how that must have felt? Have you ever kept going at something even when it seemed like it was not working?
God's plan sounded very strange. Walk around and shout. Would you have followed that plan? Why or why not?
The people shouted before the walls fell, not after. What is the difference between celebrating something God said and only celebrating something you can already see?

connections.title: The Scarlet Thread
connections.why: Rahab's red rope was in the window when the walls fell. She was safe in the middle of everything because she trusted God before it happened.

connections.title: Three Hundred Men and Torches
connections.why: Joshua said the Lord has given you the city before one wall had fallen, just like Gideon was told the victory was his before the battle.

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: The walls fell by faith, just like the Red Sea opened by faith. Both times the people trusted what God said before they could see what God would do.

quiz.question: What was God's battle plan for taking the city of Jericho?
options: Build a big ramp and climb over the walls
Sneak in at night through a secret tunnel
Wait until the people inside ran out of food
March around it, blow trumpets, and shout on the seventh day
quiz.explanation: March around once a day for six days, then seven times on the seventh day, blow the trumpets, and shout. No weapons needed.

quiz.question: What did the people do for the first six days as they marched?
options: They made no sound at all except for the trumpets
They shouted war cries to scare the people inside
They prayed loudly together as they walked
They sang songs of praise
quiz.explanation: Joshua told the people not to say a single word until the day he told them to shout. Six days of silence and just the sound of trumpets.

quiz.question: What happened when the people shouted on the seventh day?
options: The gates swung open but the walls stayed up
The walls fell completely flat to the ground
A crack appeared and soldiers squeezed through
The people inside the city ran away
quiz.explanation: The walls fell flat. Every part of them. The armies ran straight in because there was nothing left to stop them.

stories.title: Deborah the Judge
stories.hook: God's people were in trouble and needed a brave leader. God chose a wise woman named Deborah to help save them.
story: After Joshua died, God's people kept forgetting God and getting into trouble. But God was kind, and he sent special helpers called judges to rescue them.
One of the very best judges was a wise woman named Deborah.
Deborah would sit under a big palm tree, and people came from all around to ask her to help them solve their problems and to hear from God.
In those days, a mean king and his army had been hurting God's people for a long, long time. The army had nine hundred strong iron chariots, and the people were very scared.
So Deborah called for a soldier named Barak. "God says to take an army to the top of the mountain," she told him. "And God will help you win!"
But Barak was afraid to go. "I will only go," he said, "if you come with me."
So brave Deborah went with him to the battle.
When it was time to fight, Deborah said, "Go now! This is the day God is going to help you win!"
Then God sent a big rainstorm. The ground turned to thick, sticky mud, and all the enemy's heavy chariots got stuck and could not move at all!
So God's people won the battle, and at last they were safe. God had rescued them again.
Then Deborah sang a happy song to thank God for saving his people.
Everyone had been so afraid of those nine hundred iron chariots. But God showed that he is stronger than the biggest army in the world, and that he can rescue his people in ways no one ever expects.
God can use anyone who trusts him, men or women, young or old, to do brave and wonderful things.

devotional.bigIdea: God used a wise woman named Deborah and a little rainstorm to save his people from a great big army. God can use anyone who is willing to trust him.
questions: Barak was braver when Deborah went with him. Who helps you be brave?
God used a rainstorm to stop the scary chariots. What is something big that is small to God?
God used Deborah in a special way. What do you think God might use you for?

connections.title: Three Hundred Men and Torches
connections.why: Like Gideon after her, Deborah is one of the judges God raised up to rescue his people whenever they cried out to him.

connections.title: Wherever You Go
connections.why: Ruth's story takes place in these same days of the judges, when God was quietly at work through ordinary, faithful people.

connections.title: The Walls Come Down
connections.why: Just as God handed Jericho to Joshua, he hands Sisera's mighty army to a people who march out trusting his word.

quiz.question: What was Deborah?
options: A wise judge who helped God's people
A farmer
A queen of Egypt

quiz.question: How did God stop the enemy's chariots?
options: With a rainstorm and mud
With a big wind
With fire

quiz.question: What can God do with anyone who trusts him?
options: Use them for brave and wonderful things
Nothing much
Make them a king

stories.title: Three Hundred Men and Torches
stories.hook: God told a frightened farmer to fight a huge army of 135,000 men. Then God made him get rid of almost all of his own soldiers first.
story: Israel had gotten into a bad pattern. They would forget God, get in trouble, cry out to God, and God would send someone to help them. Then they would forget again. This time the trouble was a people called the Midianites, who kept sweeping in like a swarm of locusts and taking everything. Israel was hiding in caves just to survive.

God's answer came to a man named Gideon. Gideon was hiding underground, trying to do his farm work without being seen. An angel appeared and said, "Hello, mighty warrior!" Gideon looked around the underground pit he was hiding in and said, "If God is with us, why is all this bad stuff happening?"

The angel did not answer the question. Instead he said, "Go fight them. I am sending you." Gideon said, "But I am the least important person in the least important family in the whole tribe." God said, "I will be with you."

Gideon gathered an army of 32,000 men. But God said, "Too many. If you win with this many, the people will think they did it themselves." God told Gideon to send home anyone who was scared. 22,000 men left. Only 10,000 stayed.

Still too many, said God. Take them to the water and watch how they drink. Most of the men knelt down and put their faces in the water. But 300 men scooped the water up with their hands and kept watching while they drank. God said, "Keep the 300. Send everyone else home."
Three hundred men against 135,000. That is like one person against 450 people.

God's battle plan was surprising again. In the middle of the night, divide into three groups, surround the whole camp, hide your torches inside clay pots, and at the signal, smash the pots, wave the torches, blow the trumpets, and shout.

In the darkness, 300 torches blazed up from every direction at once, 300 trumpets blared, and 300 voices cried out, "For God and for Gideon!" The enemy camp woke up in total confusion. In the chaos and fear, they started fighting each other and ran away. Israel won.

Gideon went from hiding in a pit to routing a whole army. The difference was not that Gideon became braver. The difference was that God was with him, and God had made sure that when the victory came, nobody could miss who had actually won it.

devotional.bigIdea: God made Gideon's army smaller on purpose so that everyone would know the win came from God, not from how many soldiers they had.
questions: God called Gideon a mighty warrior when Gideon was hiding in a pit. Has anyone ever believed in you more than you believed in yourself? How did that feel?
God kept making the army smaller. Why do you think God sometimes takes away the things we think we need before He helps us?
Gideon and his 300 men just shone lights and made noise and God won the battle. What does that tell you about who is really in charge?

connections.title: The Burning Bush
connections.why: God called Gideon the same way He called Moses. Both felt completely wrong for the job. Both went anyway.

connections.title: One Stone
connections.why: David and Gideon both faced impossible odds and both understood the same thing: the battle belongs to God.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: Paul says God chooses the weak things to show up the strong. Gideon's 300 is exactly that.

quiz.question: What was Gideon doing when the angel of the Lord appeared to him?
options: Praying at the front of his army
Leading his village in a celebration
Hiding underground doing farm work so the enemy would not see him
Sleeping in his bed
quiz.explanation: Gideon was hiding underground trying to do his farm work without being spotted by the enemy. Not exactly where you would expect a mighty warrior.

quiz.question: How many men were in Gideon's army when the battle actually started?
quiz.explanation: God kept reducing the army until only 300 were left, so that when Israel won, nobody could say it was because of how many soldiers they had.

quiz.question: What was Gideon's battle plan?
options: Surround the camp with torches, trumpets, and a shout in the darkness
Charge the camp at dawn with swords
Dig tunnels under the camp at night
Send a small group to the gates to ask them to surrender
quiz.explanation: In the dark of night, 300 torches blazed up from every direction at once with trumpets and shouting. The enemy panicked and ran.

stories.title: Samson's Strength
stories.hook: God gave a man named Samson amazing strength. As long as he kept his special promise to God, no one could beat him.
story: God's people were having a hard time, so God sent a special baby boy named Samson to help save them.
Before Samson was even born, an angel said that he must never, ever cut his hair. It was a special sign that Samson belonged to God.
When Samson grew up, God gave him amazing strength. He was the strongest man that anyone had ever seen!
As long as Samson kept his promise and did not cut his hair, no one could ever beat him.
But sadly, Samson did not always make good choices, and he did not always listen to God.
One day, a woman named Delilah kept asking Samson why he was so very strong. She asked him again and again.
Finally, Samson told her his secret. "My strength comes from God," he said, "and the sign is my long hair, which has never been cut."
That night, while Samson was sleeping, his enemies came and cut off his hair. When Samson woke up, his great strength was gone.
His enemies caught him and put him in prison. It was a very sad time for Samson.
But slowly, while he was in prison, his hair began to grow back again.
One day, his enemies brought Samson out to laugh at him in their big building. Samson prayed, "God, please give me my strength just one more time."
Even after all the foolish mistakes that Samson had made, God had never forgotten him, and God still answered him when he finally called out for help.

And God did! Samson pushed against the big stone pillars, and the whole building came tumbling down. Even after all of Samson's mistakes, God still listened when he prayed. God is strong, and he loves to help us when we ask him.

devotional.bigIdea: God gave Samson amazing strength. Samson made many bad choices, but when he finally prayed, God still helped him. God loves to help us when we ask him.
questions: God gave Samson special strength. What special things has God given you?
Samson forgot to listen to God. What are some ways we can listen to God every day?
When Samson prayed, God helped him. How does it feel to know God hears your prayers?

connections.title: Three Hundred Men and Torches
connections.why: Like Gideon, Samson is one of the judges, but his story is a warning that God's gifts are wasted when we will not follow him.

connections.title: Deborah the Judge
connections.why: Samson comes in the same long age of the judges, when God kept rescuing a people who kept forgetting him.

connections.title: One Stone
connections.why: Like David against Goliath, Samson's victories were never really about human muscle. The power always belonged to God.

quiz.question: What made Samson so strong?
options: God gave him strength, shown by his uncut hair
He ate lots of vegetables
A magic ring

quiz.question: What was the secret of Samson's strength?
options: His long hair
His big shoes
His sword

quiz.question: When Samson prayed at the end, did God help him?
options: Yes, God gave him strength again
No, it was too late
God was asleep

stories.title: Wherever You Go
stories.hook: Ruth's husband died. Her mother-in-law's husband died too. Ruth could have gone home. She did not.
story: A woman named Naomi had moved far from home with her husband and two sons. Then her husband died. Then her two sons died. Naomi was left with just her two daughters-in-law, Orpah and Ruth.
Naomi decided to go back to her home in Bethlehem. She told Orpah and Ruth to go back to their own families. Orpah cried and hugged Naomi goodbye and went home.

But Ruth held on tight and would not let go. Naomi said go back to your own people. Ruth said something people have been repeating for thousands of years. She said, "Do not ask me to leave you. Wherever you go, I will go. Wherever you live, I will live. Your people will be my people. And your God will be my God."
So they went to Bethlehem together, just the two of them, with nothing. When they arrived Naomi told everyone to call her Bitter because her life had been so hard.

They needed food. Ruth went out to the fields to pick up leftover grain that harvesters dropped. This was how poor people could get food. She ended up in a field belonging to a good man named Boaz, who was a relative of Naomi's family.

Boaz noticed Ruth. He heard her whole story. He told his workers to leave extra grain for her on purpose and make sure nobody bothered her. He told her, "I have heard how kind you have been to Naomi. May God reward you for it."
When Ruth told Naomi whose field it was, Naomi's eyes lit up. Boaz was a relative who had the right to take care of them. And he did. He married Ruth.
Ruth and Boaz had a baby boy named Obed. Naomi held the baby and her neighbours said, "Naomi has a family again!" The woman who said call me Bitter got to hold joy in her arms.

Obed grew up and had a son named Jesse. Jesse had a son named David. And David's family line eventually led all the way to Jesus. A young woman who chose loyalty became part of the most important family in the whole story.

devotional.bigIdea: Ruth chose to stay with Naomi even when it cost her everything. Loyal love like that is one of the most beautiful things in the world.
questions: Ruth left her home and everything she knew to stay with Naomi. Have you ever given something up to help someone you love?
Boaz was extra kind to Ruth because he heard about how loyal she had been. Do you think people notice when you are kind? Does God notice?
Ruth and Naomi had a very hard time but the story ends with a baby and a happy grandmother. What does that tell you about how God works in sad stories?

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Ruth's commitment to Naomi is the same kind of love the father showed his son who came home.

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: Ruth is in the family line of Jesus. God chose her on purpose.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Boaz paid to redeem what was lost for Naomi and Ruth. Jesus is the one who paid to redeem us.

quiz.question: What did Ruth say when Naomi told her to go back to her own family?
options: She agreed to leave but promised to visit soon
Wherever you go I will go, your people will be my people and your God my God
She said she would stay until Naomi reached Bethlehem safely
She said she had nowhere else to go
quiz.explanation: Ruth said wherever you go I will go. She chose to stay with Naomi and follow Naomi's God even though she did not have to.

quiz.question: Why was Boaz extra kind to Ruth in the field?
options: Because she worked harder than anyone else
Because she was related to someone he knew
Because he heard how loyal and kind she had been to Naomi
Because Naomi had asked him to help her
quiz.explanation: Boaz had heard the whole story of how Ruth left her own family to take care of Naomi. He wanted to bless someone who had been so kind.

quiz.question: What was the name of the famous grandson of Ruth and Boaz?
options: Moses
Solomon
Abraham
David
quiz.explanation: Ruth and Boaz had a son named Obed, who had a son named Jesse, who had a son named David, the greatest king Israel ever had.

stories.title: Hannah's Prayer
stories.hook: Hannah wanted a baby more than anything. So she went to God's house and prayed a quiet prayer that only God could hear.
story: There was a woman named Hannah who loved God with her whole heart. More than anything else in the whole world, she wanted to have a baby of her own.
But year after year went by, and Hannah had no children. It made her very, very sad, and sometimes she cried and cried.
One day, Hannah went to God's house all by herself. She wanted to talk to God about the sadness deep inside her heart.
Hannah prayed and prayed. "Dear God," she prayed, "if you give me a son, I promise I will give him right back to you, to serve you his whole life long."
Hannah prayed so quietly that her lips were moving but no sound came out. Only God could hear her quiet prayer.
The priest, whose name was Eli, was watching. He thought something was wrong, but Hannah said, "I am just praying to God from the bottom of my heart."
Then Eli said something kind. "Go in peace," he told her, "and may God give you what you have asked him for."
Hannah went home, and she was not sad anymore. She had given her heavy worry to God, and she trusted him with it.
And God heard Hannah's prayer! After a while, Hannah had a baby boy. She named him Samuel, and she was so happy and thankful.
Hannah did not forget the promise she had made to God. When Samuel was still a little boy, she brought him to God's house so he could serve God there, just as she had promised.
It must have been so hard to say goodbye. But Hannah trusted God with the very gift she had begged for.
Then Hannah sang a beautiful, happy song to thank God. "My heart is so full of joy in the Lord!" she sang.
God had heard Hannah's quiet prayer and answered it. And God hears every one of our prayers too, even the quiet ones.

devotional.bigIdea: Hannah told God exactly how she felt and trusted him with her biggest wish. God heard her quiet prayer. God hears our prayers too, even the quiet ones.
questions: Hannah told God how sad she was. How does it help to tell God how you feel?
Hannah kept her promise to God even though it was hard. What is a promise you can keep?
God heard Hannah's quiet prayer. How does it feel to know God always hears you?

connections.title: Speak, Lord
connections.why: The son God gives Hannah is Samuel, who grows up to hear God's voice and anoint Israel's first kings.

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: Hannah's song of praise, that God lifts up the lowly and fills the hungry, is echoed centuries later in Mary's song.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Hannah sings that God raises the poor from the dust, the same upside-down kingdom Jesus would teach about on the mountain.

quiz.question: What did Hannah want most of all?
options: A baby
Gold
A castle

quiz.question: How quietly did Hannah pray?
options: So quietly only God could hear
Very loudly
In a shout

quiz.question: What did Hannah do after her son was born?
options: Kept her promise and gave him to serve God
Hid him
Forgot her promise

stories.title: Speak, Lord
stories.hook: A boy kept hearing his name called in the night. He thought it was the old priest. It was not.
story: A boy named Samuel lived in the house of God and helped an old priest named Eli. Samuel's mother had prayed very hard for a baby and God gave her Samuel. She brought him to live in God's house as a way of saying thank you.

One night Samuel was lying down to sleep when he heard his name called. "Samuel!" He jumped up and ran to Eli. "Here I am! You called me!" Eli rubbed his eyes and said, "I did not call you. Go back to bed." Samuel went back to bed.
The voice called again. "Samuel!" He got up and ran to Eli again. "Here I am! You called me." Eli said, "I did not call you, my boy. Go back to sleep."

A third time the voice called. A third time Samuel ran to Eli. This time the old priest understood. God was calling the boy. Eli told Samuel, "Go and lie down. If the voice calls again, say: Speak, Lord. I am listening."
Samuel went and lay down. The voice came again. "Samuel! Samuel!" Samuel said exactly what Eli had told him. "Speak. I am listening."

God talked to Samuel that night. What He said was hard. He told Samuel something sad was going to happen to Eli's family because of choices Eli had made. Samuel lay still until morning, not sure he wanted to tell Eli what he had heard.
But Eli asked him. He said, "What did God say? Do not hide anything from me." Samuel told him everything. Eli said, "He is God. He will do what is right."

Samuel grew up and God kept talking to him. All of Israel knew that Samuel was a prophet, someone God spoke through. The boy who said speak Lord, I am listening became one of the most important people in Israel's story.

devotional.bigIdea: Samuel's whole prayer was five words: Speak, Lord. I am listening. That is a prayer anyone can pray, even tonight.
questions: Samuel heard God's voice but did not recognise it at first. How do you think we learn to recognise God's voice?
Samuel said speak Lord, I am listening. Do you ever get quiet enough to listen? What makes that hard?
Samuel had to tell Eli something hard and sad. Has someone ever trusted you to tell them something difficult? What did you do?

connections.title: The Burning Bush
connections.why: Moses turned aside to look at the bush. Samuel said speak Lord I am listening. Both stories turn on paying attention to God.

connections.title: Left Behind in Jerusalem
connections.why: Samuel grew in favour with God and people, the same words used later about Jesus growing up.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: God promised sons and daughters would hear His voice. Samuel was the beginning of a long line of people who listened.

quiz.question: Why did Samuel keep running to Eli when he heard his name?
options: He had never heard God's voice before and thought it was Eli calling
He thought Eli needed help in the night
He was sleepwalking
He was playing a game
quiz.explanation: Samuel had never heard God speak before. He did not know who was calling him so he ran to the nearest grown-up he trusted.

quiz.question: What five words did Samuel say when he understood it was God calling?
options: Is that really you, God?
Speak, Lord. I am listening.
What do you want me to do?
Here I am, send me instead
quiz.explanation: Samuel said speak, Lord, I am listening. Five simple words that became the posture of his whole life.

quiz.question: What did Samuel become when he grew up?
options: A soldier who led Israel in battle
A king who ruled all of Israel
A prophet who God spoke through
A priest who served in the Temple
quiz.explanation: Samuel became a prophet. All of Israel knew that God spoke through him because everything he said came true.

stories.title: Israel's First King
stories.hook: God was already Israel's king. But the people wanted a king they could see, like all the other nations had.
story: For a long, long time, God himself was the king of his people. He led them and he took care of them, and he was the very best king they could ever have.
But the people looked at all the other nations around them. Those nations had kings with shiny crowns and tall thrones.
So the people went to the prophet Samuel and said, "We want a king too, just like all the other nations have!"
This made Samuel very sad. So he prayed about it, and God answered him.
"They are not really saying no to you," God told Samuel. "They are saying no to me as their king. They want to be like everyone else."
God told Samuel to warn the people first. "A king will take your sons for his army," Samuel said. "He will take your best fields and your animals, and one day you will be sorry."
But the people would not listen. "No!" they said. "We still want a king to rule over us!"
So God told Samuel to give them what they had asked for.
God chose a young man named Saul to be the king. Saul was very tall, taller than everyone else, and he looked just like a king should look.
And at first, Saul was humble. When the big day came to make him king, Saul was so shy that he went and hid behind the baggage!
The people had to go and find him and bring him out. Then Samuel made Saul the king, and everyone shouted, "Long live the king!"
Saul started out well. But the people would slowly learn an important lesson.
No king they could see with their eyes could ever take the place of God, their one true King, who loved them best of all.

devotional.bigIdea: The people wanted to be like everyone else and have a king they could see. But only God can truly be the King our hearts need.
questions: When do you want to be just like everyone else?
Saul was humble and even hid behind the bags. Why is it good to be humble?
God is the best King of all. What do you love about God being your King?

connections.title: Speak, Lord
connections.why: Samuel, the boy who once heard God's voice, is now the old prophet who warns the people and anoints their first king.

connections.title: God Looks at the Heart
connections.why: Saul looked exactly like a king should look, but God would soon choose David instead, looking past the outside to the heart.

connections.title: The Cave and the King
connections.why: Saul began so humbly, but jealousy of David would grow in him until he was chasing the very man God had chosen.

quiz.question: Who was Israel's king before they asked for one?
options: God himself
A giant
Samuel

quiz.question: Why did the people want a king?
options: To be like the other nations
To win a game
God told them to

quiz.question: Where did Saul hide when it was time to be king?
options: Among the baggage
In a cave
Up a tree

stories.title: God Looks at the Heart
stories.hook: Samuel went to pick the next king. Seven strong brothers walked by, but God was waiting for the youngest one, who was still out with the sheep.
story: King Saul had stopped following God and obeying him. So God had a brand new king in mind for his people.
God sent the prophet Samuel to a little town called Bethlehem, to the house of a man named Jesse.
"One of Jesse's sons will be the next king," God told Samuel. "I will show you which one."
When Samuel arrived, Jesse lined up his sons. The oldest one stepped forward first. He was tall and strong and handsome.
"Surely this is the one God has chosen!" Samuel thought to himself.
But God said, "No, not this one. Do not look at how tall he is or how he looks on the outside. People look at the outside, but I look at the heart."
So Jesse called his next son, and the next, and the next. Seven big, strong sons walked past Samuel, and each time, God said, "Not this one either."
Samuel was puzzled. "Are these all the sons you have?" he asked Jesse.
"There is still my youngest son," said Jesse. "His name is David, but he is out in the fields, watching the sheep."
Nobody had even thought to call David in! So they ran out to the fields to get him.
When David came in, with the wind and the sun on his face, God said, "This is the one! Stand up and anoint him."
So Samuel poured oil on David's head, right in front of all his big brothers, to show that David was God's chosen king.
All of David's tall, strong older brothers had stood there in line first. But God had been looking for something on the inside the whole time.
God did not pick David because he was the biggest or the strongest. God picked him because of what was in his heart. And God always sees what is inside each one of us.

devotional.bigIdea: Everyone looked at the big strong brothers, but God looked at David's heart. God does not care how we look on the outside. He sees who we really are inside.
questions: Why do we often look at how people look on the outside?
God looks at our hearts. What do you think God sees when he looks at your heart?
Everyone forgot about David, but God did not. How does it feel that God never forgets you?

connections.title: Israel's First King
connections.why: Saul looked like a king on the outside, but God now sends Samuel to find a king after his own heart instead.

connections.title: One Stone
connections.why: The shepherd boy anointed here is the same David who will soon face the giant Goliath with nothing but a sling and trust in God.

connections.title: The Lord Is My Shepherd
connections.why: David was out watching the sheep when God chose him, and the shepherd boy would grow up to write the most famous shepherd song of all.

quiz.question: What does God look at when he chooses someone?
options: The heart
How tall they are
Their clothes

quiz.question: Where was David when Samuel came?
options: Watching the sheep
Sleeping
Eating dinner

quiz.question: Why did God choose David?
options: Because of his heart
Because he was the biggest
Because he was the oldest

stories.title: One Stone
stories.hook: A giant soldier had been scaring the whole army for forty days. Then a boy with a sling and five smooth stones walked out to meet him.
story: The Philistine army had a secret weapon. His name was Goliath. He was taller than any man the Israelites had ever seen, covered head to foot in heavy armour, carrying a spear that most men could barely lift. Every morning and evening Goliath walked out and shouted his challenge across the valley. And every morning and evening the whole army of Israel ran away.

This had been going on for forty days when a young boy named David arrived at the camp. He had come to bring food to his older brothers who were soldiers. David heard Goliath shouting. He looked at all the scared soldiers and said, "Who does this man think he is? Why is everyone just standing here?"
David's big brother got angry. "Go back to your sheep!" But David kept asking questions until King Saul heard about him and called him in.

David told the king he would fight Goliath. Saul said, "You are just a boy." David said, "I have killed lions and bears with my bare hands to protect my sheep. God rescued me from the lion. He will rescue me from this giant too."

Saul put his own armour on David. It was huge and heavy and David could barely walk. He took it all off. He went to the stream and picked up five smooth stones and put them in his bag. He had his sling and he had God. That was enough.

When Goliath saw David coming he burst out laughing. "Am I a dog, that you come at me with a stick? Come here and I will feed you to the birds!" David answered, "You come at me with sword and spear. I come at you in the name of the God of Israel, the one you have been making fun of. Today God will hand you over to me."
David ran at Goliath. He put a stone in his sling, swung it around, and let it fly. The stone hit Goliath right in the middle of his forehead. The giant fell face down on the ground.
One stone. One boy who had learned to trust God with the lion and the bear before he ever faced a giant. The whole Philistine army turned and ran.

devotional.bigIdea: David was not brave because he had no fear. He was brave because he trusted God more than he was afraid of Goliath. That is a big difference.
questions: David had already faced lions and bears before he faced Goliath. Can you think of a small brave thing you have done that might be getting you ready for a bigger brave thing?
Everyone in the army saw the same giant. David saw a different battle. What made his thinking different from everyone else's?
David said I come at you in the name of God. What do you think it means to do something in God's name?

connections.title: Three Hundred Men and Torches
connections.why: David said the battle belongs to God, the same thing Gideon understood with his 300 men.

connections.title: The Burning Bush
connections.why: God chose David not for what people saw on the outside but for what was in his heart.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Paul asks who can be against us if God is for us. David lived that question out loud in the valley.

quiz.question: How long had Goliath been challenging the Israelite army before David arrived?
options: Seven days
One whole year
Three days
Forty days
quiz.explanation: Goliath had been walking out twice a day for forty days and the whole army had been too scared to face him.

quiz.question: What did David take with him to face Goliath?
options: Five smooth stones and his sling
King Saul's sword and armour
A spear borrowed from a soldier
Just his shepherd's staff
quiz.explanation: David chose five smooth stones from the stream and his sling. He refused the king's heavy armour.

quiz.question: What did David say when Goliath made fun of him?
options: I am not scared of you because I am stronger than I look
You come with weapons but I come in the name of God and the battle is His
God told me in a dream I would win today
I have beaten bigger enemies than you
quiz.explanation: David said you come with sword and spear but I come in the name of the Lord. He knew who was really fighting the battle.

stories.title: The Best of Friends
stories.hook: Jonathan was a prince, and David would one day be king in his place. But instead of being rivals, they became the very best of friends.
story: After David bravely beat the giant Goliath, he came to live at the palace of King Saul.
There, David met the king's son, Prince Jonathan. And from the very first day, something wonderful happened.
Jonathan loved David like his own brother, and the two of them became the very best of friends.
Jonathan even gave David his own royal robe, and his sword, and his bow. Jonathan was the prince, but he gladly shared everything he had with his friend.
But there was a problem. Everyone loved David and sang happy songs about him. And this made King Saul very, very jealous.
Saul got so jealous that he wanted to hurt David. Poor Jonathan was caught between his father and his best friend.
Jonathan chose to do what was right. He warned David that he was in danger, and he even spoke up for David to his father.
But Saul's jealousy came back, worse than before. So David and Jonathan made a secret plan to keep David safe.
Jonathan would shoot some arrows in a field. If he shot them far away, it would be a secret signal that David must run away to stay safe.
When Jonathan saw that his father really did want to hurt David, his heart was very sad. So he shot the arrows far out into the field.
That was the signal. David knew he had to run away.
Before David left, the two friends hugged each other and cried, because they did not know when they would see each other again.
"Go in peace," said Jonathan. "We will always be friends." David and Jonathan loved each other and helped each other, and that is exactly what good friends do.

devotional.bigIdea: Jonathan and David were the best of friends. Jonathan even gave up his own things and kept his friend safe. Real friends want what is best for each other.
questions: Jonathan shared his special things with David. How can you be a good friend by sharing?
Jonathan kept David safe. Who are the friends who help you?
What do you think makes someone a really good friend?

connections.title: One Stone
connections.why: It was right after David defeated Goliath that Prince Jonathan's heart was knit to David's in friendship.

connections.title: The Cave and the King
connections.why: Jonathan's own father, King Saul, grows so jealous of David that Jonathan must risk his life to protect his friend.

connections.title: Kindness for Jonathan's Sake
connections.why: David and Jonathan promise to be loyal forever, and years later David keeps that promise to Jonathan's son.

quiz.question: Who was David's best friend?
options: Jonathan
Goliath
Pharaoh

quiz.question: What did Jonathan give David?
options: His royal robe and sword
A bowl of soup
A boat

quiz.question: How did Jonathan warn David to run away?
options: With a signal using arrows
With a letter
With a song

stories.title: The Cave and the King
stories.hook: The king who wanted David dead walked into the same cave where David was hiding. David had the perfect chance. He did not take it.
story: After David killed Goliath something strange happened. King Saul started to hate David. David had not done anything wrong. He had served the king faithfully. But Saul was jealous because the people loved David, and scared because he knew God had chosen David.
Saul took thousands of his best soldiers into the wilderness to hunt David down. David and his small band of followers hid in caves to survive.
One day Saul came to the very cave where David and his men were hiding in the darkness at the back. Saul came in alone to rest. He had no idea David was there.

David's men whispered, "This is your chance! God has put your enemy right in front of you!" David crept forward in the dark. But he did not hurt Saul. He only cut off a small piece of Saul's robe. And even doing that made David feel bad.

David went back to his men. When Saul left the cave David came out and called after him. "My lord, the king!" Saul turned around. David bowed down. He held up the piece of robe. "See this? I could have hurt you in that cave but I did not. I will not lift my hand against you because God chose you to be king."

Saul stood there looking at the piece of his own robe. His eyes filled with tears. He said, "You are a better man than I am. You have done good to me and I have done evil to you. I know now that you will be king one day." Then he took his soldiers and went home.
David did not take what was his by grabbing it. He waited for God to give it. That took many more years. But when David finally became king everyone knew he had got there the right way.

devotional.bigIdea: David had the chance to take what he wanted. He chose to wait for God instead. Waiting for the right thing is sometimes the hardest and bravest choice.
questions: David's men said this is your chance. But David chose not to take it. Have you ever had to choose between taking something you wanted and doing the right thing?
David respected Saul even when Saul was being very unfair to him. Is it hard to be kind to someone who is not kind to you?
David waited a very long time before he became king. Why do you think it matters how we get what we want, not just that we get it?

connections.title: One Stone
connections.why: David killed Goliath because the battle was God's. He refused to kill Saul for the same reason. Both came from trusting God.

connections.title: The Lord Is My Shepherd
connections.why: David wrote Psalm 57 hiding in a cave from Saul. His most honest prayers came from his hardest places.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus said love your enemies. David practised this before Jesus ever said it.

quiz.question: Why was Saul trying to hurt David even though David had done nothing wrong?
options: David had stolen something from the palace
David had refused to fight in Saul's army
Saul was jealous because the people loved David and scared because God had chosen David
David had said something rude to the king
quiz.explanation: Saul was jealous and scared. Jealousy made Saul want to get rid of David.

quiz.question: What did David do when Saul came into the cave where David was hiding?
options: He jumped out and captured the king
He hid completely and did nothing at all
He called out to Saul and surrendered
He quietly cut off a small piece of Saul's robe but did not hurt him
quiz.explanation: David crept forward and cut off a small piece of Saul's robe. Even that small thing made David feel guilty.

quiz.question: What did Saul say when he saw the piece of his robe in David's hand?
options: He cried and said David was a better man than he was
He was furious and ordered his soldiers to attack
He said nothing and walked away
He promised to stop chasing David
quiz.explanation: Saul cried and said you are more righteous than I am, for you paid me good when I paid you evil. He admitted the truth.

stories.title: Dancing Before the Lord
stories.hook: When King David brought God's special golden chest home to the city, he was so happy that he danced with all his might, and he did not care who was watching!
story: David was now the king of all of God's people. He wanted to bring something very special home to his city, Jerusalem.
It was the ark of the covenant, a beautiful golden chest. The ark was a sign that God himself was right there with his people.
David wanted God to be at the very center of his city and his whole life.
So the people carried the ark carefully into the city, doing it the right way, just like God had said to.
And what a celebration it was! There were trumpets and harps and tambourines.
Everyone sang and shouted for joy as the ark of God came home to Jerusalem.
King David was so full of happiness that he danced and danced with all his might, right down the middle of the street!
He did not care one little bit how he looked. He just wanted to show God how much he loved him.
But David's wife, Michal, watched from a window. She thought a king should not dance around like that, and she felt embarrassed by him.
When David came home, she said, "You looked so silly today!"
But David said, "I was dancing for God! I do not care if I look silly. I just want to give God all my praise and honor."
David loved God so much that his joy came bursting right out of him.
Some people thought a king should always act serious and grand. But David knew that the very best way to honor God was to give him all of his love and all of his joy.
Worshiping God is not about looking proper or fancy. It is about loving God with your whole heart, exactly the way David did.

devotional.bigIdea: King David danced with all his might because he loved God so much. Worshiping God is about loving him with your whole heart, not about looking proper.
questions: David danced to show his love for God. What are fun ways you can worship God?
David did not care if he looked silly. Why do you think he was so happy?
How can you put God at the center of your day, like David did?

connections.title: God Looks at the Heart
connections.why: The shepherd boy God chose for his heart grows into a king whose heart overflows with joyful worship.

connections.title: The Lord Is My Shepherd
connections.why: David, who danced before the Lord with all his might, was also the singer and poet who gave us the Psalms.

connections.title: The Wisest Request
connections.why: David brings the ark to Jerusalem, and his son Solomon would later build the great Temple to be its permanent home.

quiz.question: What did David bring home to the city?
options: God's special golden chest, the ark
A new crown
A big drum

quiz.question: What did David do because he was so happy?
options: He danced with all his might
He took a nap
He hid

quiz.question: What did David care about most?
options: Honoring God, not looking proper
Looking like a fancy king
Winning a dance contest

stories.title: Kindness for Jonathan's Sake
stories.hook: King David made a promise to his best friend long ago. Now he kept that promise by being kind to someone everyone else had forgotten.
story: David was now the king. He was safe and strong, sitting on his throne in the palace.
One day, David remembered his very best friend, Jonathan, who had died. Long ago, David had made Jonathan a promise to always be kind to his family.
So David asked his servants, "Is there anyone left from Jonathan's family that I can show kindness to?"
There was one person. Jonathan had a son named Mephibosheth, who was all grown up now.
When Mephibosheth was just a little boy, he had been hurt in both of his feet, and he could not walk.
For a long time, Mephibosheth had been hiding far away. He was scared that the new king would want to get rid of him.
So David sent for him. Poor Mephibosheth was shaking with fear as he was carried in before the king. He was sure he was in big trouble.
But David said gently, "Do not be afraid! I am going to be kind to you, because I loved your father, Jonathan."
"I am giving you back all of your grandfather's land," said David. "And from now on, you will eat here at my table with me every single day, just like one of my own sons."
Mephibosheth could hardly believe his ears! "Why would you be so kind to someone like me?" he asked.
He had walked in expecting the worst, and instead he was given a home, and land, and a special place at the king's table forever.
Mephibosheth did nothing to earn all that kindness. It was all because of David's love for his friend.
And that is exactly how God welcomes us to be with him, not because we have earned it, but simply because he loves us so very much.

devotional.bigIdea: Mephibosheth thought he was in trouble, but David gave him a place at the king's table because of love. That is how God welcomes us, because he loves us, not because we earned it.
questions: Mephibosheth was scared, but David was kind. When has someone surprised you with kindness?
David kept his promise to his friend. What is a promise you can keep?
God gives us a special place with him because he loves us. How does that make you feel?

connections.title: The Best of Friends
connections.why: David promised his friend Jonathan to be loyal to his family forever, and now, years later, he keeps that promise.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Like the father who welcomes his lost son to the feast, David welcomes a frightened, forgotten man to eat at the king's table.

connections.title: The Man in the Tree
connections.why: Just as Jesus invited himself to the table of an outcast, David invites the last of Saul's family to share his own table.

quiz.question: Why was David kind to Mephibosheth?
options: Because he loved Jonathan, Mephibosheth's father
Because he had to be
Because Mephibosheth was rich

quiz.question: What was special about Mephibosheth?
options: He could not walk
He was a giant
He was a king

quiz.question: What wonderful thing did David give him?
options: A place at the king's table forever
A horse
A crown

stories.title: David's Great Mistake
stories.hook: Even King David, who loved God, did something very wrong. Then he tried to hide it. But no one can hide from God.
story: David was a great king who loved God very much. The Bible tells us about the good things David did, but it also tells us the truth about his mistakes.
One day, David made some very bad and very selfish choices. He did something that was wrong, and he hurt someone in the process.
After David did the wrong thing, he tried to keep it a secret. He hoped and hoped that no one would ever find out.
On the outside, it looked like nobody knew what David had done. Life just went on as usual.
But there was Someone who saw everything. God knew exactly what David had done, even the parts that no person had seen.
But God loved David far too much to just leave him stuck in his sin. So God sent a brave helper, a prophet named Nathan, to talk to David.
Nathan did not yell at David. Instead, he told the king a story. "There was a rich man who had everything," said Nathan, "but he stole a poor man's one little lamb."
When David heard the story, he got very angry. "That is so unfair!" he said. "That rich man should be punished!"
Then Nathan looked David right in the eye and said, "You are that man."
Suddenly, David understood. The story was about him! And David did not make any excuses.
"I have sinned against the Lord," he said. David was truly, deeply sorry for what he had done.
And God, who is full of mercy, forgave him. There were still some sad things that happened because of David's choices, but David was forgiven.
We cannot hide our wrong things from God, and we do not need to. When we tell God we are sorry, he always forgives us, just like he forgave David.

devotional.bigIdea: Even David did something wrong and tried to hide it. But God sees everything. When David said he was sorry, God forgave him. God forgives us too when we tell him the truth.
questions: Why do we sometimes try to hide it when we do something wrong?
David said, I am sorry, and God forgave him. How does it feel to be forgiven?
Is there something you can tell God you are sorry about, knowing he will forgive you?

connections.title: God Looks at the Heart
connections.why: God chose David for his heart, and even when that heart went badly wrong, God did not give up on him.

connections.title: The Lord Is My Shepherd
connections.why: After his sin, David poured out his sorrow in songs of repentance, some of the most honest prayers in the whole Bible.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Like the lost son who finally came home, David stopped hiding, admitted his sin, and found a Father ready to forgive.

quiz.question: What did David try to do after he did wrong?
options: Hide it
Tell everyone
Run away

quiz.question: Who saw everything David did?
options: God
No one
A lion

quiz.question: What happened when David said he was sorry?
options: God forgave him
Nothing
He had to leave

stories.title: The Lord Is My Shepherd
stories.hook: The most famous song ever written. A king who used to be a shepherd wrote it, and he never forgot what having a shepherd felt like.
story: David grew up looking after sheep on the hills near Bethlehem. He knew exactly what sheep were like. They needed someone to lead them to water because they would not drink from fast rushing streams, only still quiet ones. They needed someone to walk ahead of them into dark valleys so they would not be too scared to go. They needed someone to fight off lions and wolves.
When David became king he wrote a song about God being his shepherd. It has been the most loved poem in the world for three thousand years.
The Lord is my shepherd. I have everything I need. He lets me lie down in soft green fields. He leads me beside quiet streams. He gives my soul a rest.

He leads me on good paths. Even if I walk through the darkest scariest valley, I will not be afraid. Because He is right there with me. His shepherd's stick to guide me and His staff to protect me make me feel safe.
He prepares a meal for me right in front of my enemies. He pours oil on my head as a sign of honour. My cup is so full it spills over.
Surely goodness and love will follow me every day of my life. And I will live in the house of God forever.

David wrote this from his own experience. He had been in dark valleys, hiding from enemies who wanted to kill him, unsure what was coming next. And he had found that God was there. Not watching from far away. Right there with him.

The shepherd David wrote about is the same God who sent Jesus. Jesus said, I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd gives his life for the sheep. The song David wrote was pointing to someone even greater.

devotional.bigIdea: The Lord is your shepherd, not just David's. He leads you, stays with you, fights for you, and is taking you somewhere good.
questions: David says he will not be afraid even in the darkest valley because God is with him. Is there something that scares you? What does it mean that God is with you in that scary place?
David says his cup is so full it spills over. What are some things God has given you that are so good they overflow?
Sheep follow their shepherd because they know his voice. How do you think we get to know God's voice better?

connections.title: One Stone
connections.why: David walked through the valley of the shadow of death with Goliath. He had practised this trust on real hills with real danger.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: Jesus said I am the good shepherd. David's song was describing someone, and Jesus said that someone is Me.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: The father ran to his son just like a shepherd goes looking for the one lost sheep.

quiz.question: Why does a shepherd lead sheep to still quiet water instead of fast rushing streams?
options: Because still water is cleaner
Because sheep will not drink from fast rushing water, only from quiet streams
Because the shepherd gets thirsty there too
Because fast water is too far away
quiz.explanation: Sheep are easily scared and will not drink from rushing water. The shepherd knows this and finds the quiet streams. God meets us where we actually are.

quiz.question: What does David mean when he says his cup is so full it spills over?
options: He is complaining about having too much to carry
He knocked over a drink by accident
He is saying God has given him more good things than he can even hold
He is describing rain falling on dry land
quiz.explanation: David is saying God's goodness to him is so great it overflows. He has more than enough.

quiz.question: Who did Jesus say He was that connects to this psalm?
options: The King of Israel
The Light of the World
The Gate to heaven
The Good Shepherd
quiz.explanation: Jesus said I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd gives his life for the sheep. David's song was pointing to Jesus all along.

stories.title: The Wisest Request
stories.hook: God told Solomon he could ask for anything he wanted. Solomon asked for wisdom. God was so pleased He gave Solomon wisdom and everything else too.
story: After David died his son Solomon became king of Israel. Solomon was young and the job was very big. He loved God and tried to do what was right just like his father David.
One night God appeared to Solomon in a dream. God said, "Ask for whatever you want me to give you." What would you ask for if God said that?

Solomon did not ask for money. He did not ask for his enemies to be defeated. He did not ask for a long life. He said, "Lord, you have made me king but I am young and I do not know how to do this properly. Your people are so many I cannot count them. Please give me a wise heart so I can lead them well and know the difference between right and wrong."

God was very pleased. He said, "Because you asked for wisdom to help other people instead of asking for things for yourself, I will give you exactly what you asked for. I will give you a wise and understanding heart like no one has ever had before. And because you did not ask for riches or long life, I will give you those things too."
Soon Solomon's wisdom was put to the test. Two women came to him with a terrible argument. Each one said the same baby was hers. One baby had died in the night and both claimed the living one.

Solomon called for a sword. He said, "Cut the baby in two and give half to each woman." One woman said, "Yes, do it." The other woman cried out, "No! Give the baby to her, just do not kill him!" Solomon said, "Give the baby to the second woman. She is the real mother." The real mother would rather give up her baby than see it hurt.
Word spread across Israel that their king had been given extraordinary wisdom by God. And it all started because Solomon asked for the one thing that would help him take care of other people.

devotional.bigIdea: Solomon could have asked for anything. He asked for what would help him serve others well. God loves when we think about other people more than ourselves.
questions: If God said to you tonight ask for whatever you want, what would you ask for? What does that tell you about what you care about most?
Solomon said he did not know how to do his job and needed help. Do you find it easy or hard to say you need help?
God gave Solomon wisdom and then also riches and long life even though Solomon did not ask for those. Have you ever got more than you asked for?

connections.title: Speak, Lord
connections.why: Solomon loved God and walked in His ways and God appeared and spoke, just as He had spoken to Samuel.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus said seek first the kingdom of God and all these things will be added to you. Solomon did exactly this.

connections.title: The Lord Is My Shepherd
connections.why: Solomon wrote that the fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom, the same trust his father David showed in Psalm 23.

quiz.question: What did Solomon ask God for when God said he could have anything?
options: A wise heart to lead God's people well
Lots of gold and a big palace
Victory over all his enemies
A very long life
quiz.explanation: Solomon asked for wisdom to lead God's people and know right from wrong. He thought about other people instead of himself.

quiz.question: Why was God so pleased with Solomon's request?
options: Because it showed Solomon was the cleverest person alive
Because Solomon asked for something to help others rather than just for himself
Because Solomon had prayed in exactly the right way
Because God had been hoping someone would ask for wisdom
quiz.explanation: God was pleased because Solomon did not ask for riches or long life but for the ability to serve God's people well.

quiz.question: How did Solomon work out which woman was the real mother of the baby?
options: He prayed and God told him in a dream
He questioned them for three days until one confessed
He said cut the baby in two, knowing the real mother would rather give the baby away than see it hurt
He asked the women's neighbours who had seen the babies before
quiz.explanation: When Solomon said cut the baby, the real mother cried out and said give him to her instead. Love revealed the truth.

stories.title: Solomon Builds the Temple
stories.hook: King David dreamed of building a beautiful house for God. His son Solomon built it: the most amazing temple anyone had ever seen.
story: King David had always dreamed of building a beautiful house for God, a special place where everyone could come to worship him.
But God told David that his son would be the one to build it. So when David's son Solomon became king, he set out to build a wonderful temple for God.
It was a huge and important job! Solomon gathered the finest cedar wood from far away and the very best stone from the quarries.
Thousands and thousands of workers helped to build it. And it took seven whole years to finish.
Here is something amazing about the temple. All the stones were cut and shaped far away, before they were brought to the building site.
So the whole temple was built without the sound of a single hammer! It rose up quietly and peacefully.
Inside, the temple was beautiful. The walls were carved with flowers and palm trees, and they were covered all over with shining gold.
In the very center was the most special room of all. There, they carefully placed the ark of the covenant. At last, it had a forever home.
When everything was finished, a thick cloud filled the whole temple. It was the glory of God! God himself had come to fill his house.
Then Solomon stood up and prayed in front of all the people. He spread out his hands toward heaven.
"God," he prayed, "you are so big that even all of heaven cannot hold you. How wonderful that you would come and meet with us here!"
Solomon knew that no building, no matter how grand and golden, could ever hold the great big God who made everything.
And here is the very best part. Today, God comes to live right inside the hearts of people who love him. We get to be God's home!

devotional.bigIdea: Solomon built God a beautiful temple, but he knew nothing could hold our great big God. The wonderful news is that God now lives in the hearts of people who love him.
questions: The workers gave their very best to build God's temple. How can you give God your best?
God is bigger than all of heaven! What is something that helps you remember how great God is?
God wants to live in our hearts. How does it feel that God wants to be close to you?

connections.title: The Wisest Request
connections.why: The same Solomon who asked God for wisdom now uses it to build the most beautiful house of worship the world had ever seen.

connections.title: Dancing Before the Lord
connections.why: David brought the ark to Jerusalem in a tent, and now his son builds it a permanent home in the Temple's Most Holy Place.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: God's glory once filled the Temple, and at Pentecost his Spirit came to fill his people, who are now God's living temple.

quiz.question: Who built the great Temple?
options: Solomon
Noah
Pharaoh

quiz.question: What was special about how it was built?
options: No hammer was heard at the site
It was built in one day
It was made of sand

quiz.question: Where does God live today?
options: In the hearts of people who love him
Only in buildings
On the moon

stories.title: Fire from Heaven
stories.hook: One prophet stood against 850 false prophets on a mountain. He poured water all over his altar first. Then fire fell from the sky.
story: Israel had a very bad king named Ahab who worshipped a fake god called Baal. The prophet Elijah told Ahab there would be no rain until Elijah said so. Then God told Elijah to hide. For three and a half years there was no rain and everything dried up.

God fed Elijah by sending ravens that brought him bread and meat by a stream. When the stream dried up God sent Elijah to a widow whose jar of flour and bottle of oil never ran out the whole time the drought lasted.

After three and a half years God told Elijah it was time. He set up a contest on Mount Carmel. The 450 prophets of Baal on one side and Elijah all by himself on the other. Each side would prepare an altar and call on their god. Whoever answered with fire was the real God.

The false prophets went first. They shouted and danced and cried out to Baal from morning until afternoon. Nothing happened. Elijah called out, "Shout louder! Maybe your god is asleep!" They shouted louder but still nothing happened.

Then it was Elijah's turn. He built his altar and put the offering on it. Then he did something surprising. He told the people to pour four large jugs of water over the altar. Then four more. Then four more. Twelve jugs of water soaking everything.

Then Elijah prayed one simple prayer. "Lord God, let everyone know today that you are the real God in Israel." Fire fell from heaven. It burned up the offering, the wood, the stones, and even licked up all the water in the trench. Every drop.

The people fell on their faces and shouted, "The Lord, He is God!" Elijah told Ahab rain was coming. He prayed seven times on the hilltop. On the seventh time his servant saw a tiny cloud the size of a man's hand. Before long the sky went dark and the rain poured down.

devotional.bigIdea: Elijah poured water on his altar to make it harder for God to light it, because he knew nothing was too hard for God. He wanted everyone to see clearly who had done it.
questions: Elijah poured twelve jugs of water over his altar before asking God for fire. Why do you think he did that? What was he trying to show?
The false prophets shouted louder and louder. Elijah prayed one quiet sentence. What is the difference between those two ways of talking to God?
The rain started from a cloud the size of a man's hand. Have you ever seen something tiny that became something enormous?

connections.title: Three Hundred Men and Torches
connections.why: Elijah prayed I have done all this at your command, the same posture as Gideon. Both acted under God's direction and gave Him the credit.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: James says Elijah was a normal person just like us who prayed and the rain stopped, then prayed and it came back.

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: When the fire fell the people cried the Lord is God, just as Israel shouted after the Red Sea.

quiz.question: How many prophets of Baal were on Mount Carmel against Elijah?
options: Ten
A hundred
A thousand
Four hundred and fifty
quiz.explanation: There were 450 prophets of Baal against one prophet of God. Elijah stood alone and God showed up for him.

quiz.question: What did Elijah do to make his contest harder before asking God for fire?
options: He poured twelve jugs of water over the altar so everyone would know only God could light it
He built the smallest altar he could find
He blindfolded himself
He moved the altar to the top of the mountain
quiz.explanation: Elijah poured twelve jugs of water over everything. He made it obviously impossible so nobody could miss who did it.

quiz.question: What did the people shout when they saw fire fall from heaven?
options: Long live King Ahab!
The Lord He is God!
Elijah is the greatest prophet!
We are sorry for worshipping Baal!
quiz.explanation: The people fell on their faces and shouted the Lord He is God. The contest was over.

stories.title: Naboth's Vineyard
stories.hook: A king already had everything, but he wanted his neighbor's little garden too. When he could not have it, a very unfair thing happened. But God was watching.
story: King Ahab had a big palace and lots of riches. He had almost everything a person could want.
But there was one little thing that Ahab did not have. Right next to his palace was a vineyard, like a garden, and it belonged to his neighbor, a man named Naboth.
"Naboth, sell me your vineyard," said King Ahab. But Naboth said, "I am sorry, but I cannot. This land belongs to my family. God gave it to us long ago."
When Ahab heard this, he was so upset that he went to his room, lay down on his bed, turned his face to the wall, and would not even eat his dinner.
He had almost everything in the world, but he pouted and sulked about the one little thing he could not have.
Now Ahab's wife was a wicked queen named Jezebel. "Are you not the king?" she said. "Get up and eat. I will get you that vineyard myself."
And then Jezebel did a truly wicked thing. She told terrible lies about Naboth.
Because of those lies, poor Naboth was treated very unfairly, and he lost his life. It was a terrible thing to do.
Then Jezebel told Ahab, "Now the vineyard is yours." So Ahab went down to take it, as if nothing was wrong at all.
But Someone had seen the whole thing. God saw everything that had happened to Naboth.
So God sent the prophet Elijah to King Ahab. "You have done a very wicked thing," Elijah told him. "And God saw it all."
Naboth had no one powerful to protect him. But he was never truly alone, because God saw, and God cared about him.
Our God always sees when people are treated unfairly, and he cares about everyone, especially those who need help.

devotional.bigIdea: Ahab had so much but still wanted more. Naboth was treated unfairly, but God saw everything and cared. God always sees when things are unfair, and he cares about people who need help.
questions: Ahab had so much but was sad about one thing. Why is it good to be thankful for what we have?
God saw what happened to Naboth. How does it feel to know God sees when things are unfair?
God cares about people who are treated unfairly. How can you be kind and fair to others?

connections.title: Fire from Heaven
connections.why: It is the prophet Elijah whom God sends to stand up to wicked King Ahab and Queen Jezebel after they wrong Naboth.

connections.title: The Man Who Lost Everything
connections.why: Like Job, Naboth is a good man treated unjustly, and the story reminds us that God sees and cares when people suffer wrongly.

connections.title: The Man Who Walked Away
connections.why: King Ahab already had everything, yet he could not be happy without more, showing how wanting more and more can poison a heart.

quiz.question: What did King Ahab want from his neighbor?
options: His vineyard
His crown
His horse

quiz.question: Why would Naboth not sell it?
