options: It was his family's land from God
It was haunted
It was too small

quiz.question: What did God do about the unfair thing?
options: He saw it and sent Elijah to speak up
He did nothing
He gave Ahab more land

stories.title: Naaman and the Servant Girl
stories.hook: Naaman was a strong, important general, but he was very sick. A kind servant girl knew just where he could find help.
story: Naaman was the leader of a great big army. He was a strong and important man, and everyone respected him.
But Naaman had a problem that he could not fix. He had a bad skin sickness called leprosy, and all his money and power could not make it go away.
Now, a young servant girl from Israel lived in Naaman's house. She had been taken far from her home, but she was still kind.
"I wish my master could see the prophet who lives in Israel," she said. "That prophet could make him well again!"
So Naaman traveled all the way to Israel to find the prophet Elisha. He brought horses and chariots and lots of treasure.
But when he got there, Elisha did not even come outside. He just sent a messenger to the door with a simple message.
"Go and wash in the Jordan River seven times," the message said, "and you will be all better."
Naaman was very angry! "I thought the prophet would come out and do something big and fancy!" he said. "And the Jordan is just a muddy little river!" And he stomped away.
But Naaman's servants spoke to him gently. "Master," they said, "if the prophet had asked you to do something hard, you would have done it. So why not try this simple thing?"
So Naaman swallowed his pride and went down to the river. He dipped under the water one time, two times, all the way to seven times, just as Elisha had said.
And when he came up the seventh time, his skin was completely healed! It was fresh and new, like the skin of a little child.
Naaman was amazed and so thankful. "Now I know," he said, "that the God of Israel is the one true God!"
And it all began because one young servant girl was brave and kind enough to speak up. God can use anyone who trusts him, even you!

devotional.bigIdea: Naaman almost missed being healed because the cure seemed too simple. God used a little servant girl to help him. God can use anyone, even when they feel small, and he just wants us to trust him.
questions: Naaman wanted something fancy, but God's way was simple. Why is it good to trust what God asks, even when it seems simple?
A young servant girl helped a great general. How might God use you to help others?
Naaman had to stop being proud. What is something it is good to ask for help with?

connections.title: The Bronze Snake
connections.why: Like the people who only had to look at the snake, Naaman only has to do something simple, wash in the river, and trust God to heal him.

connections.title: God Looks at the Heart
connections.why: God uses a young, captured servant girl that the world would overlook to point a great general to healing.

connections.title: Jesus Heals the Ten Lepers
connections.why: Naaman is healed of the same disease Jesus would later heal, and like the one grateful leper, Naaman comes back to give thanks.

quiz.question: What was wrong with Naaman?
options: He had a skin sickness
He was lost
He was poor

quiz.question: Who told him where to find help?
options: A young servant girl
A king
A soldier

quiz.question: What did Naaman have to do to be healed?
options: Wash in the river seven times
Pay gold
Fight a battle

stories.title: The Floating Axe Head
stories.hook: A man was chopping wood when the head of his axe flew off and sank into the river. But nothing is too small to bring to God.
story: A group of God's helpers lived and worked together with the prophet Elisha. But their home had gotten too small for all of them.
"Let us go down to the Jordan River," they said, "and each chop down a tree, so we can build ourselves a bigger place to live."
Elisha said yes, and he went along with them. When they got to the river, everyone got busy chopping down trees.
But while one man was chopping really hard, the heavy iron head flew right off the top of his axe!
It went sailing through the air and splashed into the deep river, sinking down, down, down, out of sight.
"Oh no!" the man cried out. "That axe was not even mine. I borrowed it from someone!"
Back in those days, iron cost a lot of money, and the poor man knew he could never buy a new one to give back.
"Where did it fall in?" Elisha asked him. The man pointed to the exact spot in the water where the axe head had disappeared.
Then Elisha did something surprising. He cut a stick and threw it into the river, right where the axe head had sunk.
And the heavy iron axe head floated right up to the top of the water, as light as a little leaf!
"Reach out and grab it," said Elisha. The man reached down and took it, hardly able to believe his own eyes.
It was such a small thing, just one borrowed tool. But God still cared about it.
The poor man had been so worried about that borrowed axe. And God took care of the whole problem as easily as anything.
And that shows us something wonderful. God cares about the little things in our lives too. Nothing is ever too small to bring to God.

devotional.bigIdea: A lost axe head seems too small to bother God about. But God cared! God cares about every part of our lives, even the little worries.
questions: The man was sad about losing the borrowed axe. What little worries can you tell God about?
God cared about one small tool. How does it feel that God cares about little things for you?
What was the most surprising part of this story?

connections.title: Naaman and the Servant Girl
connections.why: The same prophet Elisha who pointed a great general to healing also cares about one worker's lost, borrowed tool.

connections.title: The Widow's Oil
connections.why: Through Elisha, God again steps into an ordinary person's everyday worry and helps in a way no one expected.

connections.title: The Shunammite Woman's Faith
connections.why: Another story from Elisha's life where God shows he cares about the details of regular people's lives.

quiz.question: What sank into the river?
options: The man's iron axe head
A fish
A boat

quiz.question: Why was the man so worried?
options: The axe was borrowed
He was hungry
He was lost

quiz.question: What did the axe head do when Elisha threw in a stick?
options: It floated to the top
It melted
It disappeared

stories.title: The Widow's Oil
stories.hook: A poor mom had only one small jar of oil left. But when she trusted God, that tiny bit became more than enough.
story: One day, a poor woman came to the prophet Elisha, crying. She was very worried and afraid.
Her husband had died, and now she owed money that she could not pay back.
"The man we owe is coming to take my two sons away to work for him!" she said. She was about to lose her boys, and she did not know what to do.
"How can I help you?" asked Elisha kindly. "Tell me, what do you have in your house?"
The woman shook her head sadly. "I have nothing at all," she said, "except one little jar of oil." It was the very last thing she owned.
But that little jar was exactly what God would use! "Go," said Elisha, "and borrow lots of empty jars from all your neighbors. Get as many as you possibly can!"
So the woman and her two sons hurried around and gathered empty jars from everyone, until they had a great big pile of them.
Then they went inside, shut the door, and the woman began to pour her one little jar of oil into the empty jars.
And the oil just kept pouring and pouring! She filled up one jar, and there was still more. She filled another, and another, and the oil did not run out!
"Bring me another jar!" she kept saying to her sons. They ran back and forth until at last every single jar was full to the top.
Only when there were no more empty jars did the oil finally stop flowing.
The woman sold all that oil, paid back the money she owed, and still had plenty left over for her family. Her boys were safe!
She had given God only the tiniest little bit, but God turned it into more than enough. God loves to do big things with the little we give him.

devotional.bigIdea: The widow had just one little jar of oil, but God made it more than enough. God loves to take the little we have and do big things with it.
questions: The woman only had a little, but God used it. What little thing could you give to God?
She had to keep pouring and trusting. When is it hard to keep trusting God?
God gave her plenty. How has God taken care of your family?

connections.title: Five Loaves and Two Fish
connections.why: Just as Jesus multiplied a boy's small lunch, God multiplies a widow's last little jar of oil until it overflows.

connections.title: Fire from Heaven
connections.why: Like the widow whose flour and oil never ran out for Elijah, this widow learns that God's supply does not run dry.

connections.title: The Shunammite Woman's Faith
connections.why: Both stories show God working through Elisha to rescue women in desperate need.

quiz.question: What did the woman have left in her house?
options: One little jar of oil
A bag of gold
A horse

quiz.question: What did Elisha tell her to gather?
options: Lots of empty jars
Firewood
Water

quiz.question: What happened to the oil?
options: It kept pouring until all the jars were full
It spilled
It dried up right away

stories.title: The Shunammite Woman's Faith
stories.hook: A kind woman made a special room for God's prophet. So God gave her a wonderful gift: a baby boy.
story: In a town called Shunem lived a kind and generous woman. Whenever the prophet Elisha came through her town, she invited him in for a good meal.
One day she said to her husband, "Let us build a little room on our roof for this man of God, with a bed and a table, so he always has a place to rest."
She was so kind, and she never asked for a single thing in return.
Elisha wanted to do something kind for her too. He found out that she had always wished for a child of her own.
So Elisha told her, "About this time next year, you will be holding a baby boy in your arms." And just as he said, the woman had a son!
The little boy grew bigger, and he was the joy of his mother's heart.
But one day, the boy got very, very sick. His mother held him on her lap, but the little boy died.
The woman was so sad. But she did not give up, because she trusted God. She laid her son on Elisha's bed and hurried off as fast as she could to find the prophet.
Elisha came back to the house with her. He went into the room where the boy was lying, and he shut the door, and he prayed to God.
Then he stretched himself out over the little boy, and slowly, the child's body grew warm again.
And then the little boy sneezed and opened his eyes! He was alive again!
"Come and take your son," Elisha said. The happy mother scooped up her boy and held him close, and she thanked God with her whole heart.
This woman had been kind and full of faith. And God showed her, long before Jesus ever came, that he is even stronger than death itself.

devotional.bigIdea: The kind woman gave to others without expecting anything back, and she trusted God even when things were very sad. God saw her faith and showed he is stronger than death.
questions: The woman was kind to Elisha. How can you be kind to others?
When her son got sick, she ran to God for help. Who do you go to when you are sad or scared?
God brought the boy back to life. How does it feel to know God is stronger than anything?

connections.title: The Widow's Oil
connections.why: Another woman whose life God touches through Elisha, this time rewarding her kindness and answering her deepest longing.

connections.title: Jairus's Daughter
connections.why: Like Jesus raising Jairus's little girl, Elisha prays and God brings a beloved child back to life.

connections.title: Come Out
connections.why: Long before Jesus called Lazarus from the tomb, God showed through Elisha that he has power even over death.

quiz.question: How was the woman kind to Elisha?
options: She made him a room to stay in
She gave him a horse
She paid him money

quiz.question: What special gift did God give her?
options: A baby boy
A big house
A crown

quiz.question: What happened after Elisha prayed for her sick son?
options: The boy came back to life
Nothing
He fell asleep

stories.title: The Man Who Lost Everything
stories.hook: Job was the best man in the whole world and he lost absolutely everything. And God never told him why.
story: There was a man named Job who loved God more than anyone else alive. He was kind and fair and always tried to do what was right. He had a big family, lots of animals, and a good life.

Then in one terrible day Job lost almost everything. Bad news came one after another. His animals were taken. His servants were killed. And then a great wind knocked down the house where all his children were having a meal. All ten of his children were gone.

Job was heartbroken. He tore his clothes and sat on the ground. But he said something amazing. He said, "I came into the world with nothing and I will leave with nothing. God gave me everything and God has taken it away. May the name of God be praised." Job did not blame God.
Then Job got very sick. His whole body was covered in painful sores. His wife said, "Just give up on God." Job said, "Should we only accept good things from God and never hard things?"

Three friends came to sit with Job. They sat in silence for seven days, which was kind. But then they started saying that all these terrible things must have happened because Job had done something wrong. They were wrong about that, and Job knew it.
Job was very honest with God. He cried out and said he did not understand. He asked God why this was happening. He did not pretend to be fine when he was not.

Then God spoke from inside a great storm. He did not explain why everything had happened. Instead He asked Job question after question about how the world was made and how it works. Were you there when I laid the foundations of the earth? Job realised how much bigger God was than anything Job could understand.

Job said, "I had only heard about you before. But now I have really met you." God gave back everything Job had lost and more. But the most important thing was not the stuff. It was that Job had met God in the storm.

devotional.bigIdea: Job never got an explanation for what happened to him. He got something better. He got to really meet God. Sometimes God does not answer our questions. He shows up instead.
questions: Job's friends said he must have done something wrong to have so many bad things happen. Have you ever thought that when something goes wrong it must be someone's fault?
Job was very honest with God about how sad and confused he was. Do you think you can tell God when you are sad or angry or confused?
Job said I had only heard about you before but now I have really met you. What is the difference between knowing about God and really knowing God?

connections.title: The Lord Is My Shepherd
connections.why: Job said I know my rescuer lives. Even in the worst suffering he held on to the same certainty David sang about.

connections.title: Come Out
connections.why: Jesus said Lazarus's illness was for God's glory even though it was not explained ahead of time.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus said blessed are those who mourn for they will be comforted. Job is the oldest picture of exactly that.

quiz.question: What did Job say after losing everything in one terrible day?
options: I will never trust God again after this
This must be punishment for something I did wrong
God gave me everything and God has taken it away. May His name be praised
I cannot go on living after this
quiz.explanation: Job was heartbroken but he did not blame God. He said the name of God be praised even in his worst moment.

quiz.question: What was wrong with what Job's three friends told him?
options: They said God did not exist
They told Job to stop praying
They said Job should run away
They said all the bad things must have happened because Job had done something wrong, but that was not true
quiz.explanation: Job's friends insisted the suffering must be his fault. God said later they were wrong. Job had done nothing to deserve what happened.

quiz.question: What did God give Job instead of an explanation?
options: Questions about how the world was made that showed Job how big and amazing God is
A list of reasons each thing had happened
A vision of heaven
An angel who stayed with Job
quiz.explanation: God did not explain. He showed Job His own greatness and power. Job said now I have really met you. That was better than an answer.

stories.title: The Servant Who Suffers
stories.hook: Seven hundred years before Jesus was born, a man named Isaiah wrote down exactly what was going to happen to Him. Every detail came true.
story: Isaiah was a prophet who lived about seven hundred years before Jesus was born. God showed Isaiah things that had not happened yet and Isaiah wrote them down.
One day God showed Isaiah a picture of someone very special. This person was going to come one day and do something no one had ever done before.

Isaiah wrote that this special servant would not look like a great powerful king. He would grow up like a small plant in dry ground. He would not be tall or handsome or impressive. People would not be drawn to him because of how he looked.
Isaiah wrote that people would look down on this servant. He would know what it felt like to be sad. He would know what it felt like to suffer.

But here is the most important part. Isaiah wrote that this servant would be hurt for the wrong things that other people had done. He would be wounded for our mistakes. He would be crushed for the wrong choices we make. And because of what he suffered, we could be healed and made right with God.
Isaiah wrote that this servant would be like a lamb being led to the place where it would die. He would not complain or fight back. He would be quiet.
Isaiah wrote that this servant would die and be buried. But then he would see the reward of his suffering. He would make many people right with God by taking on what they had done wrong.

Isaiah wrote all this seven hundred years before Jesus was born. When Jesus came and was arrested and hurt and killed and rose again, everyone who knew Isaiah's writing recognised it. Isaiah had been describing Jesus the whole time. The cross was always the plan.

devotional.bigIdea: Isaiah described the cross seven hundred years before it happened. Jesus coming to take our place was not a last-minute idea. It was planned before any of us were born.
questions: Isaiah wrote about Jesus hundreds of years before Jesus was born. Does that surprise you? What does it tell you about how much God planned ahead?
Isaiah said the servant was hurt for things that other people did wrong. What does it mean to you that Jesus took the punishment for your mistakes?
Isaiah said after all his suffering the servant would see the reward and be satisfied. What do you think the reward was?

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Every detail Isaiah wrote landed exactly at the cross. He was describing what he could not yet see.

connections.title: A Promise as Big as the Stars
connections.why: God told Abraham all nations would be blessed through him. Isaiah shows how, through a servant who would carry what we deserved.

connections.title: The Ark and the Rainbow
connections.why: We all like sheep have gone wrong. Isaiah pointed to the same problem and its solution.

quiz.question: How long before Jesus was born did Isaiah write about the suffering servant?
options: Ten years
Seven hundred years
Fifty years
Two hundred years
quiz.explanation: Isaiah lived about seven hundred years before Jesus. He described the cross in detail without ever seeing it happen.

quiz.question: Why did the servant suffer according to Isaiah?
options: Because he had done many wrong things himself
Because his enemies were stronger
Because he was wounded for the wrong things we had done so we could be healed
Because God was punishing Israel
quiz.explanation: Isaiah said he was pierced for our wrongdoings and crushed for our mistakes. He suffered in our place so we could be made right.

quiz.question: What did Isaiah compare the servant to when he was being led to his death?
options: A soldier who would not give up
A lion facing its hunters
A king going into battle
A lamb being quietly led to the place where it would die
quiz.explanation: Isaiah said the servant was like a lamb going quietly to the slaughter. Jesus did not fight back when He was arrested and taken away.

stories.title: Daniel and the King's Food
stories.hook: Daniel was taken far away to serve a king. On his very first day, he bravely chose to honor God, even with something as simple as his food.
story: A long, long way from home, in a land called Babylon, lived a young man named Daniel and his three good friends.
They had been taken away from their home in Jerusalem to serve the king in his great big palace. It was a strange new place, far from everything they knew.
The king said that these young men should eat the very best food from his own royal table every day.
But Daniel knew that some of that food went against God's ways. So Daniel decided, deep in his heart, that he would honor God instead.
It took real courage. Daniel was far from home, and everyone around him was doing things differently. It would have been so easy to just go along with the crowd.
Instead, Daniel politely asked the man in charge, "Please, may my friends and I eat only vegetables and drink only water?"
The man was worried. "But the king chose your food himself," he said. "If you look thin and weak, the king will be angry with me!"
So Daniel had a good idea. "Just test us for ten days," he said. "Give us only vegetables and water. Then see how we look compared to everyone else."
The man agreed to try it for ten days.
When the ten days were over, Daniel and his three friends looked healthier and stronger than all the other young men who ate the king's rich food!
So they were allowed to keep eating their simple food. And God was pleased with them.
God gave Daniel and his friends great wisdom, more than anyone else in the whole kingdom.
Daniel honored God even in a small, everyday choice about his dinner. And the little choices we make to follow God are very important too.

devotional.bigIdea: Daniel was far from home, but he still chose to honor God, even in a small choice about food. God was pleased with him. Our small choices to follow God really matter.
questions: Daniel decided to do the right thing ahead of time. Why is that a good idea?
It is hard to be different from everyone else. When is it hard for you to do the right thing?
What is one small way you can honor God today?

connections.title: The Furnace
connections.why: Daniel's three friends here are the very same men who later refuse to bow to the golden statue and are thrown into the fiery furnace.

connections.title: The Lion's Den
connections.why: The young man who quietly chooses to honor God with his food grows into the old man who keeps praying even when it is against the law.

connections.title: The Dreamer's Long Road
connections.why: Like Joseph in Egypt, Daniel is a young man taken far from home who stays faithful to God in a foreign palace, and God lifts him up.

quiz.question: Where was Daniel taken to live?
options: A king's palace far from home
A farm
A boat

quiz.question: What did Daniel ask to eat instead of the king's rich food?
options: Vegetables and water
Candy
Nothing

quiz.question: How did Daniel and his friends look after ten days?
options: Healthier and stronger
Sick
Sleepy

stories.title: The Furnace
stories.hook: The king built a huge gold statue and told everyone to bow down to it. Three friends said no. The king threw them into a fire. Then the king saw four people walking around inside.
story: A powerful king named Nebuchadnezzar built an enormous gold statue and told every single person in his whole empire that when the music played everyone had to bow down and worship it. Anyone who did not bow would be thrown into a blazing hot furnace.
Three Jewish men who worked for the king refused. Their names were Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego. When the music played and everyone else bowed down they stood straight up.

The king was very angry. He called the three men in and gave them one more chance. "When the music plays again bow down. If you do not I will throw you into the furnace. What god could possibly save you from my power?"

The three men gave the king an amazing answer. They said, "Our God is able to save us from the furnace. He will save us. But even if He does not, we want you to know we will still not bow down to your gold statue."
Even if He does not. They were not promising God would definitely save them. They were saying that even if He did not they would still not bow. That is very brave.

The king was so angry he told his men to heat the furnace seven times hotter than usual. It was so hot that the soldiers who threw the three men in were killed by the heat. But Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego fell into the fire.

Then the king jumped up from his seat. He looked into the furnace and said, "Didn't we throw three men in? I see four men walking around in the fire! They are not hurt! And the fourth one looks like a son of the gods!"
He called them out. They walked out of the fire. Their hair was not burned. Their clothes were not burned. They did not even smell like smoke.
The king said, "Blessed be the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego who sent His messenger and rescued His servants because they trusted Him."

devotional.bigIdea: The three friends said even if God does not save us we will not bow. That is the deepest kind of faith. Not faith that always gets a miracle, but faith that holds on whether the miracle comes or not.
questions: The three men said even if God does not save us we will not bow down. Do you think you could be that brave? What would help you?
The king saw a fourth person in the fire with them. Who do you think that was? What does it mean that God was in the fire with them instead of keeping them out of it?
The men were not burned and did not even smell like smoke. Have you ever been protected from something without even realising it was happening?

connections.title: The Lion's Den
connections.why: These three friends refused to bow to the statue just as Daniel refused to stop praying. Both stories are about the same kind of brave faith.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: God promised through Isaiah that when you walk through fire you will not be burned. These three men proved it.

connections.title: Singing at Midnight
connections.why: The men trusted God and defied the king's command. Paul and Silas did the same thing in a Roman prison.

quiz.question: What did Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego refuse to do when the music played?
options: They refused to bow down and worship the king's gold statue
They refused to work for the king any more
They refused to leave the city
They refused to eat the king's food
quiz.explanation: When the music played and everyone else bowed, the three men stood straight up. They would not worship anyone or anything except God.

quiz.question: What did the three men say when the king gave them one more chance?
options: They asked for more time to think
They said God can save us but even if He does not we will still not bow
They said they would bow once and never again
They said nothing and quietly refused
quiz.explanation: They said our God is able to save us. But even if He does not, we will not worship your statue. That is what brave faith looks like.

quiz.question: What did the king see when he looked into the furnace after throwing in three men?
options: Three men on fire screaming
Three men lying still on the floor
Four men walking around in the fire completely unharmed
The three men had disappeared
quiz.explanation: The king had thrown in three men but saw four walking around completely unharmed. The fourth one looked like a son of the gods.

stories.title: The Lion's Den
stories.hook: Some men passed a law making it illegal to pray to God. Daniel prayed anyway. Right in front of his open window.
story: Daniel had been taken from his home in Israel as a young man and brought to Babylon. He worked so hard and was so wise and honest that he rose to one of the highest jobs in the whole empire. Even after a new king named Darius took over Daniel kept his important position.

This made other officials jealous. They looked for something to use against Daniel. They could not find anything. He was not corrupt. He was not lazy. He did everything right. The only thing they could find was his faith.
So they tricked King Darius into signing a law. The law said that for thirty days no one could pray to any god or person except the king. Anyone who did would be thrown to the lions.

Daniel heard about the law. He went home and opened his window toward Jerusalem, the city he missed, and knelt down and prayed three times that day just like he always did. He did not hide it. He did not close the window. He just prayed.

The jealous officials watched and then ran to tell the king. King Darius was very upset. He liked Daniel. He tried all day to find a way around his own law. But a law of the Medes and Persians could not be changed even by the king himself.
That evening Darius said to Daniel, "May your God, whom you serve all the time, rescue you." They put Daniel in the pit with the lions and rolled a stone over the opening.
The king went back to his palace. He could not sleep. He did not eat. He did not listen to music. He just lay awake worrying about Daniel.

At the very first light of dawn the king ran to the lions' den and called out, "Daniel! Was your God able to rescue you?" And a voice came back from inside. "My God sent His angel and shut the lions' mouths. They have not hurt me."
Daniel was lifted out without a single scratch. Darius sent a message to every nation saying the God of Daniel is the living God who rescues and saves and does miraculous signs.

devotional.bigIdea: Daniel prayed the same prayer in the same place at the same time every single day whether it was easy or against the law. That kind of steady faithfulness is very powerful.
questions: Daniel prayed every day at the same time by his open window. Do you have something you do every day to spend time with God?
The jealous officials could not find anything wrong with Daniel except his faith. What do you think it means to live in a way where the only thing people can point at is how much you love God?
Darius said may your God rescue you and then could not sleep all night hoping. Do you think Darius believed in God by the end of the story?

connections.title: The Furnace
connections.why: Daniel was saved because he trusted in his God, the same faith that saved his three friends. Both were in enemy territory and both trusted God completely.

connections.title: Speak, Lord
connections.why: Daniel prayed three times a day his whole life, the same steady posture as Samuel's speak Lord I am listening.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Hebrews says Daniel shut the mouths of lions by faith. The stone rolled away from an even darker place three days after the cross.

quiz.question: Why could the jealous officials not find anything wrong with Daniel?
options: Because he was very good at hiding his mistakes
Because he bribed people to keep quiet
Because he was new to the job
Because he was completely honest and hardworking and they could not find any fault
quiz.explanation: Daniel was not corrupt or lazy. He did everything right. The only vulnerability they could find was his faith.

quiz.question: What did Daniel do when he heard about the new law against praying?
options: He went home, opened his window, and prayed three times just like he always had
He stopped praying for thirty days until the law ended
He prayed secretly with all the windows shut
He went to the king to argue against the law
quiz.explanation: Daniel did not hide and did not stop. He opened his window and prayed exactly as he always did. His faithfulness did not change because of a law.

quiz.question: What did Daniel say to the king when the king called out at dawn?
options: He had been praying all night and was exhausted
God sent His angel and shut the lions' mouths and they had not hurt him
He did not answer because he was too scared to speak
He said he had made friends with the lions
quiz.explanation: Daniel called back that God had sent His angel to shut the lions' mouths. He was completely unharmed.

stories.title: For Such a Time as This
stories.hook: A young Jewish girl became queen of the most powerful country in the world. Then she found out her whole people were in danger. Only she could help.
story: A young Jewish woman named Esther lived in the Persian empire with her older cousin Mordecai, who had raised her after her parents died. The king of Persia was looking for a new queen and Esther was chosen. She was now queen of one of the most powerful countries in the world. But she kept it secret that she was Jewish because Mordecai told her to.

A man named Haman had been given the highest position in the empire. Everyone bowed to Haman. Everyone except Mordecai. Mordecai would not bow to any person the way he bowed to God. Haman was furious. He decided not just to hurt Mordecai but to destroy all the Jewish people in the whole empire.
Haman tricked the king into signing a law that said on a certain day all Jewish people everywhere could be killed. The date was set.

Mordecai heard about it and sent Esther a message. "You must go to the king and ask him to stop this." Esther sent a message back. "If I go to the king without being invited I could be killed. Even the queen cannot just walk in. Unless the king holds out his golden sceptre, anyone who comes uninvited faces death."

Mordecai sent back one of the most famous messages ever written. "Do not think you are safe just because you are in the palace. If you stay silent help will come for the Jewish people from somewhere else. But who knows? Maybe this is exactly why you became queen. Maybe you are here for such a time as this."
Esther asked Mordecai to get all the Jewish people to fast and pray for three days. Then she said words that people have loved for thousands of years. "And if I die, I die."
After three days Esther put on her best clothes and walked into the throne room. The king saw her and held out his golden sceptre. She was safe.

Esther had two dinner parties and at the second one she told the king the truth. A man named Haman was planning to kill her people. The king was furious. Haman was punished and his plan was stopped. The Jewish people were saved.
Esther had been exactly the right person in exactly the right place at exactly the right time.

devotional.bigIdea: Esther said if I die, I die, and then she walked in anyway. Courage is not the same as not being scared. It is doing the right thing even when you are very scared.
questions: Mordecai told Esther maybe you are here for such a time as this. Do you think God puts people in special places for special reasons? Can you think of an example?
Esther said if I die I die. That is very brave. Has there ever been a time when you did the right thing even though you were scared?
Esther asked everyone to fast and pray for three days before she went to the king. Why do you think she did that before she acted?

connections.title: Wherever You Go
connections.why: Ruth said wherever you go I will go. Esther said if I die I die. Both women chose bravely for the people they loved.

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: Mary said let it be to me as you say. Esther said if I die I die. Both said yes to something terrifying because they trusted God.

connections.title: The Man Who Hunted Christians
connections.why: Mordecai said help will come from another place if you stay silent. He trusted that God's plan could not be stopped.

quiz.question: What was dangerous about Esther going to see the king?
options: The king did not know who she was
Haman had told the guards not to let anyone in
Anyone who came to the king without being invited could be killed unless he held out his golden sceptre
The king was angry at Esther
quiz.explanation: Even the queen could not just walk in uninvited. Anyone who came without being called faced death unless the king chose to hold out his sceptre.

quiz.question: What did Mordecai say to Esther when she hesitated?
options: He said God will protect you so you have nothing to worry about
He said if you do not go I will go instead
He said the king loves you so you will definitely be safe
He said maybe you became queen exactly for a time like this
quiz.explanation: Mordecai said who knows whether you came to the kingdom for such a time as this. He believed God had placed Esther exactly where she needed to be.

quiz.question: What did Esther say before she went to the king?
options: Gather everyone and fast and pray for me, and if I die I die
I know God will protect me so I am not scared
I will go to the king tomorrow and see what happens
I will only go if you promise everything will be fine
quiz.explanation: Esther asked everyone to fast and pray for three days. Then she said if I die I die. She was brave but also honest about the cost.

stories.title: The Man Who Ran From God
stories.hook: God told a prophet to go east. He got on a ship going west. Then things got very interesting.
story: God told a man named Jonah to go to a big city called Nineveh and tell them to stop doing wrong things. Nineveh was the capital of Israel's worst enemy. Jonah did not want to go. He was afraid they might listen and God might forgive them, and Jonah did not want that.
So Jonah ran the other way. He went to a port town, found a ship going as far from Nineveh as possible, paid for a ticket, went down into the bottom of the ship, and fell asleep.
God sent a huge storm. The ship was tossing and the sailors were terrified. They threw cargo overboard to make the ship lighter. Everyone was praying to their gods. Except Jonah, who was fast asleep.

The captain found Jonah and woke him up. They decided to find out who had caused the trouble. The lot fell on Jonah. Jonah admitted everything. He said, "I am running from the God who made the sea and the land. Throw me overboard and the storm will stop."
The sailors tried very hard to row back to land. They could not. The storm got worse. Finally they threw Jonah into the sea. The storm stopped immediately.

But Jonah did not drown. God sent a huge fish to swallow him. Jonah was inside the fish for three days and three nights. While he was in there in the dark he prayed. He said sorry. He trusted God. He said, "I will do what I promised." After three days the fish spat Jonah out on dry land.

God spoke to Jonah a second time. "Go to Nineveh." This time Jonah went. He walked into the city and told them to stop doing wrong. The whole city listened. From the king to the poorest person everyone stopped and said sorry to God.

And God forgave them. Jonah was furious. He sat outside the city sulking hoping God would destroy it anyway. God asked Jonah, "Is it right for you to be angry?" The book ends with that question hanging in the air. Jonah needed to learn that God's love reaches further than we want it to.

devotional.bigIdea: Jonah ran from God and ended up in a fish. God brought him back and gave him the same job again. God does not give up on the people He has called even when they run.
questions: Jonah did not want God to forgive Nineveh because they were his enemies. Have you ever not wanted God to be kind to someone? What was underneath that feeling?
Jonah prayed his most honest prayer from inside a very smelly fish. Why do you think people sometimes pray most honestly when they are in the worst situations?
God gave Jonah the exact same job a second time after Jonah ran away. What does that tell you about how God treats us when we get things wrong?

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Jonah was angry that God was gracious. The older brother in the prodigal story was angry his father threw a party. Both stories show how hard it is to accept grace for others.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: Jesus said just as Jonah was three days in the fish, the Son of Man would be three days in the earth. Jonah pointed to the resurrection.

connections.title: The Man Who Hunted Christians
connections.why: Both Jonah and Paul were going the wrong way when God stopped them. Both were given a second chance and sent to people they would not have chosen.

quiz.question: Why did Jonah run away from God's instructions to go to Nineveh?
options: He was afraid they would hurt him
He did not want God to forgive Nineveh because they were Israel's enemy
He did not believe he could do the job
He had already planned a trip in the other direction
quiz.explanation: Jonah admits at the end he ran because he knew God would be kind to Nineveh if they listened. He did not want that.

quiz.question: What happened to the storm when the sailors threw Jonah overboard?
options: It gradually calmed down over the next few hours
It got worse before stopping
It stopped immediately as soon as Jonah hit the water
It went on for another day and then stopped by itself
quiz.explanation: The moment Jonah went into the water the sea became calm. The sailors were amazed.

quiz.question: What did Jonah do inside the big fish?
options: He tried to find a way out through the fish's mouth
He went to sleep because there was nothing else to do
He was unconscious for all three days
He prayed honestly to God and said sorry
quiz.explanation: Inside the fish in the dark Jonah prayed. He was honest with God and said he would do what he had promised. Then the fish put him back out.

stories.title: The Wall Goes Up
stories.hook: Jerusalem's walls had been broken down for one hundred and forty years. A man who worked for a king in another country decided to fix them. The wall went up in fifty-two days.
story: A man named Nehemiah worked as a trusted helper to the king of Persia. He was Jewish but had never been to Jerusalem, the city his people called home.

One day visitors came from Jerusalem and Nehemiah asked how the city was doing. The news was bad. The walls of Jerusalem were still broken down from when enemies had destroyed them one hundred and forty years ago. The gates had been burned. The city had no protection.
Nehemiah sat down and cried. Then he prayed for a long time. He told God he was sorry for the ways his people had turned away and he asked God to help him do something.
Four months later the king noticed Nehemiah looked sad. The king asked what was wrong. Nehemiah was nervous but honest. He told the king about Jerusalem and said he wanted to go and rebuild it.
The king said yes. He gave Nehemiah letters for safe travel and instructions for getting wood and supplies. Nehemiah went to Jerusalem.

He did not make any big announcements straight away. He waited three days then went out alone at night on a horse to look at the broken walls in the dark. He looked at everything quietly and made a plan.
Then he called the people together and said, "You can see what a mess everything is. Let us rebuild the wall." The people said, "Yes! Let us start!" And they did.

People who wanted to cause trouble came and mocked them. They said the wall was so weak a fox could knock it down. Nehemiah prayed and kept building. When they threatened to attack Nehemiah gave half the workers weapons and had them keep watch while the others built.
In fifty-two days the wall was finished. The people who had been trying to stop them were amazed. They could see that the work had happened with God's help.

devotional.bigIdea: Nehemiah heard sad news, cried, prayed, and then made a plan and got to work. Real faith is not just asking God to do things. It is asking God for help and then doing the work alongside Him.
questions: Nehemiah prayed for four months before he did anything. Why do you think he waited and prayed that long before acting?
When people mocked the workers and said the wall was too weak Nehemiah prayed and kept building. Have you ever had someone say something discouraging about something you were working on?
The wall went up in fifty-two days after sitting broken for one hundred and forty years. What does that tell you about what can happen when people work together with God?

connections.title: For Such a Time as This
connections.why: Both Esther and Nehemiah were Jewish people in positions of importance in foreign courts who used those positions for their people.

connections.title: The Burning Bush
connections.why: Nehemiah's prayer begins Lord God of heaven, the great and awesome God. Both Moses and Nehemiah were ordinary people given big jobs by the same God.

connections.title: The Walls Come Down
connections.why: Joshua's people knocked walls down by faith in God. Nehemiah's people built walls up by faith in the same God.

quiz.question: What was Nehemiah's job before he went to rebuild Jerusalem?
options: A cup bearer and trusted helper to the king of Persia
A soldier in the Persian army
A merchant who travelled between countries
A scribe who copied documents
quiz.explanation: Nehemiah was cup bearer to the king, one of the most trusted positions possible.

quiz.question: What did Nehemiah do when he first arrived in Jerusalem?
options: He called everyone together immediately and made a big speech
He waited three days then went out alone at night to look at the broken walls and make a plan
He started building straight away
He prayed for another four months before doing anything
quiz.explanation: Nehemiah was patient and careful. He waited three days then went out alone at night so he could look at everything quietly before telling anyone his plan.

quiz.question: How long did it take to rebuild the walls of Jerusalem?
options: Three years even with everyone helping
One year working as fast as they could
Fifty-two days which amazed everyone who saw it
Six months including a summer break
quiz.explanation: The wall was finished in fifty-two days. The enemies who had been trying to stop the work were amazed and could see that God had helped.

stories.title: Impossible News
stories.hook: An angel visited a young woman in a small town with the most surprising news anyone had ever heard.
story: In a small town called Nazareth a young woman named Mary was getting ready to be married to a man named Joseph. She was just an ordinary person in an ordinary place. But God had been watching her.

One day an angel named Gabriel appeared to her right there in the room. Mary was very frightened. Gabriel said, "Do not be afraid, Mary. God is very pleased with you. You are going to have a baby and you are to name Him Jesus. He will be great and will be called the Son of God. His kingdom will never end."

Mary asked one question. She said, "How can this happen? I am not married yet." Gabriel answered, "The Holy Spirit will come on you and the power of God will be with you. Nothing is impossible for God. Look, your relative Elizabeth is also going to have a baby even though everyone said it was too late for her."
Mary said something very brave. She said, "I am God's servant. Let it happen to me just as you said." She said yes before she knew how hard it would be. She said yes because she trusted God.

Mary went to visit Elizabeth. The moment she walked through the door and called out hello the baby inside Elizabeth jumped for joy. Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit and said, "You are the most blessed woman! And blessed is the baby you are carrying!"
Mary sang a song. She sang about how God lifts up the people the world looks down on. She sang about how God keeps every promise He has ever made.

Nine months later a Roman emperor made an announcement that everyone had to travel back to their home towns to be counted. Joseph's family came from Bethlehem so Mary and Joseph made the long trip even though the baby was almost ready to be born.
In Bethlehem there was no room anywhere. That night in a stable where animals slept Mary had her baby. She wrapped Him in strips of cloth and laid Him in the feeding trough that the animals ate from.
The angels had called Him the Son of God. Tonight He was lying in a hay box. That is how God came into the world. Not with power from the top. As a tiny baby at the very bottom.

devotional.bigIdea: Mary said yes to something she did not fully understand. That is what faith looks like. Not knowing everything, just trusting the one who does.
questions: Gabriel told Mary nothing is impossible for God. Is there something that feels impossible in your life or someone you know? What would it mean to believe God about that?
Mary said I am God's servant, let it happen as you said. She said yes before she knew how hard it would be. What does it take to say yes like that?
Jesus was born in a stable with animals not in a palace. Why do you think God chose to come into the world that way?

connections.title: For Such a Time as This
connections.why: Mary said let it be to me as you say, the same brave surrender as Esther's if I die I die. Both women said yes before they knew the cost.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: When Jesus was eight days old a man told Mary that a sword would pierce her own soul. The cost of her yes was coming.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: Gabriel said his kingdom will never end. The resurrection is the moment that promise became impossible to doubt.

quiz.question: What was Mary's one question when the angel told her she was going to have a baby?
options: Why has God chosen someone as ordinary as me?
What will Joseph say when he finds out?
Will this baby really be the king of Israel?
How can this happen since I am not married yet?
quiz.explanation: Mary asked how can this happen since I am not yet married. It was a practical question not an argument. Gabriel answered honestly.

quiz.question: What did Mary say when the angel finished explaining?
options: I am God's servant, let it happen just as you have said
She asked for more time to think
She said she needed to ask Joseph first
She said she was scared and did not know if she could do it
quiz.explanation: Mary said I am God's servant and let it happen as you said. She trusted God completely even before she knew what it would cost.

quiz.question: Where was Jesus born and why was it unusual?
options: In a palace in Jerusalem
In a stable in Bethlehem where animals lived, in a hay-filled feeding trough
In a house in Nazareth where Mary and Joseph lived
In the Temple in Jerusalem
quiz.explanation: There was no room anywhere so Mary and Joseph stayed in a stable. Jesus was laid in the animals' feeding trough. God came into the world not at the top but at the very bottom.

stories.title: The Wise Men Follow the Star
stories.hook: Far away, wise men saw a bright new star in the sky. They knew it meant a special king was born, so they followed it to find baby Jesus.
story: Far away in lands to the east, there lived wise men who studied the stars in the night sky.
One night, they saw a brand new star, brighter and more wonderful than any star they had ever seen before.
The wise men knew that this special star meant something amazing. A great new king had been born to God's people!
So they packed up some precious gifts and set out on a very long journey to find the new king and worship him.
When they came to the city of Jerusalem, they asked everyone, "Where is the baby who has been born to be king? We saw his star, and we have come to worship him."
When King Herod heard this, he was troubled and upset. He was the king, and he did not want anyone taking his place.
Herod found out from the wise teachers that the special king was to be born in a town called Bethlehem.
So the wise men set off again. And the wonderful star went ahead of them, leading the way, until it stopped right over the place where the child was.
The wise men were filled with joy! They went inside and saw the young child Jesus with his mother, Mary.
They knelt down low to the ground and worshiped him.
Then they opened their treasures and gave Jesus their very best gifts: shiny gold, and sweet-smelling frankincense and myrrh.
They had traveled such a very long way, not to see a fancy palace, but to kneel down before one special child and give him their best.
The wise men teach us something wonderful. We can seek Jesus with all our hearts, and we can worship him by giving him our very best too.

devotional.bigIdea: The wise men traveled very far just to find Jesus and worship him with their best gifts. We can seek Jesus and give him our best too.
questions: The wise men traveled far to find Jesus. How can you look for Jesus every day?
They gave Jesus their best gifts. What good gift could you give to God?
How do you think the wise men felt when they finally found Jesus?

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: The baby the wise men travel so far to worship is Jesus, the child the angel promised to Mary.

connections.title: Left Behind in Jerusalem
connections.why: The little child the wise men knelt before grows into the boy who amazes the teachers in the Temple.

connections.title: The King Rides In
connections.why: The wise men were the first to call Jesus a king, and one day he would ride into Jerusalem as the humble King of all.

quiz.question: What did the wise men follow to find Jesus?
options: A bright new star
A big map
A road sign

quiz.question: What gifts did they bring Jesus?
options: Gold, frankincense, and myrrh
Toys and candy
Books

quiz.question: What did the wise men do when they found Jesus?
options: Knelt down and worshiped him
Ran away
Took a nap

stories.title: Left Behind in Jerusalem
stories.hook: Jesus was twelve years old when His parents lost Him for three days. They found Him exactly where He told them He would be.
story: Every year Mary and Joseph travelled to Jerusalem for a big celebration called Passover. They went with a large group of family and friends, a whole caravan of people travelling together.

When Jesus was twelve the family went as usual. After the celebration was over the big group started the journey home. Children ran between the groups of adults. Mary and Joseph each assumed Jesus must be with other relatives somewhere in the crowd.

At the end of the first day of walking they looked for Jesus. He was not there. They went back to all the relatives and friends. Nobody had seen Him. With growing fear Mary and Joseph turned around and went back to Jerusalem.
They searched for three days. Three days of walking through the city asking everyone if they had seen their son. Three very frightening days.

Then they found Him. He was in the Temple, the great house of God in the centre of Jerusalem. He was sitting with the teachers, the most learned men in Israel, listening carefully and asking them questions. Everyone who heard Him was amazed at how wise He was.
Mary went straight to Him. "Child! Why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been so worried! We have been searching everywhere for you!"

Jesus said something that surprised them. "Why were you looking for me? Didn't you know I would be here in my Father's house?" He meant God was His Father. He had always known this about Himself. He was surprised they had not thought to look there first.
Mary and Joseph did not fully understand what He meant. But Mary stored it carefully in her heart, turning it over and over, waiting for the day it would all make sense.

Then Jesus went home with them to Nazareth and was obedient to them. He grew up. He became wiser and taller and the people around Him loved Him more and more. Eighteen quiet years before His big work began.

devotional.bigIdea: Even as a twelve-year-old boy Jesus knew exactly who He was and whose He was. God was His Father. That was the most important thing about Him.
questions: Jesus was twelve years old and already knew that God was His Father and that the Temple was His Father's house. How do you think about who you are and who God is to you?
Mary kept storing things in her heart that she did not fully understand yet. Is there anything about God or the Bible that you do not fully understand but are holding onto?
Jesus went home and obeyed Mary and Joseph for eighteen more years even though He was the Son of God. Why do you think that mattered?

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: Mary stored all these things in her heart, the same thing she did when the shepherds came at His birth. She kept collecting moments she could not yet understand.

connections.title: Speak, Lord
connections.why: Jesus grew in wisdom and in favour with God and people, the same words used about Samuel. Both boys grew up in God's house.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus began His public work at about thirty. These eighteen quiet years were not wasted.

quiz.question: How long did Mary and Joseph search for Jesus before they found Him?
options: One afternoon
One whole day
Three days of searching all through Jerusalem
A whole week
quiz.explanation: Mary and Joseph walked a full day before they realised Jesus was missing then turned back. It was three days of searching before they found Him in the Temple.

quiz.question: What was Jesus doing when Mary and Joseph finally found Him?
options: Playing with other children in the Temple courtyard
Helping the priests prepare for ceremonies
Praying quietly alone in a corner
Sitting with the great teachers listening and asking questions that amazed everyone
quiz.explanation: Jesus was sitting with the greatest teachers in Israel asking and answering questions. Everyone who heard Him was amazed at His understanding.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say when Mary asked why He had stayed behind?
options: He said why were you looking, did you not know I would be in my Father's house?
He said He had got lost in the crowd
He said He had made friends with the teachers and wanted to stay
He said He was sorry for worrying them
quiz.explanation: Jesus was surprised they had not thought to look in the Temple. It was His Father's house. Where else would He be?

stories.title: Jesus Is Baptized
stories.hook: A preacher named John was getting people ready for God. Then Jesus came to the river, and heaven opened up!
story: Out in the wilderness, near the Jordan River, a man named John was preaching to anyone who would listen.
John looked a little wild. He wore rough clothes made of camel hair, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
"Turn back to God!" John told the crowds. "Get your hearts ready, because someone much greater than me is coming soon."
People who were sorry for the wrong things they had done came to John. He baptized them in the river, dipping them under the water, to show that they were starting fresh and clean with God.
Then one day, Jesus himself came to the river. And he asked John to baptize him too.
John was very surprised. "It should be the other way around!" he said. "You should baptize me!"
You see, Jesus had never done anything wrong, so he did not need to be washed clean like the others.
But Jesus said, "Let it be this way. It is the right thing for us to do." Jesus wanted to stand together with all the people he came to save. So John baptized him.
The very moment Jesus came up out of the water, something wonderful happened. The sky opened up above him!
The Spirit of God came down softly, gently, like a dove, and rested on Jesus.
And then God the Father spoke from heaven. "This is my Son, whom I love," he said. "With him I am very pleased."
God said that he loved his Son before Jesus had done any miracles or preached any sermons at all.
And that is exactly how God loves you. Not because of what you do, but simply because you are his child.

devotional.bigIdea: God the Father said he loved Jesus before Jesus had done anything. God loves us that way too, not for what we do, but just because we are his children.
questions: God said he loved his Son right away. How does it feel that God loves you just because you are his?
Jesus was baptized to stand with us. What does that tell you about Jesus?
What do you think it was like to hear God's voice from heaven?

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: The Father calls Jesus his beloved Son, the very child the angel had promised would be called the Son of God.

connections.title: Tested in the Wilderness
connections.why: Right after his baptism, the same Spirit who came down like a dove leads Jesus into the wilderness to be tested.

connections.title: In the Beginning
connections.why: As at creation the Spirit hovered over the waters and God spoke, here the Spirit descends over the water and the Father's voice is heard.

quiz.question: Who baptized people in the river?
options: John
Pharaoh
Goliath

quiz.question: What came down on Jesus like a dove?
options: The Spirit of God
Snow
A leaf

quiz.question: What did God say from heaven?
options: This is my Son, whom I love
Go home
Build a boat

stories.title: Tested in the Wilderness
stories.hook: Right after Jesus was baptized, he went into the desert. He was very hungry when the devil came to tempt him to do wrong.
story: Right after Jesus was baptized, the Spirit of God led him out into the lonely desert.
Jesus stayed there for forty days and forty nights, and he did not eat any food the whole time. He was praying and getting ready for the important work ahead.
By the end of the forty days, Jesus was very, very hungry.
That is when the devil came to tempt him, to try to get him to do wrong.
"If you really are the Son of God," said the devil, "then turn these stones into bread!"
But Jesus said no. He answered with God's own words: "People do not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from God."
The devil tried again. He took Jesus up to a very high place. "Jump off!" he said. "God's angels will catch you."
But Jesus said, "No. The Bible says we must not test God."
Then the devil showed Jesus all the kingdoms of the whole world. "I will give all of this to you," he said, "if you will just bow down and worship me."
But Jesus said firmly, "Go away! The Bible says we must worship and serve only God."
Three times the devil tried to get Jesus to do wrong. And three times, Jesus said no by trusting God and remembering God's words.
Finally, the devil gave up and went away. Then angels came and took care of Jesus.
Each time the devil tried something new, Jesus stood firm and trusted his Father in heaven. He never gave in to the devil, not even one single time.
Jesus knows exactly what it feels like to be tempted to do wrong. So when we are tempted, Jesus understands, and he helps us to do what is right.

devotional.bigIdea: The devil tried three times to get Jesus to do wrong, but Jesus said no every time by trusting God and God's words. Jesus understands when we are tempted, and he helps us do what is right.
questions: Jesus said no to wrong things by remembering God's words. How can knowing about God help you do right?
What is something that sometimes tempts you to not do the right thing?
Jesus understands when things are hard. How does it help to know Jesus can help you?

connections.title: Jesus Is Baptized
connections.why: Straight from the joy of his baptism, the Spirit leads Jesus into the desert to be tested before his ministry begins.

connections.title: The First Bad Choice
connections.why: Where Adam and Eve gave in to the tempter in a beautiful garden, Jesus stands firm against him in a barren desert.

connections.title: The Ten Commandments
connections.why: Jesus fights every temptation with God's own words, the Scriptures, showing how powerful and precious they are.

quiz.question: Where did Jesus go after his baptism?
options: The desert
A castle
A boat

quiz.question: How did Jesus answer the devil each time?
options: With God's words
By running
By hiding

quiz.question: How many times did Jesus say no to the devil?
options: Three times
Never
One time

stories.title: Come, Follow Me
stories.hook: Jesus walked by a big lake and saw some fishermen. He said three simple words that changed their lives: Come, follow me.
story: Jesus began to travel from town to town, telling people the good news about God's kingdom.
One day, Jesus was walking beside a big, beautiful lake called the Sea of Galilee.
Lots of people worked at that lake, catching fish to sell. It was hard work, and they did it every day.
As Jesus walked along, he saw two brothers, Simon Peter and Andrew. They were busy throwing their fishing net into the water.
Jesus called out to them, "Come, follow me! And I will make you fishers of people!"
And right away, the two brothers left their nets behind and followed Jesus.
A little farther down the shore, Jesus saw two more brothers, James and John. They were sitting in a boat with their father, fixing their nets.
Jesus called them too. And right away, they left their boat and their father and went to follow Jesus.
Now think about this. Jesus did not go to a palace to find kings, or to a school to find the smartest teachers.
Instead, Jesus walked along a lake and chose ordinary, hardworking fishermen to be his friends and followers.
And when Jesus called them, they did not wait or make excuses. They got up and followed him right away.
Over time, Jesus gathered twelve special followers, called disciples. They were just ordinary people, but Jesus loved them and taught them.
Those ordinary fishermen could never have guessed where following Jesus would take them, or all of the wonderful things they would get to see him do.
And Jesus still calls ordinary people like us today. He simply says, "Come, follow me!"

devotional.bigIdea: Jesus called ordinary fishermen, and they followed him right away. Jesus calls ordinary people like us too. He just wants us to trust him and follow.
questions: The fishermen followed Jesus right away. What do you think it means to follow Jesus?
Jesus picked ordinary people. How does it feel that Jesus wants you?
Is there something that would be hard to leave to follow Jesus?

connections.title: Five Loaves and Two Fish
connections.why: These ordinary fishermen Jesus calls become the disciples who help him feed thousands and learn from him every day.

connections.title: Stepping Out
connections.why: Simon Peter, who drops his nets here to follow Jesus, will later step out of a boat to walk to him on the water.

connections.title: The Man Who Walked Away
connections.why: The fishermen leave everything in a heartbeat to follow Jesus, unlike the rich young man who walks away sad, unwilling to let go.

quiz.question: What job did Simon Peter and Andrew have?
options: They were fishermen
They were kings
They were farmers

quiz.question: What did Jesus say to them?
options: Come, follow me
Go home
Give me a fish

quiz.question: How fast did they follow Jesus?
options: Right away
After many years
They did not

stories.title: Water into Wine
stories.hook: Jesus' very first miracle happened at a wedding party, when the drinks ran out and everyone was worried.
story: Jesus, his mother Mary, and his friends were all invited to a big wedding party in a town called Cana.
Back in those days, a wedding was a huge celebration with lots of food and fun. It could go on for many days!
But right in the middle of the party, something went wrong. They ran out of wine to drink.
This was very embarrassing for the family who was hosting the wedding. They did not know what to do.
Mary noticed the problem. She went to Jesus and said simply, "They have no more wine."
Then Mary turned to the servants nearby and told them, "Do whatever Jesus tells you to do."
Standing close by were six great big stone jars. Jesus said to the servants, "Fill the jars up with water." So they filled them all the way to the top.
Then Jesus said, "Now dip some out and take it to the man in charge of the party."
The servants did exactly what Jesus said, wondering what would happen.
But when the man in charge tasted it, the water had turned into wine! And it was not just any wine. It was the very best wine of all!
"Most people serve the best wine first," he said, surprised, "but you have saved the best for last!"
This was the very first miracle that Jesus ever did. It showed everyone how amazing and powerful he is, and his friends believed in him.
Only the servants who had filled up those big jars knew the wonderful secret of where the very best wine had really come from.
Jesus did his first miracle at a happy wedding. That shows us that Jesus cares about our happy times too, and whatever he touches, he makes wonderfully good.

devotional.bigIdea: Jesus' first miracle was at a happy wedding, where he made the very best wine. Jesus cares about our happy times too, and whatever he touches, he makes good.
questions: Mary said, do whatever Jesus tells you. Why is that good advice for us?
Jesus made the best wine, more than enough. What does that show about God?
Jesus' first miracle was at a happy party. What does that tell you about Jesus?

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: It is Mary, the mother who once said yes to God, who tells the servants here, do whatever he tells you.

connections.title: Five Loaves and Two Fish
connections.why: Just as Jesus would make a little food feed thousands, here he makes ordinary water into the very best wine, with plenty to spare.

connections.title: The Night Before
connections.why: Jesus' first sign uses cups of wine at a joyful feast, pointing ahead to the cup he shares with his friends the night before he dies.

quiz.question: Where did Jesus do his first miracle?
options: At a wedding party
On a boat
In a cave

quiz.question: What ran out at the party?
options: The wine to drink
The chairs
The music

quiz.question: What did Jesus turn the water into?
options: The best wine
Juice
Ice

stories.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
stories.hook: Jesus sat on a hillside and taught the most important things anyone has ever said. A lot of it was completely different from what people expected.
story: Large crowds followed Jesus everywhere because of the amazing things He was doing. One day He went up on a hillside, sat down, and began to teach.

He started with a list of surprising statements. He said blessed are the people who know they need God. Blessed are the people who are sad. Blessed are the gentle people. Blessed are the people who want to do what is right more than anything. Blessed are the kind people. Blessed are the people who make peace. Blessed are the people who are treated unfairly for doing right.
In those days people thought blessed meant rich and powerful and successful. Jesus said no. The people who are truly happy in the deepest way are the ones the world overlooks.
Then Jesus talked about anger. He said everyone knew the rule do not murder. But He said the real problem starts much earlier in your heart when you let anger grow and grow.
He talked about telling the truth. He said just say yes when you mean yes and no when you mean no. Keep it simple and honest.

He said something very hard. He said love your enemies. Pray for the people who treat you badly. Anyone can be kind to people who are kind to them. But God wants His people to love even the ones who are not kind.

He taught a prayer that people have prayed for two thousand years. Our Father in heaven. Your name is so great. May Your Kingdom come. Give us what we need today. Forgive us for the wrong things we have done and help us forgive others. Keep us safe from the evil one.

He said do not worry about food and clothes. Look at the birds. God feeds them. Are you not worth more than birds? Look at the wildflowers. God dresses them more beautifully than any king. Do not worry about tomorrow. Today is enough.

He finished with a picture. If you hear these words and do them you are like a man who builds his house on solid rock. Rain and wind and floods came. The house stood. But if you hear and do not do them you are like a man who builds on sand. When the storm came the house fell flat.

devotional.bigIdea: Jesus did not just tell people how to behave. He talked about what happens inside us in our hearts. He wanted people who were changed on the inside not just on the outside.
questions: Jesus said love your enemies and pray for people who treat you badly. Is there someone who is hard to be kind to? What would it look like to pray for them today?
Jesus said do not worry about tomorrow. Is there something you worry about? What do you think Jesus would say to you about it?
Jesus said those who hear His words and do them are like a house built on rock. What is one thing Jesus said that you want to actually do, not just know?

connections.title: The Ten Commandments
connections.why: Jesus said He came to complete the law not get rid of it. He showed the rules were always about the heart.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Jesus taught the Lord's prayer, our Father. The prodigal son's journey home is that prayer lived out.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Jesus said love your enemies. From the cross He prayed Father forgive them. He practised what He taught.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say about worry when He pointed to the birds?
options: He said worry is natural and everyone does it so try your best
He said God feeds the birds and they do not worry so trust that God will care for you too
He said birds are different from people so this is not really helpful
He said pray hard enough and your worries will go away
quiz.explanation: Jesus said God feeds the birds and clothes the flowers and you are worth much more than birds. Trust God with what you need.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say about the man who hears His words and does them?
options: He will be the most popular person everyone knows
He will never have any problems
He is like a man who builds his house on solid rock that stands even in a storm
He will be given lots of blessings and good things
quiz.explanation: Jesus said hearing and doing His words is like building on solid rock. The storms still come but the house stands.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say about loving enemies?
options: He said it was too hard so just try to stay away from them
He said love your enemies but you do not have to spend time with them
He said enemies usually become friends if you are patient
He said love your enemies and pray for people who treat you badly
quiz.explanation: Jesus said love your enemies and pray for people who are unkind to you. Anyone can love people who love them back. God asks for more.

stories.title: The Farmer and the Seeds
stories.hook: So many people came to hear Jesus that he taught from a boat. He told a story about a farmer who scattered seeds on four kinds of ground.
story: One day, such a big crowd came to hear Jesus teach that he got into a boat and pushed out a little way from the shore.
That way, all the people standing on the beach could see and hear him. Then Jesus taught them with a story.
"A farmer went out to plant his seeds," Jesus began. "And as he scattered them, the seeds fell on four different kinds of ground."
"Some of the seeds fell on the hard path. And the birds quickly flew down and gobbled them all up."
"Some of the seeds fell on rocky ground. They sprouted up fast, but their roots could not go down deep. So when the hot sun came out, the little plants dried up and died."
"Some of the seeds fell among thorny weeds. The seeds started to grow, but the prickly weeds grew up too and crowded out the little plants."
"But some of the seeds fell on good, soft soil. Those seeds grew up strong and healthy and made lots and lots of new grain!"
Later, Jesus told his friends what the story really meant.
The seed, he said, is God's word, the good news about him. And the different kinds of ground are like the different ways people's hearts receive it.
The hard path is a heart that hears God's word but does not understand it. The rocky ground is a heart that gives up when trouble comes.
The thorny ground is a heart that lets worries crowd God's word out. But the good soil is a heart that hears God's word, holds it close, and lets it grow.
Jesus wants each of us to have a heart like the good soil.
When we listen to God's word and hold on to it, it grows in us and makes our lives beautiful and good, like a field full of grain.

devotional.bigIdea: The same seeds fell everywhere, but only the good soil grew a big crop. God's word grows best in a heart that listens and holds on to it. Jesus wants our hearts to be like good soil.
questions: What do you think makes a heart like good soil?
Some things crowded out the little plants. What things might keep us from listening to God?
How can you keep your heart soft and ready to listen to God?

connections.title: The Tiny Mustard Seed
connections.why: Another of Jesus' seed stories about God's kingdom, showing how something tiny can grow into something great.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Like the wise builder who hears Jesus and obeys, the good soil stands for those who hear God's word and let it take root.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Jesus loved to teach with stories, and like the prodigal son, the parable of the sower reveals what our hearts are really like.

quiz.question: Why did Jesus teach from a boat?
options: The crowd was very big
He was fishing
He was tired

quiz.question: What did the seed in the story stand for?
options: God's word
Real food
Money

quiz.question: What kind of heart does Jesus want us to have?
options: A heart like good soil
A hard path
A thorny patch

stories.title: The Tiny Mustard Seed
stories.hook: Jesus said God's kingdom is like the smallest little seed. It looks like nothing, until you see what it grows into!
story: One day, Jesus wanted to help people understand what God's kingdom is like. So he told them a short story about a very small seed.
"The kingdom of heaven," Jesus said, "is like a tiny little mustard seed that a man planted in his garden."
Now, a mustard seed is one of the very smallest seeds of all.
You could hold a whole big bunch of them in the palm of your hand. And if you dropped just one on the ground, you might not even be able to find it again!
It really does look like nothing important at all.
"But when that tiny little seed is planted and begins to grow," Jesus went on, "it becomes the biggest plant in the whole garden."
"It grows into a tree so big and so tall that the birds of the sky come and build their nests in its branches!"
The smallest seed you can imagine grows into a tree big enough to give the birds a home. That is what God's kingdom is like.
God's kingdom often starts in ways that look tiny and unimportant. It started with a baby born in a stable. It started with twelve ordinary followers.
It can start with one small act of love, or one quiet prayer.
But God takes those small beginnings and grows them into something far bigger and more beautiful than anyone could ever imagine.
So we never have to feel like we are too small, or that the little things we do for God do not matter.
Isn't it amazing to think about? The biggest, tallest tree in the whole garden began as the very littlest seed of all.
In God's hands, even the tiniest seed of faith and love can grow into something truly wonderful.
