devotional.bigIdea: A tiny seed grows into a great big tree. God loves to take small things and grow them into something wonderful. You are never too small for God to use!
questions: Have you ever seen something tiny grow into something big?
What is one small, kind thing you could do for God?
How does it feel to know God can use even little you?

connections.title: The Farmer and the Seeds
connections.why: Another of Jesus' seed parables, teaching that God's kingdom grows in ways we do not always see.

connections.title: Five Loaves and Two Fish
connections.why: Like a few loaves feeding thousands, the smallest beginning in God's hands becomes something far greater than we expect.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: Jesus' kingdom began with a tiny handful of followers and grew, just like the mustard seed, into something that filled the world.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say the kingdom is like?
options: A tiny mustard seed
A big rock
A river

quiz.question: What does the tiny seed grow into?
options: A big tree for birds
A small weed
A flower

quiz.question: What does this teach us?
options: God grows small things into big ones
Only big things matter
Seeds are boring

stories.title: The Woman at the Well
stories.hook: Jesus stopped at a well in the middle of the day and talked to a woman that most people avoided. It changed her whole life.
story: Jesus and His disciples were travelling and stopped at a well in Samaria. Jewish people usually went the long way around to avoid Samaria because Jewish people and Samaritan people did not like each other. But Jesus went through.

His disciples went into the village to buy food. Jesus sat down at the well. It was the middle of the day, hot and bright. A Samaritan woman came to draw water. This was unusual because most women came in the cool of the morning together. This woman came alone at the hottest part of the day.

Jesus asked her for a drink of water. The woman was surprised. She said, "You are a Jewish man and I am a Samaritan woman. Why are you asking me?" Jesus said, "If you knew who was asking you for water you would have asked Him and He would have given you living water."

The woman did not understand. She said the well was deep and He had nothing to draw with. Jesus said, "Everyone who drinks this water will get thirsty again. But whoever drinks the water I give will never be thirsty again. The water I give becomes a spring inside you that bubbles up to life that never ends."

The woman said, "Sir, give me that water!" Jesus said, "Go and get your husband." She said, "I have no husband." Jesus said, "That is true. You have had five husbands and the man you are with now is not your husband."

The woman was amazed. This stranger knew everything about her and He was still talking to her. She said, "I can see you are a prophet." They talked about where to worship God. Jesus said the time was coming when it would not be about a place at all but about worshipping in spirit and in truth.
The woman said, "I know the Messiah is coming. When He comes He will explain everything." Jesus said, "I am He. I am speaking to you right now."

The woman left her water jar and ran back into the village. She told everyone, "Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done. Could this be the Messiah?" People came out to see. Many believed. They asked Jesus to stay and He stayed two days. More people believed because they heard Him themselves.

devotional.bigIdea: Jesus crossed every barrier to talk to a woman everyone else avoided. He knew everything about her and He still wanted to give her the best gift. Nobody is too far away for Jesus to find.
questions: The woman came to the well alone in the middle of the day. She was probably trying to avoid people. Has there ever been a time you felt like hiding from everyone? What happened?
Jesus knew everything about the woman and still sat and talked with her. How does it feel to know that Jesus knows everything about you and still loves you?
The woman ran to tell her whole village about Jesus. She had just met Him! What made her want to tell everyone so quickly?

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: The father ran to the prodigal son past every barrier. Jesus crossed into Samaria past every barrier to find one woman. Both show God pursuing people others have written off.

connections.title: Water From the Rock
connections.why: Moses hit a rock and water poured out. Jesus offered a woman living water that would never run dry. The rock in the desert was pointing to this conversation.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: One woman's story brought a whole village to Jesus. At Pentecost one speech brought three thousand. Both show how one encounter with Jesus spreads.

quiz.question: Why was it surprising that Jesus spoke to the Samaritan woman?
options: Because Jewish people and Samaritan people did not talk to each other and men did not speak to women alone in public
Because she was very important and He was just a traveller
Because the well was private property
Because it was too hot in the middle of the day for anyone to be out
quiz.explanation: Two barriers were crossed at once. Jewish people and Samaritans did not mix. And men and women did not speak alone in public. Jesus crossed both.

quiz.question: What did Jesus offer the woman that was better than the water in the well?
options: A place to stay with His disciples
Living water that would become a spring inside her welling up to life that never ends
Healing for whatever was making her sad
A way to make peace with the people she had been avoiding
quiz.explanation: Jesus offered living water, not physical water that would make her thirsty again but something that would satisfy the deepest thirst forever.

quiz.question: What did the woman do immediately after her conversation with Jesus?
options: She sat quietly at the well and thought about everything
She went home and told just her closest friend
She left her water jar and ran to tell the whole village about Him
She stayed at the well and waited for His disciples to come back
quiz.explanation: She left her water jar and ran. She told everyone come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done. She could not keep the news to herself.

stories.title: Through the Roof
stories.hook: Four friends wanted to bring their friend to Jesus. The house was so full that they had to lower him down through the roof!
story: One day, Jesus was teaching inside a house in a town called Capernaum.
So many people had crowded inside to hear him that there was no room left at all, not even outside the door!
Now, in that town lived a man who could not walk. And this man had four very good friends.
His four friends believed that if they could just get their friend to Jesus, Jesus could make him well.
So they carried their friend on his sleeping mat, all the way to the house. But when they got there, they could not get in. It was just too crowded.
But these friends did not give up! They had a bold idea.
They carried their friend up the outside stairs, all the way to the flat roof of the house. Then they began to dig a hole right through the roof!
When the hole was big enough, they carefully lowered their friend down on his mat, right in front of Jesus.
When Jesus saw how much the four friends believed, he was very pleased. He looked at the man on the mat and said, "Your sins are forgiven."
Some of the teachers sitting there were surprised, because only God can forgive sins. They did not understand that Jesus is God.
Then Jesus said to the man, "Get up, pick up your mat, and go home."
And right away, the man stood up! He picked up his mat and walked out through the crowd, all better. Everyone was amazed and praised God.
Imagine the looks on his four friends' faces as they watched him walk right out the door, carrying the very mat that he had been carried in on!
Good friends bring each other to Jesus. And Jesus made that man well, both inside and out.

devotional.bigIdea: Four friends would not let anything stop them from bringing their friend to Jesus. Jesus forgave him and made him walk. Good friends help each other get close to Jesus.
questions: The friends worked so hard to bring their friend to Jesus. How can you help a friend?
Jesus made the man well. What do you want to thank Jesus for?
What was the most exciting part of this story?

connections.title: Jesus Heals Blind Bartimaeus
connections.why: Another time Jesus heals someone whose faith would not be stopped, no matter what stood in the way.

connections.title: Jairus's Daughter
connections.why: Like Jairus, these friends are sure that if they can just get to Jesus, everything will change, and they are right.

connections.title: Jesus Calms the Storm
connections.why: Just as Jesus shows his power over the storm, here he shows his authority to do what only God can do, forgive sins.

quiz.question: Why couldn't the friends get inside?
options: The house was too crowded
The door was locked
It was raining

quiz.question: How did they get their friend to Jesus?
options: Through a hole in the roof
Through a window
They gave up

quiz.question: What did Jesus do for the man?
options: Forgave him and made him walk
Sent him away
Nothing

stories.title: Jesus Calms the Storm
stories.hook: A scary storm shook the little boat, and the disciples were afraid. But Jesus was fast asleep in the back!
story: After a long day of teaching, Jesus said to his friends, "Let us go across to the other side of the lake."
So they all climbed into a boat together and started to sail across the Sea of Galilee.
Jesus was very tired. So he went to the back of the boat, rested his head on a soft cushion, and fell fast asleep.
But suddenly, without any warning, a fierce storm came roaring down on the lake.
The wind howled, and the waves grew bigger and bigger. They crashed right over the sides of the boat, and water started pouring in.
The disciples were terrified, even though many of them were fishermen who knew the lake well.
But through it all, Jesus kept sleeping peacefully in the back of the boat.
The disciples could not believe it. They shook him awake, crying out, "Teacher! Don't you care that we are about to drown?"
Jesus stood up in the rocking boat. He spoke to the wind, and he said to the crashing waves, "Quiet! Be still!"
And in an instant, the wind stopped blowing, and the water became perfectly calm and smooth. The storm was completely gone!
Then Jesus turned to his friends. "Why were you so afraid?" he asked them gently. "Don't you trust me yet?"
The disciples were filled with wonder. "Who is this?" they whispered to each other. "Even the wind and the waves obey him!"
One moment the wild waves were crashing over the boat, and the next, the whole lake was as smooth and quiet as glass. Only God could do something like that!
Jesus was with them in the boat the whole time. And when we are scared, Jesus is with us too. We can always trust him.

devotional.bigIdea: A big storm scared the disciples, but Jesus calmed it with just his words. Jesus was with them the whole time. When we are scared, Jesus is with us, and we can trust him.
questions: The disciples were scared, but Jesus was calm. What helps you feel calm when you are afraid?
Jesus was in the boat the whole time. How does it help to know Jesus is with you?
What does it show you that the wind and waves obeyed Jesus?

connections.title: Stepping Out
connections.why: Another night on the same lake where Jesus shows that the wind and waves are no match for him.

connections.title: Come, Follow Me
connections.why: The frightened men in the boat are the fishermen Jesus called, learning that the one they followed has power over creation itself.

connections.title: In the Beginning
connections.why: The disciples ask, who is this that even the wind and waves obey him? The answer is the very One who made the sea in the beginning.

quiz.question: What was Jesus doing when the storm came?
options: Sleeping in the boat
Swimming
Fishing

quiz.question: What did Jesus say to the storm?
options: Quiet! Be still!
Hello!
Faster!

quiz.question: What happened after Jesus spoke?
options: The storm stopped and it was calm
It rained more
The boat sank

stories.title: Five Loaves and Two Fish
stories.hook: There were five thousand people and only five small loaves of bread and two little fish. Jesus said that would be enough.
story: A huge crowd had been following Jesus all day because they had seen Him heal sick people. By the end of the day they were far from any town and everyone was getting hungry.
Jesus asked His disciple Philip, "Where can we buy food for all these people?" Philip looked at the crowd and said, "Even eight months of wages would not be enough to buy a little bite for everyone."
Andrew, another disciple, came up with something. He said, "There is a boy here who has five small barley loaves and two small fish. But what use is that for so many people?"
The boy gave what he had. He did not know what Jesus would do with it. He just handed it over.

Jesus told everyone to sit down on the grass. About five thousand men sat down plus women and children too. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up to heaven and said thank you. Then He started breaking the bread and the fish and giving pieces to the disciples to pass out.
Everyone ate. Not just a little taste. Everyone had as much as they wanted. Five thousand people plus and everyone was full.

When they were done Jesus said, "Gather up the leftovers so nothing is wasted." The disciples went around collecting. They filled twelve large baskets with leftover pieces from the five barley loaves.
Twelve baskets of leftovers from what had started as five small rolls and two little fish. More was left over than what they had started with.

The people were amazed. They said, "This must be the prophet we have been waiting for!" A boy gave Jesus everything he had, which was not very much at all. Jesus gave thanks and it became more than enough.

devotional.bigIdea: A boy gave Jesus everything he had which was not very much at all. Jesus gave thanks and it became more than enough. What you have is never the point. What Jesus does with what you give is.
questions: The disciples said what use is this for so many people? Have you ever felt like what you had to offer was too small to matter? What does this story say about that?
Jesus gave thanks before the miracle happened before there was any sign it would work. What does it mean to be thankful before you see the result?
The crowd wanted to make Jesus king because He fed them. But Jesus was after something more important than full stomachs. What do you think He was really offering people?

connections.title: Water From the Rock
connections.why: The crowd called Jesus the Prophet who is to come. Moses brought water in the wilderness. Jesus fed thousands on a hillside.

connections.title: The Ark and the Rainbow
connections.why: Jesus gave thanks before distributing the food exactly as a Jewish family gave thanks at every meal. Gratitude to God for provision runs through the whole Bible.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus pulled back when the crowd tried to make Him king by force. He had already said His kingdom was not like worldly kingdoms.

quiz.question: How much food was there at the start?
options: A large basket of bread one family had brought
Twelve small portions the disciples had been keeping
Just one loaf of bread and nothing else
Five small loaves and two small fish that a boy had
quiz.explanation: A boy had five small barley loaves and two small fish. He gave them all to Jesus.

quiz.question: How many people ate and how much food was left over?
options: Five thousand men plus women and children all ate until full and twelve baskets of leftovers were collected
About five hundred people ate and a little was left over
The disciples ate first and then there was only a little left
Everyone had a small taste and the rest was left for the next day
quiz.explanation: Five thousand men plus women and children all ate as much as they wanted. Then the disciples collected twelve large baskets of leftovers.

quiz.question: What did Jesus do before He started breaking and sharing the bread?
options: He asked everyone to be absolutely quiet and still
He looked up to heaven and said thank you to God
He closed His eyes and concentrated very hard
He walked around the whole crowd so everyone could see Him
quiz.explanation: Jesus looked up to heaven and gave thanks before the miracle. Gratitude came before the multiplication.

stories.title: Stepping Out
stories.hook: The disciples were in a boat in a storm at night when they saw someone walking toward them on the water. Then Peter got out of the boat.
story: After feeding the five thousand Jesus sent His disciples ahead in a boat across the lake while He went up a hill to pray alone. By the middle of the night the boat was far from shore being battered by big waves because the wind was against them.
Then sometime between three and six in the morning the disciples saw someone walking on the water toward them through the waves. They screamed in terror. They thought it was a ghost.
Immediately Jesus called out, "It is alright! It is me! Do not be afraid!"
Peter called back, "Lord, if it is really you, tell me to come to you on the water!" Jesus said one word. "Come."
Peter got out of the boat. He stood on the water. He walked toward Jesus on the water. This was extraordinary. No one had ever done this before.
But then Peter looked at the wind and the massive waves around him. He got scared. And he started to sink.
"Lord! Save me!" he cried out. Immediately Jesus reached out His hand and caught him. Jesus said, "Why did you doubt? Why did you look away?"
They got back into the boat and the wind stopped. Every single disciple in the boat fell on their knees and said, "You really are the Son of God."

Peter got out of the boat. He was the only one who did. He also sank. But he walked first. And when he was going down he called the right name. And the hand was right there immediately. That is the whole story. Eyes on Jesus: walking. Eyes on the waves: sinking. And when you are sinking, call His name.

devotional.bigIdea: Peter stepped out and walked on water. He also sank. Both things matter. The getting out was brave. The sinking was real. And Jesus was right there immediately when Peter cried out.
questions: Peter was the only one who got out of the boat. Eleven disciples stayed safe inside. Which would you have been? What would it take to get out?
Peter walked on water until he looked at the waves. Have you ever been doing well at something until you got distracted by scary things around you?
Peter cried Lord save me and Jesus was right there immediately. What does the word immediately tell you about how Jesus responds when we call out to Him?

connections.title: Five Loaves and Two Fish
connections.why: This story happens right after the feeding of the five thousand. Two impossible things in the same evening.

connections.title: Three Hundred Men and Torches
connections.why: Gideon took his eyes off God and got scared too. Both stories show that faith is about where you look, not how strong you feel.

connections.title: Through the Sea on Dry Ground
connections.why: God made a path through the Red Sea on dry ground. Jesus walked on the surface of the same kind of sea. Water does not limit God.

quiz.question: What did the disciples think when they saw Jesus walking toward them on the water?
options: They recognised Him immediately and were very relieved
They thought it was someone from another boat who needed help
They thought it was a ghost and screamed in terror
They thought they were dreaming because they were so tired
quiz.explanation: They were terrified and thought it was a ghost. Jesus immediately called out and told them not to be afraid.

quiz.question: What happened when Peter got scared and looked at the waves?
options: He walked back to the boat safely
He froze and could not move at all
He swam the rest of the way to Jesus
He started sinking and cried out Lord save me
quiz.explanation: Peter took his eyes off Jesus and looked at the wind and huge waves. He got scared and began to sink. He cried out Lord save me.

quiz.question: What did Jesus do when Peter started sinking?
options: He immediately reached out His hand and caught him
He waited to see if Peter could sort it out himself
He called out instructions from where He was standing
He sent another disciple from the boat to help
quiz.explanation: Jesus immediately reached out and caught Peter. Not eventually. Immediately. That is how fast Jesus responds when we cry out to Him.

stories.title: Blind Bartimaeus
stories.hook: A blind man named Bartimaeus sat by the road. When he heard Jesus coming, he shouted as loud as he could!
story: One day, Jesus was leaving the town of Jericho, and a big crowd of people was following along with him.
Beside the road sat a blind man named Bartimaeus. Because he could not see, he could not work, so he sat there and begged people for help.
When Bartimaeus heard that Jesus was coming down the road, his heart leaped with hope.
He began to shout out as loud as he could, "Jesus! Please have mercy on me!"
Some of the people in the crowd told him to be quiet. "Hush!" they said. "Stop making such a noise!"
But Bartimaeus would not stop. He just shouted even louder, "Jesus, please have mercy on me!"
Then Jesus stopped walking. In the middle of that whole big crowd, Jesus stood still. "Call him over here," he said.
Suddenly the people changed their tune. "Cheer up!" they told Bartimaeus. "Get up! Jesus is calling for you!"
Bartimaeus threw off his coat, jumped up, and hurried over to Jesus as fast as he could.
"What do you want me to do for you?" Jesus asked him kindly.
"Teacher," said Bartimaeus, "I want to see!"
"Go," said Jesus. "Your faith has made you well." And right away, Bartimaeus could see!
The very first thing his eyes ever saw was the kind, loving face of Jesus.
Bartimaeus had not let the crowd, or his blindness, or anything else at all stop him from coming to Jesus. And Jesus had all the time in the world for him.
And then Bartimaeus did something wonderful. He followed Jesus right down the road. Jesus always hears us when we call out to him, and he stops for every single person.

devotional.bigIdea: Everyone told Bartimaeus to be quiet, but he kept calling out to Jesus, and Jesus stopped just for him. Jesus always hears us when we call to him.
questions: Bartimaeus would not stop calling for Jesus. How can you talk to Jesus when you need help?
Jesus stopped for one man. How does it feel that Jesus would stop for you?
After he could see, Bartimaeus followed Jesus. How can you follow Jesus?

connections.title: Through the Roof
connections.why: Like the four friends who would not give up, Bartimaeus refuses to let anything stop him from reaching Jesus.

connections.title: Jairus's Daughter
connections.why: Again Jesus tells someone, your faith has made you well, showing how he honors those who trust him.

connections.title: The Man in the Tree
connections.why: In the very same town of Jericho, Jesus stops for another person the crowd ignored, showing he notices every overlooked soul.

quiz.question: What could Bartimaeus not do?
options: See
Walk
Hear

quiz.question: What did he do when Jesus came near?
options: Shouted for help
Hid
Stayed quiet

quiz.question: What did Jesus do for him?
options: Helped him see
Gave him money
Walked away

stories.title: Jairus's Daughter
stories.hook: A worried dad begged Jesus to help his very sick little girl. Then sad news came: she had died. But Jesus said, do not be afraid.
story: A man named Jairus was an important leader in his town. But one day, he came to Jesus as a scared and worried father.
Jairus fell down at Jesus' feet and begged him, "My little girl is so sick. Please come and put your hands on her, so she will get well!" His daughter was only twelve years old.
So Jesus went with Jairus toward his house. But the crowd was so big, pushing in on every side, that they could only walk very slowly.
Jairus must have been so worried, because every moment counted.
Then, before they could get to the house, some people came with terrible news. "Your daughter has died," they said. "There is no need to bother the teacher anymore."
It seemed like all hope was gone. But Jesus heard the news, and he turned to Jairus.
"Do not be afraid," Jesus said gently. "Just believe."
When they reached the house, it was full of people crying and weeping with sadness.
Jesus said to them, "Why all this crying? The little girl is not dead. She is only sleeping." And the people laughed at him, because they were sure she was gone.
But Jesus sent everyone outside. Then he took the girl's mother and father, and three of his friends, into the room where she was lying.
Jesus gently took the little girl by the hand and said, "Little girl, I say to you, get up."
And right away, the little girl stood up and began to walk around! Her mother and father were filled with amazement and joy.
Then Jesus, so kind and caring, told them to give her something to eat. Jesus is powerful enough to defeat even death, and he is gentle and loving too. Nothing is ever hopeless with him.

devotional.bigIdea: When it seemed too late, Jesus said, do not be afraid, just believe. Jesus is powerful even over death, and he is gentle and kind. Nothing is ever hopeless with Jesus.
questions: Jesus said, do not be afraid. What helps you feel brave when you are scared?
Jesus made the little girl well. What do you want to thank Jesus for?
Jesus said to give her food. What does that show about how kind Jesus is?

connections.title: The Shunammite Woman's Faith
connections.why: Long before, God raised a child to life through Elisha, and now Jesus does the same for a desperate father's daughter.

connections.title: Come Out
connections.why: Just as Jesus calls Lazarus from the tomb, here he gently takes a little girl by the hand and brings her back to life.

connections.title: Through the Roof
connections.why: Jesus tells Jairus, do not be afraid, just believe, the same trusting faith that brought the paralyzed man to him.

quiz.question: What did Jairus ask Jesus to do?
options: Help his sick little girl
Build a house
Tell a story

quiz.question: What did Jesus tell Jairus when sad news came?
options: Do not be afraid, just believe
Go home
Too late

quiz.question: What happened when Jesus took the girl's hand?
options: She got up and walked
She slept
Nothing

stories.title: The One Who Said Thank You
stories.hook: Ten sick men were all made well by Jesus on the same day. But only one of them remembered to come back and say thank you!
story: One day, as Jesus was traveling along, he came near a village where ten men were waiting. All ten of them had a terrible skin sickness called leprosy.
Because they were sick, these men were not allowed to live with everyone else. They had to stay far away from their families and friends. They were very lonely.
Since they could not come close, the ten men stood at a distance and called out together as loud as they could.
"Jesus, Master, please have mercy on us!" they cried.
When Jesus saw them, he felt sorry for them. "Go," he said, "and show yourselves to the priests."
Now, only a priest could say a person was healed and could go home. So Jesus was asking the men to start walking before anything had even happened. He was asking them to trust him.
And the ten men did! They obeyed and started off down the road.
And as they were walking, an amazing thing happened. Their skin became clean and healthy. Every single one of them was completely healed!
Nine of the men were so excited that they hurried off to start their new lives. But one of the men stopped.
When he saw that he was healed, he turned around and came running back to Jesus, praising God at the top of his voice!
He threw himself down at Jesus' feet and said thank you, again and again. And this man was a foreigner, someone many people looked down on.
Jesus looked around and asked, "Weren't all ten of them healed? Where are the other nine? Only this one came back to say thank you."
All ten men received the wonderful gift of healing. But only one remembered to come back and say thank you.
God gives us so many good gifts every day. Let us always remember, like that one thankful man, to stop and say thank you to God.

devotional.bigIdea: Ten men were healed, but only one came back to say thank you. God gives us so many good gifts. Let's always remember to thank him.
questions: Why do you think it is easy to forget to say thank you?
What are some things you can thank God for today?
How can you remember to be thankful every day?

connections.title: Naaman and the Servant Girl
connections.why: Like Naaman, who was healed of leprosy and turned to thank God, one healed man here returns with a grateful heart, and he too is a foreigner.

connections.title: Blind Bartimaeus
connections.why: Like Bartimaeus, these men cry out from a distance, Jesus, have mercy on us, and Jesus answers their cry.

connections.title: Mary and Martha
connections.why: Both stories ask what really matters most: not just being busy or healed, but turning back to be with Jesus.

quiz.question: How many men did Jesus make well?
options: Ten
One
Three

quiz.question: How many came back to say thank you?
options: Only one
All of them
None

quiz.question: What should we always remember to do?
options: Thank God for his gifts
Run fast
Stay quiet

stories.title: The One Who Stopped
stories.hook: A man was badly hurt on the road and two important religious men walked right past him. Then a Samaritan came along.
story: A man who knew the law very well came to test Jesus. He asked, "What do I have to do to have life that never ends?" Jesus said, "What does the law say?" The man answered, "Love God with everything you have and love your neighbour as much as you love yourself." Jesus said, "Right. Do that."
But the man wanted to limit the question. He said, "And who is my neighbour?" He was trying to find out how small he could make the circle of people he had to care about.

Jesus told him a story. A man was travelling from Jerusalem to Jericho, a road that went through wild rocky country where robbers often hid. He was attacked. The robbers beat him up badly, took everything he had, and left him lying on the road barely alive.
A priest came along the same road. He saw the hurt man. He moved to the other side of the road and walked past.
Another religious man who worked in the Temple came along. He saw the hurt man. He also moved to the other side of the road and walked past.

Then a Samaritan man came along. Jewish people and Samaritans did not like each other and usually had nothing to do with each other. The Samaritan saw the hurt man. And he felt so sorry for him that he could not keep walking.

He went to the man. He cleaned and bandaged his wounds using olive oil and wine. He put him on his own donkey and walked beside him to an inn. He took care of him all night. The next morning he gave the innkeeper money and said, "Look after this man. If it costs more than this I will pay you back when I come through again."
Jesus said to the man who had asked the question, "Which of these three was a neighbour to the man who was hurt?" The man said, "The one who was kind to him." Jesus said, "Go and do the same."

devotional.bigIdea: The priest and the Levite knew all the right rules but they walked past a person who needed help. The Samaritan knew less but he stopped. Knowing what to do and doing it are two different things.
questions: The priest and the Levite both knew the law very well but they walked past the hurt man. Have you ever known the right thing to do but found it hard to actually do it?
The Samaritan helped someone he was not even supposed to like. Is there someone you find it hard to be kind to? What would it look like to be like the Samaritan toward them?
Jesus said go and do the same. Not just think about it. Do it. What is one thing you could do this week that looks like the Samaritan stopping?

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: The expert quoted the two greatest commandments correctly. The whole sermon was about the difference between knowing the law and actually living it.

connections.title: The Woman at the Well
connections.why: In both stories a Samaritan is the hero. Jesus kept honouring people His audience looked down on.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: The father ran to the prodigal son. The Samaritan stopped for a stranger. Both did more than was required and paid a price nobody asked them to pay.

quiz.question: What did the priest and the other religious man do when they saw the hurt man?
options: They stopped and prayed for him from a safe distance
They moved to the other side of the road and walked past without helping
They went to get help and planned to come back later
They were too far away to see him properly
quiz.explanation: Both the priest and the Levite saw the man and walked past on the other side. They knew the rules but they did not stop.

quiz.question: What did the Samaritan do when he saw the hurt man?
options: He felt sorry but kept going because he was in a hurry
He gave the man a little money and wished him well
He stopped, cleaned his wounds, put him on his donkey, took him to an inn, paid for his care, and promised to pay more if needed
He stayed with the man until someone else came along
quiz.explanation: The Samaritan did everything. He bandaged wounds, used his own donkey, paid for the inn, and promised to pay more later. He went far beyond what anyone could expect.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say at the end of the story?
options: He said knowing who your neighbour is is the most important thing
He said remember this story so you can tell it to others
He said the priest and the Levite should have done better
He said go and do the same as the Samaritan did
quiz.explanation: Jesus said go and do the same. Not think about it, not feel good about it. Do it. The point was always action.

stories.title: The Lost Sheep
stories.hook: Jesus told a story about a shepherd who had a hundred sheep. When just one got lost, he went to find it!
story: Some people did not like that Jesus was so kind to people who had done wrong things. They grumbled about it.
So Jesus told them a story to help them understand God's heart.
"Imagine that you have a hundred sheep," said Jesus, "and one of them wanders off and gets lost."
"What would you do? Wouldn't you leave the ninety-nine other sheep and go out to look for the one that is lost, until you found it?"
Every single sheep was special to the shepherd. He would not just shrug and say, "Oh well, I still have ninety-nine."
That one little lost sheep, all alone and scared, was worth going after.
So the shepherd went looking. He climbed up the hills and down through the valleys, calling and searching, until at last he found his lost little sheep!
And when he found it, he was not even angry. He was filled with joy!
Gently, the shepherd lifted the sheep up and laid it across his shoulders. Then he carried it all the way home.
When he got home, he called all his friends and neighbors together. "Be happy with me!" he said. "I have found my lost sheep!"
Then Jesus explained the story. "In the very same way," he said, "all of heaven is filled with joy when even one lost person comes back to God."
Jesus is just like that good shepherd. He loves every single one of us so much.
The shepherd could have decided that one little sheep was not worth all the trouble. But to him, that one lost sheep was worth absolutely everything.
You are never too lost or too far away for Jesus to come looking for you. And when you are found, all of heaven celebrates!

devotional.bigIdea: The shepherd went to find his one lost sheep and was so happy when he found it. Jesus is that shepherd. He loves you so much that he would come looking just for you.
questions: How does it feel to know Jesus would come looking just for you?
The shepherd was so happy he had a party. Why is finding the lost sheep such good news?
How can you show love to someone who feels lost or left out?

connections.title: The Lost Coin
connections.why: Jesus tells this story right alongside the lost coin, both about the joy of finding what was lost.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: All three stories in a row, the lost sheep, the lost coin, and the lost son, show how much heaven rejoices when the lost are found.

connections.title: The Lord Is My Shepherd
connections.why: Jesus is the Good Shepherd who goes after the one lost sheep, just as the shepherd of Psalm 23 watches over his own.

quiz.question: What did the shepherd do when one sheep got lost?
options: Went to find it
Ignored it
Got a new one

quiz.question: How did the shepherd feel when he found the sheep?
options: So happy he had a party
Angry
Sad

quiz.question: Who is like the good shepherd?
options: Jesus
A king
A farmer

stories.title: The Lost Coin
stories.hook: A woman had ten special coins. When she lost just one, she searched her whole house until she found it!
story: Right after Jesus told the story of the lost sheep, he told another little story that meant the very same thing.
"Imagine that a woman has ten silver coins," said Jesus, "and one day, she loses one of them."
Those ten coins were very precious to the woman. They might have been all the money she had in the whole world.
So losing even one of them made her very sad and worried.
What did the woman do? Did she just shrug and say, "Oh well, I still have nine"? No, she did not!
Instead, she lit a lamp to brighten up the house. Then she got a broom and swept the floor, looking carefully in every single corner.
She searched and searched, low and high, and she would not stop looking until she found that one lost coin.
And then, at last, she found it! Oh, how happy she was!
The woman was so full of joy that she ran to call all her friends and neighbors together.
"Be happy with me!" she said. "I have found my lost coin!" Her joy was so big that she just could not keep it to herself.
Then Jesus told everyone what the story meant. "In the very same way," he said, "the angels of heaven are filled with joy when even one lost person comes back to God."
You are so very precious to God, just like that one special coin.
Just think about it. The woman would not stop and rest until that one little coin was found and safe in her hand again. That is exactly how much each one of us matters to God.
God never loses interest and never gives up. And when one lost person is found, all of heaven breaks into a happy celebration!

devotional.bigIdea: The woman searched everywhere for one special coin. That is how special you are to God. He never gives up, and heaven celebrates when one person comes home to him.
questions: Have you ever looked really hard for something you lost?
How does it feel to be so precious to God?
The woman shared her happy news. Who could you tell about God's love?

connections.title: The Lost Sheep
connections.why: Jesus tells this story right after the lost sheep, making the very same point: God searches for what is lost.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: The third in the trio of lost-and-found stories, all about the joy of heaven when one lost person comes home.

connections.title: The Man in the Tree
connections.why: Jesus said he came to seek and to save the lost, which is exactly what the searching woman in this story does.

quiz.question: What did the woman lose?
options: One of her special coins
Her sheep
Her shoes

quiz.question: What did she do to find it?
options: Swept the whole house and searched
Took a nap
Bought a new one

quiz.question: How precious are you to God?
options: Very precious, like the special coin
Not very
A little

stories.title: Let the Children Come
stories.hook: Parents brought their little children to Jesus. When the disciples tried to send them away, Jesus said, let them come to me!
story: Everywhere that Jesus went, people wanted to be close to him.
One day, some parents started bringing their little children to Jesus. They hoped that Jesus would put his hands on them and bless them.
Mothers and fathers carried their babies and led their little ones toward Jesus.
But the disciples did not like this at all. They thought that Jesus was much too busy and important for little children.
So the disciples tried to shoo the families away. "Do not bother the teacher!" they said.
When Jesus saw what his disciples were doing, he was not happy with them at all.
"Let the little children come to me," Jesus said firmly. "Do not ever stop them! For the kingdom of God belongs to children just like these."
The disciples had it all backward. They thought the children were not important enough. But Jesus said God's kingdom belongs to them!
Then Jesus said something even more surprising. "Anyone who wants to come into God's kingdom," he said, "must come like a little child."
He meant that everyone, even grown-ups, must come to God simply and trustingly, the way a child does.
And then Jesus did exactly what the parents had hoped for. He took the children up into his arms, one by one.
He put his hands on them and blessed them. Jesus was never, ever too busy for the children. He had time for every single one of them.
How happy and how safe those little children must have felt, wrapped up in the arms of Jesus himself!
Jesus loves children, and that means he loves you! You are never too small or too young for Jesus. He always says, "Come to me!"

devotional.bigIdea: The disciples thought Jesus was too busy for children, but Jesus welcomed them with open arms. You are never too small for Jesus. He always has time for you.
questions: Jesus always has time for you. How does that make you feel?
Jesus took the children in his arms. What would you tell Jesus if you sat next to him?
Jesus loves children. How can you show Jesus' love to other kids?

connections.title: Jairus's Daughter
connections.why: Jesus shows again how much he loves children, taking them in his arms just as he took a little girl by the hand.

connections.title: The Lost Sheep
connections.why: Just as the shepherd treasures one little sheep, Jesus makes clear that not one child is unimportant to him.

connections.title: The Tiny Mustard Seed
connections.why: Jesus says we must receive God's kingdom like a little child, with the same simple, trusting faith.

quiz.question: What did the disciples try to do with the children?
options: Send them away
Teach them
Feed them

quiz.question: What did Jesus say?
options: Let the little children come to me
Send them home
Be quiet

quiz.question: What did Jesus do with the children?
options: Held them and blessed them
Ignored them
Walked away

stories.title: The Man in the Tree
stories.hook: The most hated man in Jericho climbed a tree to see Jesus. Jesus looked up and said come down, I am coming to your house today.
story: Jesus was passing through Jericho. A man named Zacchaeus was the chief tax collector there and he was very rich. Tax collectors worked for the Romans collecting money from their own Jewish people and keeping extra for themselves. Zacchaeus was the boss of all the tax collectors. Everyone in Jericho disliked him.

Zacchaeus heard that Jesus was coming through and desperately wanted to see Him. But he was short and the crowd was thick and he could not see a thing. So this wealthy and important man ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree like a little child just to get a look.
When Jesus reached the tree He stopped. He looked up. He said, "Zacchaeus! Come down right now! I must come to your house today!"
Zacchaeus could not get down fast enough. He was overjoyed and welcomed Jesus.
The crowd was not happy. They muttered among themselves. "Jesus has gone to stay with a sinner." They thought Jesus had made a mistake.

But something was already happening to Zacchaeus. He stood up straight and said to Jesus, "Lord, I am going to give half of everything I own to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone out of anything I will pay them back four times what I took."
Nobody had asked him to do this. Jesus had not told him to. The presence of Jesus just changed everything about what Zacchaeus wanted.
Jesus said, "Today salvation has come to this house. This man is also a son of Abraham. The Son of Man came to look for the lost and to save them."
Zacchaeus did not become good and then meet Jesus. He met Jesus and then became good. That is always how it works.

devotional.bigIdea: Zacchaeus climbed a tree just to catch a glimpse of Jesus. Jesus stopped and called him by name. The whole crowd had written Zacchaeus off. Jesus had not. Nobody is too far gone for Jesus to stop and call their name.
questions: Zacchaeus was a grown-up important man and he climbed a tree because he wanted to see Jesus so badly. Have you ever really wanted to get closer to Jesus? What did you do?
The crowd thought Jesus had made a mistake going to Zacchaeus's house. But Jesus knew exactly what He was doing. Have you ever been surprised by who Jesus loves?
Zacchaeus gave away half his money and paid back four times what he had taken and nobody even asked him to. What does that tell you about what happens when someone really meets Jesus?

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: Jesus said the Son of Man came to seek and save the lost. The father ran to the prodigal son. Both stories show God moving toward the lost rather than waiting for them.

connections.title: The Woman at the Well
connections.why: The crowd muttered that Jesus had gone to be the guest of a sinner, the same reaction as with the Samaritan woman. Jesus kept choosing people everyone else had rejected.

connections.title: The Man Who Hunted Christians
connections.why: Jesus called Zacchaeus by name out of a tree. Jesus called Paul by name on a road. Both were living in ways that put them outside the community. Jesus found both anyway.

quiz.question: Why did Zacchaeus climb a tree?
options: He was short and the crowd was thick and he wanted to see Jesus
He was hiding because he knew people did not like him
He was trying to get Jesus's attention by doing something silly
He liked climbing trees and did it all the time
quiz.explanation: Zacchaeus was short and could not see over the crowd. So this wealthy important man ran ahead and climbed a tree like a little child just to get a look at Jesus.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say when He looked up and saw Zacchaeus in the tree?
options: Come down when you are ready and we can talk later
Zacchaeus come down right now, I must come to your house today
Why are you up there? That is very undignified
I can see you up there. Now pay back the people you have cheated
quiz.explanation: Jesus called him by name and said I must come to your house today. He invited Himself to the home of the person everyone else was avoiding.

quiz.question: What did Zacchaeus say he would do after Jesus came to his house?
options: He said he would stop collecting taxes and find a different job
He said he would use his money to build a new place of worship
He said he would give half of everything to the poor and pay back four times what he had taken from people
He said he would follow Jesus and travel with His disciples
quiz.explanation: Zacchaeus said he would give half everything to the poor and repay four times what he had taken. Nobody asked him to. Meeting Jesus changed what he wanted.

stories.title: Mary and Martha
stories.hook: Jesus visited two sisters. One sister stayed busy in the kitchen. The other sat down to be near Jesus, and Jesus said she chose the better thing.
story: One day, Jesus came to visit two sisters named Martha and Mary, who lived together in a town called Bethany.
Martha was so happy to have Jesus as her guest. She wanted everything to be just perfect for him.
So Martha got very, very busy. There was a meal to cook, and a house to clean, and so much to do.
She rushed all around the house, working hard to get everything ready.
But her sister Mary did something different. Mary came and sat down at Jesus' feet.
And she just listened, quietly drinking in every word that Jesus said.
This started to bother Martha more and more. She was doing all the work, while her sister just sat there!
Finally, Martha came to Jesus, feeling a little upset. "Lord," she said, "don't you care that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Please tell her to come and help me!"
Jesus answered her gently and with great love. "Martha, Martha," he said kindly, "you are worried and upset about so many things."
"But really, only one thing is needed," Jesus told her. "Mary has chosen the better thing, and it will not be taken away from her."
Martha was not wrong to work hard, and she was not wrong to want to be a good host.
But in all of her busyness, she had nearly missed the most important thing of all, which was simply spending time with Jesus.
Martha was busy with many good and helpful things. But Mary had found the one best thing of all, which was simply being close to Jesus.
It is good to be helpful and to work hard. But Jesus reminds us that nothing in the whole world is more important than stopping to be with him.

devotional.bigIdea: Martha was so busy that she missed sitting with Jesus. Mary chose to be with him, and Jesus said that was best. Nothing is more important than spending time with Jesus.
questions: What keeps you so busy that you forget to spend time with God?
Mary sat and listened to Jesus. How can you listen to God?
How could you make time just to be with Jesus?

connections.title: Come Out
connections.why: Mary and Martha are the sisters of Lazarus, the friend Jesus would later raise from the dead in this same town of Bethany.

connections.title: The Farmer and the Seeds
connections.why: Martha's worries threaten to crowd out time with Jesus, just like the thorns that choke the seed in the parable of the sower.

connections.title: Let the Children Come
connections.why: Mary simply sits at Jesus' feet to be near him, the same trusting, childlike way Jesus welcomes everyone to come to him.

quiz.question: What was Martha busy doing?
options: Working hard to get ready
Sleeping
Playing

quiz.question: What did Mary do?
options: Sat with Jesus and listened
Went home
Cooked

quiz.question: What did Jesus say matters most?
options: Spending time with him
Cooking a big meal
Cleaning the house

stories.title: The Man Who Walked Away
stories.hook: A man ran up to Jesus and asked how to have life that never ends. He had done everything right his whole life. Then Jesus told him the one thing he was not able to do.
story: A young man came running up to Jesus and knelt down in front of Him. Something real was driving him. He said, "Good teacher, what must I do to have life that never ends?"
Jesus asked him about the commandments. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not lie. Honour your father and mother. The young man said, "I have kept all of these since I was a boy."

The Bible says something important at this point. It says Jesus looked at the young man and loved him. He saw everything about this person and loved him. Then He said, "There is one thing you are missing. Go and sell everything you have and give the money to the poor. You will have treasure in heaven. Then come and follow me."
The young man's face fell. He was very sad. And he walked away. Because he owned a great deal.

Jesus watched him go. He turned to His disciples and said, "How hard it is for those who have riches to enter the kingdom of God." The disciples were very surprised. In those days people thought that being rich meant God had blessed you.
Jesus said it was easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God. The disciples were now very confused. "Then who can be saved?" they asked.
Jesus said, "With people it is impossible. But not with God. All things are possible with God."
The man had asked the right question and come with the right posture and had kept the rules his whole life. But he had one thing he loved more than God. And he could not let go of it.

Jesus did not run after him. He did not change the terms. He watched him walk away and He was sad about it. This is one of the saddest moments in the Gospels. A person came so close and then chose the wrong thing.

devotional.bigIdea: Jesus looked at the young man and loved him. Then He told him the hard thing. Sometimes love means telling someone the true thing they are not ready to hear.
questions: The young man had kept all the rules but still felt like something was missing. Have you ever done all the right things but still felt like something was not quite right?
Jesus asked the young man to give away the thing he loved most. What do you think Jesus might ask you to give up or hold more loosely?
The young man walked away sad. Jesus did not change the terms or run after him. What does that tell you about how Jesus treats our freedom to choose?

connections.title: The Wisest Request
connections.why: Solomon asked for the one thing that would put everything in right order. The rich young man could not give up the one thing that was out of order.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus said in the Sermon on the Mount you cannot serve both God and money. This story shows what it looks like when someone makes the wrong choice.

connections.title: The Boy Who Came Home
connections.why: The prodigal son ran to his father when he came to himself. The rich young man ran to Jesus but walked away. Both were loved. Both were free to choose.

quiz.question: What did the young man say when Jesus listed the commandments?
options: He admitted he had broken several and wanted to make things right
He said he tried his best but nobody could keep them all perfectly
He said he had kept all of them since he was a boy
He said he had never heard these rules before
quiz.explanation: The young man said teacher I have kept all of these since I was a boy. He was not bragging. He was being honest. Jesus did not argue with him.

quiz.question: What did the Bible say Jesus felt when He looked at the young man?
options: He felt frustrated that the man could not see what he was missing
He felt sad because He could see the man was going to make the wrong choice
He felt impressed by how faithfully the man had kept the rules
He loved him
quiz.explanation: The Bible says Jesus looked at him and loved him. The hard thing He said next came from that love not from wanting the man to fail.

quiz.question: Why did the young man walk away sad?
options: Because he had great wealth and loved it more than he was willing to give it up
Because Jesus had told him things he had already tried and they had not worked
Because he was too proud to admit he needed help
Because he did not believe Jesus could give him what he was asking about
quiz.explanation: He had great wealth and he could not let go of it. He had done everything right but he had one thing he loved more than God.

stories.title: The Boy Who Came Home
stories.hook: A son took all his father's money and wasted every penny. When he ran out of food he decided to go home. His father saw him coming from far away.
story: Jesus told this story to people who thought they were better than others. A man had two sons. The younger son came to his father and said, "Father, give me my share of everything now." This was like saying I wish you were dead, I just want your money. But the father gave it to him.

The son took everything and travelled far away. He spent all the money on wild living and parties and things that did not last. Then a terrible famine came and suddenly there was no food and the son had nothing left.
He ended up working on a farm feeding pigs. He was so hungry he wanted to eat the pigs' food. Nobody helped him.

Then something happened. The Bible says he came to his senses. He thought, even my father's servants have plenty of food. I am here starving. I will go home and say to my father, I have sinned against God and against you. I am not good enough to be called your son. Just make me one of your servants.
He started the long walk home.

While he was still far away his father saw him. The father had been watching the road. He recognised his son at a distance and he ran. He ran down the road toward his son, threw his arms around him, and kissed him.
The son started his speech. Father I have sinned against God and against you. I am not worthy to be called your son.

The father did not let him finish. He called out to his servants, "Quick! Bring the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and new sandals on his feet. Kill the fatted calf and let us celebrate. My son was dead and is alive again. He was lost and now he is found." And the party began.
The father ran. That is the whole heart of the story. Before the apology was finished, before any conditions were set, the father ran. That is what God is like.

devotional.bigIdea: The father ran. Long before the son finished his sorry speech, before any conditions were set, the father ran toward his son. That is what God is like when we come back to Him.
questions: The father saw his son while he was still far away because he had been watching the road. What does it mean to you that God is watching and waiting for you?
The son practised a whole speech about being a servant. But the father gave him a robe, a ring, and a party instead. What is the difference between what the son expected and what the father gave?
Have you ever done something wrong and felt too ashamed to come back and say sorry? What does this story say to that feeling?

connections.title: The First Bad Choice
connections.why: The prodigal son came to his senses in a far country just like Adam and Eve hiding from God. Both stories are about people who ran from the Father and found that coming back is always possible.

connections.title: Wherever You Go
connections.why: The father ran to his son, the same extravagant love that Boaz showed Ruth. Both ran toward someone who expected rejection.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus told this story because religious people were complaining He spent time with sinners. The whole story shows why He did.

quiz.question: What did the younger son ask his father for at the start of the story?
options: Permission to travel and see the world
His share of the family's money now, before his father had died
A bigger allowance so he could save up more
His father's blessing before he went to find work
quiz.explanation: The son asked for his inheritance early which was like saying I wish you were dead and I just want your money. The father gave it anyway.

quiz.question: What did the father do when he saw his son coming home from far away?
options: He waited at the door for his son to come and apologise properly
He sent a servant out to bring the son in
He ran down the road, threw his arms around his son, and kissed him before the son could even finish his speech
He was glad his son had come back but made him wait outside
quiz.explanation: The father saw him far away and ran. He did not wait for the apology to be finished. He ran. That is the whole heart of the story.

quiz.question: What did the father say when his son started his rehearsed apology speech?
options: He listened carefully and then said he needed time to think before forgiving him
He told the son to go and work as a servant first and earn his way back
He said he forgave him but they would not have a party until the older brother agreed
He did not let the son finish but called for the best robe and ring and sandals and threw a party
quiz.explanation: The father interrupted the speech with a robe, a ring, sandals, and a feast. No conditions. No waiting. Just celebration that his son was home.

stories.title: Come Out
stories.hook: Jesus heard His friend was dying. He waited two more days before going. When He arrived His friend had been dead for four days. Then Jesus cried.
story: Jesus had close friends in a town called Bethany, two sisters named Mary and Martha and their brother Lazarus. One day a message came. Lazarus was very sick. Mary and Martha sent a simple message. Lord, the one you love is sick.
They expected Jesus to come straight away. But He did not. He stayed where He was for two more days. Then He said, "Let us go to Bethany."

By the time Jesus arrived Lazarus had been in the tomb for four days. Martha ran out to meet Him. She said, "Lord, if you had been here my brother would not have died." Then she added quietly, "But even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you."

Jesus said, "Your brother will rise again." Martha said, "I know he will rise again in the resurrection at the end of everything." Jesus said, "I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die. Do you believe this?" She said yes.

Mary came out and said the same thing Martha had said. Lord if you had been here. She was crying. All the people with her were crying. And then Jesus stood there and looked at the grief all around Him and He cried too.
He cried even though He was about to fix everything. He did not stand above the sadness and say this will all be sorted in a minute. He stood inside it with the people He loved.

They went to the tomb. It was a cave with a stone across the entrance. Jesus said roll the stone away. Martha said it had been four days and the smell would be terrible. Jesus said, "Did I not tell you that if you believe you will see the glory of God?" They rolled the stone away.
Jesus looked up and prayed out loud so everyone could hear. Then He called out in a loud voice, "Lazarus, come out!"
And Lazarus walked out. Still wrapped in the grave cloths from head to foot. Jesus said, "Unwrap him and let him go."

devotional.bigIdea: Jesus cried at the tomb of Lazarus even though He was about to raise him. He did not stand above the sadness. He walked into it. God is not far away from your sad moments. He is right inside them with you.
questions: Jesus waited two extra days before going to Bethany even though He loved Lazarus. Mary and Martha could not understand why. Have you ever had to wait for God to act and not understood the delay?
Jesus cried at the tomb even though He knew He was going to raise Lazarus. What does that tell you about how God feels about our sadness?
Jesus said I am the resurrection and the life. What do you think that means for people who love Jesus?

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: Jesus said I am the resurrection and the life at Lazarus's tomb. Three chapters later He proved it by rising from the dead Himself.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Raising Lazarus was the miracle that pushed the religious leaders to plan to kill Jesus. The gift of life to one man led to the death that gave life to all.

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: Jesus told Martha did I not tell you that if you believe you will see the glory of God. The same God who told Mary nothing is impossible was now standing at the tomb.

quiz.question: What did Jesus do when He saw Mary and all her friends crying at the tomb?
options: He stood there and cried too
He told them there was no need to cry because everything would be fine
He stayed a little back to give them space to grieve
He asked everyone to be quiet so He could concentrate
quiz.explanation: Jesus saw their grief and He cried. He did not stand above the sadness. He walked into it with them.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say He was when He spoke to Martha?
options: The way, the truth, and the life
The resurrection and the life, whoever believes in me will live even though they die
The good shepherd who lays down His life
The light of the world who takes away the darkness of death
quiz.explanation: Jesus said I am the resurrection and the life. Then He proved it.

quiz.question: What happened when Jesus called out Lazarus, come out?
options: Nothing happened immediately but Lazarus woke up later that day
A voice came from inside the tomb but nothing else happened
Lazarus walked out still wrapped in his grave cloths
The stone rolled away by itself and Lazarus walked out
quiz.explanation: Lazarus walked out wrapped in his grave cloths. Jesus said unwrap him and let him go.

stories.title: The King Rides In
stories.hook: Jerusalem was waiting for a king to arrive on a great warhorse. Jesus came in on a donkey. The crowd went absolutely wild anyway.
story: Jesus was heading to Jerusalem for the last time. He sent two disciples ahead to a village and told them exactly where to find a donkey and her young colt. He said if anyone asks why you are untying them just say the Lord needs them. They found everything exactly as Jesus had said.

This was not an accident. A prophet named Zechariah had written five hundred years before that your king is coming to you, gentle, and riding on a donkey. Jesus was doing what the prophet had said on purpose. He was announcing something.

The disciples put their cloaks on the donkey and Jesus sat on it. As He rode toward Jerusalem something amazing happened. The crowd grew and grew. People spread their cloaks on the road in front of Him like a red carpet for royalty. Others cut branches from palm trees and laid them down.
They shouted, "Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!" Hosanna means save us now. They were crying out to a king.
When Jesus entered Jerusalem the whole city was stirred up. People asked, "Who is this?" The crowd answered, "This is Jesus the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee."

Jesus went into the Temple. He saw that people had turned the house of God into a market, buying and selling things right in the place meant for prayer. He turned over the tables of the money changers and said, "My house is supposed to be a house of prayer. You have made it a den of robbers."

Then the blind and the sick came to Him in the Temple and He healed them all. The children in the Temple started shouting, "Hosanna to the Son of David!" The chief priests were angry. Jesus quoted the psalm that said from the mouths of children God has called forth praise.
The children in the Temple saw clearly what the powerful religious leaders refused to see.

devotional.bigIdea: The crowd shouted Hosanna, save us, to a king who arrived on a donkey instead of a warhorse. Jesus came in peace not to conquer armies but to deal with something much deeper. He is always a different kind of king from what we expect.
questions: The crowd was expecting a mighty warrior king on a warhorse. Jesus came on a donkey. Why do you think God so often surprises us with how He does things?
The children in the Temple were shouting praises while the important religious leaders were annoyed. What do you think the children understood that the leaders missed?
The crowd shouted Hosanna which means save us. From what did they need saving? From what do we need saving?

connections.title: Impossible News
connections.why: Zechariah wrote your king comes riding on a donkey five hundred years before Jesus was born, just as Isaiah described the suffering servant.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Hosanna means save us now. Five days after the crowd shouted it they were demanding His death. The journey from this day to Golgotha is the whole story of what kind of king Jesus came to be.

connections.title: The Servant Who Suffers
connections.why: Isaiah said the servant would be gentle and humble. Zechariah said the king would be gentle on a donkey. Both pointed to this same moment.

quiz.question: What animal did Jesus ride into Jerusalem on and why did it matter?
options: A white warhorse, to show He had the power of a king
A camel, because that was the most common animal
He walked on foot because He did not want to draw attention to Himself
A donkey, because a prophet had written five hundred years before that the king would come gently on a donkey
quiz.explanation: Jesus chose a donkey on purpose to fulfil what Zechariah had written. He was announcing that He was the promised king but a gentle king who came in peace.

quiz.question: What did the crowd shout as Jesus rode into Jerusalem?
options: Hosanna to the Son of David, blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord
Long live the king of Israel!
Make way for the prophet from Galilee
The Messiah has come
quiz.explanation: The crowd shouted Hosanna to the Son of David which means save us now, Son of David. They were welcoming a king and calling on a saviour.

quiz.question: Who was still praising Jesus in the Temple when the religious leaders had stopped?
options: The disciples who had been with Him from the start
The children who were shouting Hosanna to the Son of David
The sick people He had healed
The poor people He had helped feed
quiz.explanation: The children in the Temple were still shouting Hosanna. The religious leaders were angry about it. Jesus said God had called forth praise from the mouths of children.

stories.title: The Night Before
stories.hook: The night before Jesus died He had a special dinner with His disciples. Then He got down on His knees and washed their feet.
story: The night before He died Jesus and His twelve disciples shared a special Passover meal together. Jesus knew exactly what was coming. He knew who would betray Him that night. He knew about the cross.
And what did He do at this last dinner? He got up from the table, wrapped a towel around His waist, poured water into a bowl, and got on His knees and began washing His disciples' feet.
Washing feet was the job of the very lowest servant in a house. And the Lord and Teacher was on His knees with a towel.

When Jesus came to Peter, Peter could not stand it. "Lord, you will never wash my feet!" Jesus said, "If I do not wash you, you do not belong with me." Peter said, "Then not just my feet but my hands and head too!"

After He had washed all their feet Jesus sat back down. He said, "Do you understand what I have just done? You call me Teacher and Lord and you are right, that is what I am. But I have just washed your feet. So you should wash each other's feet. I have shown you what to do. Do it."

Then Jesus took bread and thanked God for it and broke it and gave pieces to each of them. He said, "Take and eat. This is my body." Then He took a cup of wine and thanked God and gave it to them. He said, "Drink from it all of you. This is my blood, poured out for many so their sins can be forgiven."
He told them He would not drink from the vine again until He drank it new with them in His Father's kingdom. He was telling them what was coming. He was giving them a meal to remember Him by.
They sang a hymn together. Then they went out into the night.

devotional.bigIdea: The night before He died Jesus got on His knees and washed feet. In His kingdom leading looks like kneeling.
questions: Jesus washed His disciples' feet even though He knew one of them was about to betray Him. What does that tell you about the kind of love Jesus has?
Peter did not want Jesus to wash his feet. It felt too uncomfortable to let the Lord serve him. Do you find it easy or hard to let people help you?
Jesus said I have shown you what to do now go and do it. What would it look like in your life to wash someone's feet, not with actual water but in the way you serve and care for people?

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Jesus said this is my blood poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins. The next day at Golgotha it was no longer bread and wine.

connections.title: The Scarlet Thread
connections.why: The Passover meal Jesus was sharing was the same meal Israel ate the night before leaving Egypt. Jesus was the true Passover lamb.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: After Pentecost the early church devoted themselves to breaking bread together. The last supper became the meal at the heart of every gathering.

quiz.question: What surprising thing did Jesus do at the start of the last supper?
options: He told His disciples exactly what was going to happen the next day
He cooked the whole Passover meal Himself
He asked His disciples to take turns saying what they were thankful for
He got up, wrapped a towel around His waist, and washed all His disciples' feet
quiz.explanation: Jesus the Lord and Teacher got on His knees with a towel and a bowl of water and washed His disciples' feet, the job of the lowest servant.

quiz.question: What did Jesus say the bread represented when He broke it and gave it to them?
options: His body which He was giving for them
The Passover lamb that had been sacrificed in Egypt long ago
The daily bread He had prayed about in the Lord's prayer
The bread that had fed five thousand people on the hillside
quiz.explanation: Jesus took bread, gave thanks, broke it and said take and eat, this is my body. He was showing them what was coming.

quiz.question: After washing their feet what did Jesus say His disciples should do?
options: Go out and tell everyone that Jesus was the Messiah
Wash each other's feet because He had shown them what leadership looks like
Go home and rest because the next day would be very hard
Pray all night and ask God for strength
quiz.explanation: Jesus said I have shown you what to do. Now do it. Wash each other's feet. In His kingdom leading looks like serving.

stories.title: The Darkest Friday
stories.hook: The night before Jesus prayed in a garden. Then He was arrested. By the middle of the next morning He was on a cross. By afternoon He was gone.
story: After the last supper Jesus went with His disciples to a garden called Gethsemane. He went a little further on His own and prayed. He said, "Father, if there is any other way, please let there be another way. But not what I want, what you want." He prayed this three times.

Then Judas arrived, one of Jesus's own disciples, with soldiers and the religious leaders. Judas kissed Jesus on the cheek so they would know which one to arrest. Jesus said, "My friend, do what you came to do." They arrested Jesus. His disciples ran away.

Jesus was taken to the religious leaders who said He had done wrong. They took Him to the Roman governor Pontius Pilate. Pilate could not find anything Jesus had actually done wrong. But the crowd shouted and shouted until Pilate gave in.
Soldiers mocked Jesus and put a crown made of sharp thorns on His head and pretended to bow to Him. Then they led Him out to a hill called Golgotha, which means the place of the skull.

They nailed Him to a cross. Two criminals were crucified alongside Him. The people watching shouted at Him. One of the criminals joined in. The other one said, "We deserve what we are getting. But this man has done nothing wrong." He said to Jesus, "Remember me when you come into your kingdom." Jesus said, "Today you will be with me in paradise."
At twelve noon the sky went dark and stayed dark for three hours. At three in the afternoon Jesus cried out, "It is finished." Then He bowed His head and died.

At that moment the great curtain in the Temple, the thick cloth that separated the holiest room from everything else, was torn in two from top to bottom. Not from the bottom up as a person would tear it. From the top down, the way God tears things. The door to God's presence was now open to everyone.
A soldier standing nearby said, "This man really was the Son of God." They buried Jesus in a borrowed tomb before sunset and rolled a large stone across the entrance.

devotional.bigIdea: Jesus said it is finished. In the language He spoke that was the same word written across a debt that had been paid completely. Everything that stood between us and God was finished at the cross.
questions: Jesus prayed in the garden Father if there is any other way. But He still went through with it. How does it make you feel to know that Jesus chose this for you?
The curtain in the Temple was torn from top to bottom when Jesus died. What do you think that means? What was it telling everyone?

The criminal on the cross next to Jesus had done many wrong things. He just said remember me. And Jesus said today you will be with me in paradise. What does that tell you about what you need to come to Jesus?

connections.title: A Promise as Big as the Stars
connections.why: Jesus carried wood up a hill just as Isaac carried wood up Mount Moriah. Abraham said God will provide the lamb. At Golgotha He did.

connections.title: The Scarlet Thread
connections.why: The red rope that saved Rahab pointed to this. Without the shedding of blood there is no forgiveness. Jesus's blood is what every scarlet thread in the Bible was pointing toward.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: It is finished was not defeat. It was completion. The resurrection three days later proved it.

quiz.question: What did Jesus pray in the garden before He was arrested?
options: Father send angels to protect me from what is coming
Father I am ready, let it happen quickly
Father if there is any other way please let there be another way, but not my will, yours
Father help my disciples to be brave when they take me away
quiz.explanation: Jesus prayed if there is another way let there be another way. But then He said not what I want but what you want. He chose to go through with it.

quiz.question: What happened to the great curtain in the Temple when Jesus died?
options: It fell down because of an earthquake
It caught fire from candles burning inside
It was removed by the priests as a sign of mourning
It was torn in two from top to bottom by God
quiz.explanation: The curtain was torn from top to bottom, not from the bottom up as a person would do it. From the top, the way God tears things. The door to God's presence was now open.

quiz.question: What did the criminal on the cross next to Jesus say to Him?
options: We deserve what we are getting but you did nothing wrong. Remember me when you come into your kingdom
Save yourself and us if you are really the Son of God
I believe in you, now prove it by getting us all down from these crosses
Why did God let this happen to someone as good as you
quiz.explanation: The criminal said we deserve this but you are innocent. Then he said just remember me when you come into your kingdom. Jesus said today you will be with me in paradise.

stories.title: The Empty Tomb
stories.hook: They buried Jesus on Friday. Sunday morning the stone had been moved and the tomb was empty. And then He appeared.
story: Jesus had been buried before sunset on Friday. A large stone had been rolled across the entrance of the tomb. His disciples were hiding, scared and heartbroken. It seemed like the end of everything.

Very early on Sunday morning before the sun was even fully up a woman named Mary Magdalene came to the tomb. She saw that the stone had been rolled away. She ran to find Peter and John. She thought someone had taken the body.

Peter and John ran to the tomb. John got there first and looked in. He saw the linen cloths lying there but did not go in. Peter arrived and went straight in. The cloths were lying there and the cloth that had been around Jesus's head was folded up neatly to one side. Then John went in and saw and believed.
