Mary stayed outside the empty tomb crying. She looked inside and saw two angels in white sitting where the body had been. They asked, "Why are you crying?" She said, "Someone has taken my Lord and I do not know where they have put Him."

Then she turned around and saw someone standing nearby. She thought it was the gardener. She said, "Sir, if you have taken Him, please tell me where He is." The person said one word. "Mary." She knew His voice immediately. She turned and said, "Teacher!"
It was Jesus. He was alive.
That evening the disciples were locked inside a room, scared. Jesus appeared among them. He said, "Peace be with you." He showed them His hands and His side. They were overjoyed.

Thomas was not there. When they told him he said he would not believe unless he could see and touch the wounds himself. A week later Jesus appeared again. He said to Thomas, "Put your finger here. See my hands. Stop doubting and believe." Thomas said, "My Lord and my God."

Later Jesus made breakfast on the beach for seven disciples who had been fishing all night. He told them where to cast the net. They caught so many fish they could not pull the net in. Then they had breakfast with Him on the shore. He appeared to His followers for forty days. He had kept every single thing He had ever said.

devotional.bigIdea: Jesus is alive. Not was. Is. Everything He said He would do He did. The resurrection is not a story. It is the most important thing that ever happened.
questions: Mary did not recognise Jesus until He said her name. Has there ever been a moment where something suddenly made sense and you felt like God was right there? What happened?
Thomas said he would not believe unless he could see for himself. Jesus showed up and gave Thomas exactly what he asked for. What does that tell you about how Jesus treats honest doubt?
Jesus made breakfast on the beach for His disciples. After everything that had happened He cooked them fish. What does that tell you about what kind of person Jesus is?

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Jesus showed the disciples His hands and His side. The resurrection did not erase the cross. The risen Jesus still had the wounds.

connections.title: Come Out
connections.why: Lazarus was raised to die again one day. Jesus rose never to die again. The raising of Lazarus was a preview. The resurrection of Jesus is the main event.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: Jesus appeared for forty days teaching about the kingdom of God preparing His disciples for Pentecost and everything that came next.

quiz.question: How did Mary recognise that the person she thought was the gardener was actually Jesus?
options: She saw His face clearly in the morning light
He said her name and she knew His voice
He showed her His hands where the nails had been
She saw Him walk through the solid wall of the tomb
quiz.explanation: Jesus said one word. Mary. She heard His voice and knew immediately. She turned and said Teacher.

quiz.question: What did Thomas say about the resurrection when the other disciples told him Jesus was alive?
options: He was immediately full of joy and ran to tell everyone else
He said he had already seen Jesus Himself the night before
He said he would not believe unless he could see and touch the wounds himself
He said he believed them because they were his friends and would never lie to him
quiz.explanation: Thomas said unless I see the nail marks and touch the wounds I will not believe. Jesus appeared a week later and gave Thomas exactly what he had asked for.

quiz.question: What did Jesus make for His disciples on the beach after the resurrection?
options: A feast of bread and wine like the last supper
Nothing, He just appeared and talked with them
He brought food from a nearby village for them
Breakfast of bread and fish on a charcoal fire
quiz.explanation: Jesus cooked bread and fish on a charcoal fire on the beach. After everything that had happened He made them breakfast. That is what Jesus is like.

stories.title: The Stranger on the Road
stories.hook: Two disciples were walking home after the crucifixion thinking everything was over. A stranger walked with them the whole seven miles. They did not recognise Him.
story: On the same day the tomb was found empty two of Jesus's followers were walking away from Jerusalem to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles away. They walked slowly talking about everything that had happened. Their faces were sad.
As they walked Jesus came up and walked alongside them. But they were not able to recognise who He was.

He asked, "What are you talking about?" They stopped walking. They looked at Him with sad faces. One of them said, "Are you the only person in Jerusalem who has not heard what happened these last few days?"

Jesus said, "What things?" They told Him everything. About Jesus of Nazareth who they had hoped was the one who would rescue Israel. About the crucifixion. About the empty tomb that morning and the women who had seen angels.

Then Jesus said to them, "Did the prophets not say that the Messiah had to suffer all these things before entering His glory?" And starting with Moses and going through all the prophets He explained everything in all the Scriptures that was about Him. For seven miles He opened the whole Bible to them.
They reached the village. Jesus acted as if He was going further. They said, "Please stay with us, it is getting late." So He went in to stay with them.
At the table He took bread, said thank you to God, broke it, and gave it to them. In that moment their eyes were opened and they recognised Him. And then He disappeared.
They said to each other, "Were our hearts not burning inside us while He talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?" They had felt it but not understood it.
They got up at once and walked the seven miles back to Jerusalem in the dark to tell the others. They had seen the Lord.

devotional.bigIdea: Jesus walked with two sad people for seven miles and they did not know it was Him. He is with us even when we cannot see Him. And sometimes we feel His presence before we understand it.
questions: The two disciples said their hearts were burning while Jesus talked with them even though they did not know it was Him yet. Have you ever felt something warm and alive when you heard about Jesus or read the Bible?
They recognised Jesus in the breaking of the bread, the same thing He had done at the last supper. Where do you most feel close to God?
They got up and walked seven miles in the dark to tell their friends. They could not keep it to themselves. When you hear something really good what do you do?

connections.title: The Night Before
connections.why: Jesus took bread, gave thanks, broke it, and gave it, the exact same gestures as the last supper. They recognised Him in the familiar thing He had already done.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: This story happened on the same day the tomb was found empty. Jesus met these two disciples walking away and turned them around.

connections.title: The Servant Who Suffers
connections.why: Jesus explained everything in the Scriptures about Himself starting from Moses. Isaiah's description of the suffering servant was in that conversation.

quiz.question: Why were the two disciples walking away from Jerusalem?
options: They were going home sad because they thought everything was over after the crucifixion
They had heard Jesus was alive and were going to tell people in Emmaus
They were going to Emmaus to collect food for the other disciples
They were trying to escape because they were afraid the religious leaders would arrest them too
quiz.explanation: They were walking away from Jerusalem with sad faces. They had hoped Jesus would rescue Israel. Now He was dead and it seemed like the end.

quiz.question: When did the two disciples finally recognise Jesus?
options: When He explained the Scriptures to them on the road
When He took bread, gave thanks, broke it, and gave it to them at the table
When He asked them what they were talking about
When He stayed the night at their home in Emmaus
quiz.explanation: Their eyes were opened at the breaking of the bread, the same gesture Jesus had made at the last supper. Then He disappeared.

quiz.question: What did the two disciples do immediately after recognising Jesus?
options: They sat quietly and tried to understand what had happened
They went to bed and waited until morning to tell anyone
They got up at once and walked seven miles back to Jerusalem in the dark to tell the others
They stayed in Emmaus hoping Jesus would appear again the next morning
quiz.explanation: They got up at once and walked seven miles back in the dark. They could not wait until morning. They had seen the Lord.

stories.title: The Day Everything Changed
stories.hook: Fifty days after Jesus died and rose again His followers were all together in one room when wind filled the house and fire appeared above their heads.
story: Before Jesus went back to heaven He told His disciples to wait in Jerusalem. He had promised to send them a helper. "You will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you," He said. "And you will be my witnesses everywhere, to the ends of the earth." Then He was taken up into the sky and a cloud hid Him from their sight.
About a hundred and twenty of His followers stayed in Jerusalem and waited. They prayed together every day.

Ten days later was the day of Pentecost, a Jewish celebration. The disciples were all together in one place when something amazing happened. Suddenly a sound came from heaven like a very strong wind blowing and it filled the whole building. Then what looked like flames of fire appeared and separated and one sat on each person there. And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit.

Jerusalem was full of Jewish people who had come from all over the world for the celebration. They all came running when they heard the noise. And then something extraordinary happened. Each person in the crowd heard the disciples speaking in that person's own home language. People from different countries all heard the wonders of God in their own language.

Peter stood up in front of the enormous crowd. This was the same Peter who had said three times he did not know Jesus on the night of the arrest. Now he stood up boldly and told the whole story. Jesus of Nazareth, His life, His death, His resurrection. He said, "God has made this Jesus, whom you killed, both Lord and Messiah."

The crowd felt it to their core. They said, "Brothers, what should we do?" Peter said, "Turn from your wrong ways. Be baptised in the name of Jesus Christ so your sins will be forgiven. And you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit."
That day three thousand people believed and were baptised. Three thousand. In one afternoon.
What God had broken apart at Babel when He confused the languages His Spirit put back together at Pentecost. Every language heard the same wonderful news. The church had begun.

devotional.bigIdea: At Babel God confused the languages and scattered people. At Pentecost the Spirit let every language hear the same good news and brought people together. What sin breaks apart God puts back together.
questions: The disciples waited ten days in Jerusalem before the Holy Spirit came. They did not know how long they would wait. Have you ever had to wait for something without knowing when it was coming?
Peter had been too scared to admit he knew Jesus when Jesus was arrested. But on the day of Pentecost he stood in front of thousands of people and preached boldly. What changed him?
Three thousand people believed and joined in one afternoon. What do you think it must have been like to be there that day?

connections.title: The Tower of Babel
connections.why: At Babel God confused languages and scattered people. At Pentecost the Spirit enabled every language to hear the same message. What was broken was put back together.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: Peter says Jesus has been lifted up and has received the Holy Spirit from the Father and poured it out. Pentecost is the direct result of the resurrection.

connections.title: A Promise as Big as the Stars
connections.why: Peter says the promise is for you and your children and for all who are far off. The promise to Abraham that all nations would be blessed was being fulfilled in real time.

quiz.question: What happened when the Holy Spirit came on the disciples?
options: A bright light flashed and they all fell to the ground
An angel appeared and told them what to do next
They all fell asleep and had the same dream at the same time
A sound like strong wind filled the building, something like flames sat on each person, and they spoke in other languages
quiz.explanation: A sound like rushing wind filled the house, flames appeared on each person, and they were filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke in other languages.

quiz.question: Why were the people from other countries so amazed?
options: Because each person heard the disciples speaking in their own home language
Because they had never heard about Jesus before
Because the disciples were performing miracles and healing people
Because a great light was shining from the building they could see from far away
quiz.explanation: People who had come from all over the world each heard the good news about God in their own native language. It was like Babel being undone.

quiz.question: How many people believed and were baptised on the day of Pentecost?
options: About three hundred
About three thousand
About thirty thousand
About three hundred thousand
quiz.explanation: About three thousand people believed and were baptised that day. The church went from about a hundred and twenty people to over three thousand in one afternoon.

stories.title: Silver and Gold I Do Not Have
stories.hook: A man who could never walk sat begging at the temple. He asked Peter for some coins, but Peter gave him something much better!
story: One afternoon, Peter and John were on their way to the temple to pray.
At the temple gate sat a man who had never been able to walk in his whole life.
Every single day, his friends carried him to that gate, so he could beg for coins from the people going in to worship.
When the man saw Peter and John coming, he asked them for some money, just as he asked everyone.
Peter looked straight at the man. "Look at us," he said. The man looked up, hoping to get a coin or two.
But Peter said something surprising. "I do not have any silver or gold," he said. "But what I do have, I will give to you!"
"In the name of Jesus Christ," said Peter, "get up and walk!"
Then Peter took the man by his right hand and helped him up.
And instantly, the man's feet and ankles became strong! For the very first time in his life, he was standing up!
The man jumped to his feet, and then he began to walk. Then he started leaping and jumping for joy!
He went right into the temple with Peter and John, walking and jumping and praising God as loud as he could.
Everyone who saw him was filled with wonder, because they knew he was the same man who used to sit and beg at the gate.
The man who used to sit and beg in that same spot every single day was now walking and leaping for the very first time in his life. What a wonderful gift God had given him!
It was not Peter's own power that healed the man. It was the wonderful power of Jesus' name. Jesus was still helping and healing people, now through his followers.

devotional.bigIdea: Peter had no money, but he gave what he had, the power of Jesus' name, and the man could walk! We all have something to give, and Jesus' name is more powerful than gold.
questions: The man got something better than money. What good things has God given you?
Peter gave what he had. What could you give to help someone?
The man praised God for his new legs. What can you praise God for?

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: Filled with the Spirit at Pentecost, Peter now does in Jesus' name the very kind of miracle Jesus once did.

connections.title: Through the Roof
connections.why: Just as Jesus made a paralyzed man walk, Peter, in Jesus' name, lifts up a man who had never walked in his life.

connections.title: The Ethiopian's Chariot
connections.why: This is the early church beginning to spread the good news of Jesus, just as he told them to, starting in Jerusalem.

quiz.question: What could the man at the gate not do?
options: Walk
See
Hear

quiz.question: What did Peter give him instead of money?
options: Healing in Jesus' name
Gold
Bread

quiz.question: What did the man do when his legs were strong?
options: Jumped and praised God
Sat back down
Ran away

stories.title: The First Martyr
stories.hook: Stephen stood in front of the same people who had killed Jesus and told them the truth about everything. It was the last thing he ever said.
story: The early church was growing quickly in Jerusalem. A man named Stephen was chosen to help look after practical things so the leaders could focus on teaching and prayer. But Stephen also spoke with such wisdom and the power of God's Spirit that people could not argue against what he said.

Some people were angry with Stephen and brought him before the religious council, the same group that had sent Jesus to the cross. They brought people who told lies about him. But everyone on the council looked at Stephen and saw his face looked like the face of an angel.

The high priest asked if the things being said about him were true. Stephen answered with a very long speech. He told the whole story of Israel from Abraham to Moses to the Temple to Jesus. At every point in the story he showed how the people had turned away from the messengers God sent them.

He ended by saying, "You are just like your ancestors. You always fight against the Holy Spirit. Which prophet did your ancestors not treat badly? They killed the ones who said the Messiah was coming. And now you have betrayed and killed the Messiah Himself."
The council members were furious. They ground their teeth with rage.
But Stephen, full of the Holy Spirit, looked up into heaven and said, "I can see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God."

They covered their ears and rushed at him. They dragged him out of the city and started throwing stones at him. A young man named Saul stood nearby watching over the coats of those throwing the stones.
While they were throwing stones at him Stephen prayed. He said, "Lord Jesus, receive my spirit." Then he knelt down and cried out loudly, "Lord, do not count this sin against them." And he died.
He died forgiving the people who were killing him. Just like Jesus had from the cross.

devotional.bigIdea: Stephen asked God to forgive the people who were killing him while they were still throwing stones. That kind of forgiveness is only possible when you have really met Jesus.
questions: Stephen asked God not to count the sin of his killers against them while they were still hurting him. That is very hard. What do you think gave him the strength to pray that?
Stephen saw heaven open while he was being hurt. What do you think it meant for him to see that in that terrible moment?
A young man named Saul stood watching and approving. We will hear about him again soon. Why do you think this story mentions him?

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Jesus said from the cross Father forgive them. Stephen said Lord do not count this against them. Stephen did not just preach about the cross. He lived it.

connections.title: The Man Who Hunted Christians
connections.why: Saul stood watching Stephen's death. Years later Paul said that day never left him. The first martyr planted something in the heart of the greatest missionary.

connections.title: Singing at Midnight
connections.why: Stephen saw heaven open while being stoned. Paul sang hymns while chained in prison. Both found a peace that made no sense given what was happening to them.

quiz.question: What did everyone on the religious council see when they looked at Stephen as he stood before them?
options: Anger that showed he was dangerous and should be stopped
Fear in his eyes even though he was trying to look brave
His face looked like the face of an angel
Tears running down his face because he was very scared
quiz.explanation: The whole council looked at Stephen and his face was like the face of an angel. Whatever was coming he was not afraid.

quiz.question: What did Stephen see when he looked up to heaven just before the crowd attacked him?
options: An angel coming down to rescue him
A bright light that blinded the people about to hurt him
His friends and family waiting for him
Heaven open and Jesus standing at the right hand of God
quiz.explanation: Stephen said I can see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God. Knowing where he was going made all the difference.

quiz.question: What were Stephen's last words?
options: Lord Jesus receive my spirit, and Lord do not count this sin against them
I forgive you all and I pray God opens your eyes to the truth
The Messiah is alive and you will all see Him one day
I am going to be with Jesus and nothing can stop that now
quiz.explanation: Stephen prayed Lord Jesus receive my spirit. Then he cried out Lord do not count this against them. He forgave while it was still happening.

stories.title: The Ethiopian's Chariot
stories.hook: God sent Philip to a quiet desert road to meet a man in a chariot who needed help understanding God's words.
story: One day, an angel told a man named Philip to go to a lonely desert road. Philip did not know why, but he obeyed God and went.
Along that road came a chariot. Inside it rode an important official from a faraway land called Ethiopia.
This man was in charge of all the queen's treasure. He had traveled a very long way to worship God in Jerusalem.
Now, on his way home, he was reading out loud from God's word, the scroll of the prophet Isaiah.
God's Spirit said to Philip, "Go over and walk near that chariot." So Philip ran up to it.
As he got close, Philip heard the man reading. "Do you understand what you are reading?" Philip asked him.
"How can I understand," said the man, "unless someone explains it to me?" And he kindly invited Philip to climb up and sit beside him.
The man pointed to the words and asked, "Who is the prophet talking about here?"
So Philip, beginning right there with those very words, told the man all about the good news of Jesus.
He explained that Isaiah had written about Jesus, the one who gave his life to save us, hundreds of years before Jesus was even born!
As they rode along together, they came to some water. "Look, here is water!" said the man. "What is stopping me from being baptized?"
So they stopped the chariot, and Philip baptized him right there, to show that the man now belonged to Jesus.
Then the happy man went on his way home, ready to tell others. God had sent Philip to a lonely road for one searching heart, because the good news of Jesus is for everyone, everywhere.

devotional.bigIdea: God sent Philip to help one man understand his word and learn about Jesus. The good news of Jesus is for everyone, in every land!
questions: Philip obeyed God even before he knew why. When is it hard to obey?
Someone helped the man understand the Bible. Who helps you learn about God?
The man told others about Jesus. Who could you tell about Jesus?

connections.title: The Servant Who Suffers
connections.why: The Ethiopian is reading the very words of Isaiah about the suffering servant, and Philip explains that they are about Jesus.

connections.title: Jesus Is Baptized
connections.why: As soon as the Ethiopian believes, he wants to be baptized, just as Jesus was, to show he now belongs to God.

connections.title: Silver and Gold I Do Not Have
connections.why: The good news of Jesus keeps spreading outward, now reaching a traveler from a faraway land.

quiz.question: Where did God send Philip?
options: To a desert road
To a city
To a boat

quiz.question: What was the man in the chariot reading?
options: God's word about Jesus
A storybook
A map

quiz.question: What did the man do after he believed?
options: He was baptized
He went to sleep
He drove away fast

stories.title: The Man Who Hunted Christians
stories.hook: Saul was the most dangerous enemy the early church had. He hunted Christians and put them in prison. Then a blinding light knocked him off his feet and God called his name.
story: After Stephen died a great persecution began against the followers of Jesus in Jerusalem. Many of them had to leave the city and scatter to other places. And the man leading the charge against them was Saul.

Saul was a Pharisee, a very religious man who studied the law more carefully than almost anyone. He was completely convinced that the followers of Jesus were wrong and dangerous. He went from house to house dragging men and women out and putting them in prison.

He was not done. He got letters to take to synagogues in Damascus, a city about a hundred and fifty miles away. If he found any followers of Jesus there he would bring them back to Jerusalem as prisoners.
He set out with a group of men. He was on his way to arrest more Christians.

And then on the road to Damascus a light from heaven suddenly flashed around him. He fell to the ground. He heard a voice saying, "Saul, Saul, why are you hurting me?" Saul said, "Who are you, Lord?" The voice said, "I am Jesus, whom you are hurting. Get up now and go into the city. You will be told what to do."

The men travelling with Saul stood there speechless. They heard a sound but could not see anyone. Saul got up from the ground. When he opened his eyes he could see nothing. He was blind. They led him by the hand into Damascus.
For three days Saul sat there blind and did not eat or drink.

A follower of Jesus in Damascus named Ananias had a vision. God told him to go and find Saul. Ananias was nervous. He said, "Lord, I have heard how much harm this man has done to your people." God said, "Go. This man is my chosen instrument. He is going to carry my name to nations and kings and to the people of Israel."

Ananias went. He put his hands on Saul and said, "Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus who appeared to you on the road has sent me so you can see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit." Immediately something like scales fell from Saul's eyes and he could see. He got up and was baptised.
The greatest enemy of the church became its greatest missionary.

devotional.bigIdea: Saul was so convinced he was doing the right thing that he hunted Christians. And God chose him on purpose to show what grace can do with anyone who is willing to be changed.
questions: Saul was completely sure he was right about everything. Then he met Jesus and found out he was completely wrong about the most important thing. Has there ever been a time you were sure about something and then found out differently?
Ananias was scared to go to Saul because Saul had been hurting Jesus's followers. But God said go. Have you ever had to do something scary because you believed God was asking you to?
God said Saul was His chosen instrument. He said that about the man who had been hunting down Christians. Does anything seem too wrong or too messed up for God to use?

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: The persecution Saul launched scattered the believers and carried the gospel to Judea and Samaria and beyond. He tried to stop the church and accidentally spread it.

connections.title: The Darkest Friday
connections.why: Paul said Jesus came to save sinners of whom I am the worst. The cross was for the worst cases most of all.

connections.title: Singing at Midnight
connections.why: Jesus told Ananias that Paul would suffer for His name. From the moment of his change Paul knew the cost. Singing at midnight was the life he had been told to expect.

quiz.question: What was Saul doing when the light from heaven knocked him down?
options: He was praying in a synagogue in Damascus
He was travelling to Damascus to arrest followers of Jesus and bring them back as prisoners
He was debating with the followers of Jesus in Jerusalem
He was reading the Scriptures in the Temple
quiz.explanation: Saul was on his way to Damascus to hunt down more followers of Jesus and bring them back as prisoners. He was the most dangerous enemy the church had.

quiz.question: What did the voice from heaven say to Saul?
options: Stop what you are doing and go back to Jerusalem immediately
I have chosen you to be the greatest teacher Israel has ever had
Saul Saul, why are you hurting me? I am Jesus, whom you are hurting
Turn around and go home, your time for this work is not yet
quiz.explanation: Jesus said Saul Saul why are you hurting me. To hurt the followers of Jesus was to hurt Jesus Himself.

quiz.question: What happened to Saul after Jesus spoke to him on the road?
options: He immediately started preaching about Jesus to the men around him
He saw a great vision of heaven and all its wonders
He went back to Jerusalem to tell the high priest what had happened
He was struck blind and had to be led by hand into Damascus where he sat for three days without eating or drinking
quiz.explanation: Saul was struck blind. They led him by the hand to Damascus where he sat for three days without sight, food, or water, waiting for what came next.

stories.title: Good News for Everyone
stories.hook: God taught Peter a wonderful surprise: the good news about Jesus is not just for some people. It is for everyone in the whole world!
story: In a city called Caesarea lived a Roman soldier named Cornelius. He was not one of God's people Israel, but he loved God, prayed often, and was kind to the poor.
One day, an angel appeared to Cornelius. "God has heard your prayers," the angel said. "Send for a man named Peter." So Cornelius sent messengers to go and find him.
Meanwhile, Peter was up on a rooftop, praying. He got hungry, and while he waited for food, he had a strange vision, like a dream.
Peter saw a huge sheet come down from heaven, full of all kinds of animals, even ones that God's people were not supposed to eat.
A voice said, "Get up, Peter. Eat." But Peter said, "No, Lord! I have never eaten anything like that."
Then the voice said, "Do not call anything bad that God has made good." This happened three times, and Peter wondered what it could mean.
Just then, Cornelius's messengers arrived at the door. And God's Spirit told Peter to go with them.
Now in those days, Jewish people did not usually go into the homes of people from other nations. But Peter was starting to understand his vision.
When Peter arrived at the house, it was full of Cornelius's family and friends, all waiting to hear him.
"Now I understand," Peter said. "God does not have favorites. He welcomes people from every single nation who love him!"
Then Peter told them all the good news about Jesus, how he died and rose again to save anyone who believes.
And while Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit came down on everyone listening, just like at Pentecost! People from another nation had received the very same gift.
So they were all baptized. The good news of Jesus had burst past every wall and every border. It really is for the whole wide world, including you!

devotional.bigIdea: Peter learned that God does not pick favorites. The good news of Jesus is for every person, in every land. God's love is for everyone, including you!
questions: God loves people from every land. Why is that wonderful news?
Is there someone you find hard to be kind to? God loves them too.
How does it feel to know God's love is for you, no matter what?

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: The same Holy Spirit who came at Pentecost now falls on people from another nation, showing the good news is for all.

connections.title: The Man Who Ran From God
connections.why: Like Jonah learning God loved the people of Nineveh, Peter learns that God welcomes people from every nation.

connections.title: The Ethiopian's Chariot
connections.why: Step by step, God is showing his church that the good news of Jesus is meant for the whole world, not one nation only.

quiz.question: Who was Cornelius?
options: A Roman soldier who loved God
A king
A baker

quiz.question: What did God teach Peter?
options: God loves people from every nation
Only some people matter
To stay home

quiz.question: Who is the good news of Jesus for?
options: Everyone, everywhere
Only a few people
Only grown-ups

stories.title: Peter's Prison Escape
stories.hook: Peter was locked in prison with chains and guards. His friends prayed all night, and God sent an angel to set him free!
story: A wicked king named Herod was hurting the church, and he threw Peter into prison.
Peter was guarded by soldiers on every side. He was even chained between two of them, with more guards at the door. There seemed to be no way out at all.
But Peter's friends in the church did the most powerful thing they could do. They gathered together and prayed for Peter, all through the night.
On the very night before his trial, Peter was sound asleep, chained between the two soldiers.
Suddenly, an angel of the Lord appeared, and a bright light filled the whole prison cell!
The angel tapped Peter on the side and woke him up. "Quick, get up!" said the angel. And the chains fell right off of Peter's wrists!
"Get dressed and follow me," said the angel. Peter did, but he was so amazed that he thought he must be dreaming.
They walked right past the first group of guards, and then the second group. Nobody stopped them!
When they came to the big iron gate that led into the city, it swung open all by itself! They walked through and down the street, and then the angel suddenly disappeared.
Only then did Peter understand. "Now I know for sure," he said, "that God sent his angel and rescued me!"
Peter hurried to the house where all his friends were still praying for him. He knocked on the door.
A servant girl named Rhoda heard his voice. She was so overjoyed that she ran to tell everyone, but she forgot to open the door, and left Peter knocking outside!
At first, no one believed her. But Peter kept knocking, and when they finally opened the door, they were amazed. God had answered their prayers while they were still praying them!

devotional.bigIdea: Peter's friends prayed all night, and God sent an angel to rescue him. God hears our prayers and answers them, sometimes in amazing ways!
questions: Peter's friends kept praying even when it looked hopeless. Why is it good to keep praying?
God surprised everyone with his answer. Has God ever surprised you?
What is something you can pray about and trust God with?

connections.title: Singing at Midnight
connections.why: Like Paul and Silas later, Peter is locked in prison, and God steps in to set his servant free.

connections.title: The Lion's Den
connections.why: Just as God shut the lions' mouths for Daniel, he sends an angel to rescue Peter from the hand of a wicked king.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: The praying church that begs God for Peter is the same Spirit-filled community born at Pentecost.

quiz.question: What did Peter's friends do for him?
options: Prayed all night
Forgot him
Ran away

quiz.question: How did Peter get free?
options: An angel helped him and the chains fell off
He flew
He dug a hole

quiz.question: Why did Rhoda leave Peter knocking?
options: She was too happy and ran to tell everyone
She was mad
She was asleep

stories.title: Singing at Midnight
stories.hook: Paul and his friend Silas were beaten and thrown in the deepest part of a prison. Then they started singing.
story: Paul was travelling and teaching about Jesus in a city called Philippi. He and his friend Silas helped a slave girl who had been used to tell people's fortunes. The men who owned the girl were furious because they had been making money from her. They had Paul and Silas dragged before the city leaders and accused of causing trouble.

The crowd joined in attacking them. The leaders ordered Paul and Silas to be beaten with rods. Then they were thrown into the most secure part of the prison and their feet were locked in wooden stocks so they could not move.

It was midnight. Paul and Silas had been beaten. Their backs hurt. They were in the deepest darkest cell of the prison with their feet locked. And they were praying out loud and singing songs to God. The other prisoners were listening.
Suddenly there was a violent earthquake. It shook the whole prison to its foundations. Every door flew open. Every chain fell off every prisoner.

The jailer woke up. He saw all the doors open. He thought all the prisoners had escaped and that he would be killed for letting them go. He drew his sword to take his own life. But Paul shouted, "Do not hurt yourself! We are all still here!"

The jailer called for lights and ran in trembling. He fell down in front of Paul and Silas. He brought them out and asked, "Sirs, what must I do to be saved?" They said, "Believe in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved, you and your family." They told him and his whole household the good news about Jesus.

That same night the jailer washed their wounds. And the jailer and his whole household were baptised. He brought Paul and Silas into his home and fed them and was filled with joy because he had come to believe in God.

The next morning the city leaders sent word to release Paul and Silas. Paul said, "They beat us without a proper trial and we are Roman citizens. And now they want to quietly let us go? No. Let them come here themselves." The leaders were frightened when they heard Paul and Silas were Roman citizens. They came and apologised and escorted them out.

devotional.bigIdea: Paul and Silas sang at midnight in a prison with sore backs and locked feet. Not because things were good. Because God was worth singing to in the dark just as much as in the light. Maybe more.
questions: Paul and Silas sang praises to God at midnight after being beaten and thrown in prison. What do you think made that possible? How do you think they felt while they were singing?
The other prisoners were listening. The jailer was watching. Their faith in the worst moment reached people that nothing else could. Who is watching how you handle your hard moments?
The jailer asked what must I do to be saved right in the middle of the worst night of his life. Why do you think people sometimes come to God in the hardest moments?

connections.title: The Man Who Hunted Christians
connections.why: Jesus told Ananias that Paul would suffer for His name. Midnight in a Philippian prison is that promise being kept. Paul knew it was coming and he sang anyway.

connections.title: The Lion's Den
connections.why: An earthquake opened the prison doors just as an angel shut the mouths of the lions. Both Daniel and Paul were imprisoned for their faith and both rescues led to their captors believing in God.

connections.title: The Upside-Down Kingdom
connections.why: Jesus said blessed are those who are treated unfairly for doing right, rejoice and be glad. Paul and Silas were doing exactly that. Singing it.

quiz.question: What were Paul and Silas doing at midnight in prison?
options: They were praying and singing songs to God while the other prisoners listened
They were trying to find a way to escape through the window
They were asleep because they were so exhausted from the beatings
They were whispering quietly so the guards would not hear them
quiz.explanation: At midnight, beaten, with feet locked in stocks, Paul and Silas were praying out loud and singing songs. The other prisoners were listening.

quiz.question: What did the jailer do after he and his family heard about Jesus and believed?
options: He promised to tell the city leaders that Paul and Silas were innocent
He washed their wounds and had his whole family baptised and brought Paul and Silas into his home and fed them
He opened the prison and let everyone go free
He gave Paul and Silas money to help them travel to the next city
quiz.explanation: The jailer washed their wounds, had his whole family baptised, brought them into his home, and fed them. He was full of joy. All in one night.

quiz.question: Why was Paul not willing to just quietly leave the prison the next morning?
options: Because he wanted to preach to all the prisoners before he left
Because he was still in too much pain to walk
Because they had beaten him and Silas without a proper trial and they were Roman citizens and he wanted the leaders to come and apologise
Because the jailer asked him to stay and teach his family more about Jesus
quiz.explanation: Paul said they beat Roman citizens without a trial and threw them in prison and now they want to quietly let them go. No. The leaders had to come and apologise in person.

stories.title: The Purple Merchant
stories.hook: Paul sailed to Europe because of a dream about a man asking for help. The first person he found there was a woman by a river. She changed history.
story: Paul had a vision one night. He saw a man from Macedonia standing and saying, "Come over to Macedonia and help us." Paul immediately understood this was God calling him to take the good news about Jesus to Europe for the very first time.
Paul and his friends sailed across the sea and arrived in Philippi, the most important city in that part of Macedonia. It was a Roman colony.

On the Sabbath day Paul went outside the city gate to a river looking for a place to pray. There was no synagogue in Philippi which meant there were not many Jewish men in the city. But there was a group of women gathered by the river.

Paul sat down and started talking with them. One of the women was named Lydia. She had come from the city of Thyatira. She was a dealer in purple cloth. Purple cloth was one of the most valuable and expensive things in the whole ancient world made from tiny sea creatures one drop at a time. Only very wealthy people could afford purple. Lydia was a successful and important businesswoman.
Lydia was already someone who worshipped God. She had been seeking Him. And as she listened to Paul God opened her heart to take in what Paul was saying.

Lydia believed. She and her whole household were baptised. Then she asked Paul and his friends to stay at her house. She said, "If you believe I am a true follower of the Lord come and stay at my home." She kept asking until they agreed.
The very first church in Europe met in Lydia's house. The good news about Jesus had crossed from Asia into Europe through the faith of one businesswoman who had been searching for God by a river.

Paul's vision had been about a man from Macedonia. What he found was a woman. God had been more specific about the continent than the details. He just said come and help. And when Paul came and helped everything started from an unexpected place with an unexpected person.
It always does.

devotional.bigIdea: God opened Lydia's heart to hear the good news. She had been seeking and God met her by a river. The gospel reached a whole continent through one woman who was already looking for God.
questions: Paul had a vision of a man from Macedonia asking for help. But the first person he actually helped was a woman. God had said come and help without giving all the details. What does that tell you about following God?
The Bible says God opened Lydia's heart to pay attention to what Paul was saying. She was already looking for God. What does it mean for God to open your heart?
The first church in Europe started in a businesswoman's house not in a great building. Where do you think church can happen?

connections.title: The Woman at the Well
connections.why: God opened Lydia's heart to pay attention to Paul. Jesus crossed into Samaria to find one woman at a well. In both stories God is actively seeking the person before the conversation begins.

connections.title: Singing at Midnight
connections.why: Lydia's house became Paul's base in Philippi. The singing at midnight in the Philippian jail happened in the same city. Paul's letter to Philippi was written to the church that began in Lydia's living room.

connections.title: The Day Everything Changed
connections.why: At Pentecost the gospel went from a room in Jerusalem to thousands. Through Lydia the gospel crossed into Europe. The Spirit kept pushing outward exactly as Jesus had promised.

quiz.question: What was Lydia's job?
options: She baked and sold bread in the city market
She looked after the sick people in the city
She kept an inn where travellers could stay
She was a dealer in purple cloth, one of the most expensive things in the world
quiz.explanation: Lydia was a dealer in purple cloth from the city of Thyatira. Purple was incredibly expensive and only the very wealthy could afford it. She was a successful businesswoman.

quiz.question: What did God do while Lydia was listening to Paul?
options: He opened her heart to pay careful attention to what Paul was saying
He gave her a vision of heaven to confirm everything Paul was saying
He sent an angel to stand beside Paul as a sign his message was true
He caused a miracle to happen so she would know the message was real
quiz.explanation: The Bible says the Lord opened her heart to pay attention to what Paul was saying. She was already seeking God. God opened what was already searching.

quiz.question: What did Lydia do after she and her household were baptised?
options: She went back to Thyatira to tell her family about Jesus
She asked Paul and his friends to come and stay at her house and they did
She gave all her money to Paul to help him travel to more cities
She started going from house to house in Philippi telling her neighbours about Jesus
quiz.explanation: Lydia asked Paul to stay at her home and kept asking until he agreed. Her house became the gathering place for the first church in Europe.

stories.title: The Unknown God
stories.hook: Paul visited the big city of Athens and saw statues of pretend gods everywhere. So he told the people about the one true God.
story: Paul came to a famous city called Athens. It was full of clever thinkers and grand, beautiful buildings.
But as Paul walked around the city, his heart felt troubled. Everywhere he looked, there were idols, statues of pretend gods, on every corner.
Paul began to tell people in the marketplace the good news about Jesus.
The famous thinkers of Athens were curious about him. "This man has some new ideas," they said. "Let us hear what he has to say."
So they brought Paul to a special hill where people gathered to talk about big questions.
Paul stood up and said, "People of Athens! I can see that you are very religious. As I walked around, I even found an altar with these words on it: TO A GOD WE DO NOT KNOW."
"You worship a god you do not even know," said Paul. "Well, let me tell you who he is!"
"The God who made the whole world and everything in it," Paul said, "is the Lord of heaven and earth. He does not live inside statues built by people."
"He does not need anything from us," Paul went on, "because he is the one who gives life and breath to every living thing. And he is not far away from any one of us."
Paul told them that the real God is alive. He is not like gold or stone shaped by human hands. He made us, and we are his children!
Then Paul told them the most important news of all. God now asks everyone, everywhere, to turn to him. And God proved that Jesus is Lord by raising him from the dead.
When some of the people heard about someone rising from the dead, they laughed and sneered. But others said, "We want to hear more about this!"
And some of them believed. Even in a city full of pretend gods, the truth about the one true God found its way into hearts that were ready to listen.

devotional.bigIdea: The people of Athens worshiped statues, even a god they did not know. Paul told them the truth: the real God made everything and is close to us. We can really know God through Jesus.
questions: The people worshiped statues. What is the difference between a statue and the real, living God?
Paul said God is close to us. How does that make you feel?
Some people wanted to hear more about God. Do you like learning about God?

connections.title: In the Beginning
connections.why: Paul tells the Athenians about the God who made the world and everything in it, the Creator of Genesis.

connections.title: The Golden Calf
connections.why: Athens was full of idols, but Paul explains that the living God is not a statue made by human hands.

connections.title: The Empty Tomb
connections.why: Paul points the Athenians to Jesus, proven to be Lord of all by being raised from the dead.

quiz.question: What did Paul see all over Athens?
options: Statues of pretend gods
Lots of trees
Many boats

quiz.question: What did Paul tell the people about the real God?
options: He made the whole world and is close to us
He lives in a statue
He is far away

quiz.question: What good news did Paul share about Jesus?
options: God raised him from the dead
He was rich
He liked Athens

stories.title: The Boy Who Fell Asleep
stories.hook: Paul preached a very long sermon late at night. A boy named Eutychus was sitting in a window, and he got sleepier and sleepier.
story: Paul was visiting his friends, the believers, in a town called Troas.
Because Paul was leaving the very next morning, he had so much that he wanted to say to them.
So everyone gathered together in an upstairs room. The room was warm, and it was lit by many flickering lamps.
Paul began to teach, and he talked on and on, late into the night.
Now, sitting in an open window was a young man named Eutychus.
As Paul kept on preaching, and the hour grew later and later, poor Eutychus began to get very, very sleepy.
His eyes grew heavy, and his head started to nod. And then, sound asleep, Eutychus tumbled right out of the window!
It was a long, long way down to the ground below. When the people rushed down and picked him up, the young man had died.
What a terrible, sad moment! The happy gathering had suddenly turned to crying. But Paul hurried down the stairs to the boy.
Paul bent down and threw his arms around the young man, holding him close.
Then Paul stood up and said, "Do not be afraid! He is alive!"
And it was true! The young man was alive and well again! The people could hardly believe their happy relief.
Then they all went back upstairs, shared a meal together, and Paul kept right on talking until the sun came up!
Everyone had been so worried and sad, and then all at once their tears turned into smiles and happy hugs. What a wonderful surprise God had given them that night!
God turned their sadness back into joy. He is the God of life, and he can always bring hope, even out of our saddest moments.

devotional.bigIdea: A boy fell asleep and fell out a window, but God brought him back to life and turned everyone's sadness into joy. God is the God of life and hope.
questions: Eutychus got sleepy during a long talk. How can you pay attention when you learn about God?
The sad night became happy again. When has God turned something sad into something happy for you?
What was the most surprising part of this story?

connections.title: Jairus's Daughter
connections.why: Like Jesus with Jairus's daughter, God brings a young person back to life and turns grief into joy.

connections.title: The Shunammite Woman's Faith
connections.why: Just as Elisha stretched himself over a boy and God restored his life, Paul throws himself over Eutychus and the boy lives.

connections.title: Come Out
connections.why: From Elisha to Jesus to Paul, God keeps showing that death is never the end of the story for those he loves.

quiz.question: What happened to Eutychus while Paul preached?
options: He fell asleep and fell out the window
He sang a song
He left

quiz.question: What did Paul do for him?
options: Helped him, and he came back to life
Kept preaching
Got angry

quiz.question: How did the night end?
options: Happy, with a meal and more teaching
Sad
Everyone went home

stories.title: Shipwrecked but Safe
stories.hook: Paul was on a ship in a terrible storm. Everyone was scared, but God had given Paul a promise that they would all be safe.
story: Paul was being taken on a big ship across the sea, all the way to the great city of Rome.
But not long into the trip, the weather turned terrible. A furious wind, the kind sailors feared the most, caught the ship and pushed it helplessly across the sea.
The storm was so bad that the sailors threw the ship's cargo overboard to make it lighter.
Day after day went by with no sun and no stars to be seen, and the wild wind never stopped blowing.
At last, everyone on board gave up all hope. They were sure they were going to be lost in the storm.
But then Paul stood up among the frightened people. "Keep up your courage!" he said.
"Last night, an angel of God stood beside me," Paul told them. "The angel said, Do not be afraid, Paul. And God will keep safe everyone who is sailing with you."
"So keep up your courage," Paul said, "because I trust God. It will happen just as he told me. Not one of us will be lost!"
There were two hundred and seventy-six people on that ship. And in the middle of the worst storm of their lives, Paul was the calmest one of all, because he trusted God's promise.
Finally, after many days, the ship came near land. But it crashed onto a sandbar and started to break apart in the pounding waves.
So Paul told everyone to head for the shore. Those who could swim jumped in first. The others grabbed onto planks and broken pieces of the ship and floated in.
And it happened just as God had promised! Every single one of the two hundred and seventy-six people reached the land safely.
The ship was gone, but not one life was lost. God had kept his word, and Paul's trust in God had given courage to everyone around him.

devotional.bigIdea: Everyone on the ship was scared, but Paul trusted God's promise and stayed calm. His courage helped everyone else. When we trust God, we can help others be brave too.
questions: Everyone was scared, but Paul was calm. What helps you feel brave when you are scared?
Paul's trust helped others be brave. How could trusting God help the people around you?
God kept his promise to keep everyone safe. How does it feel that God keeps his promises?

connections.title: Jesus Calms the Storm
connections.why: Another terrifying storm at sea, and again God shows that the wind and waves cannot defeat his plans for his people.

connections.title: The Man Who Ran From God
connections.why: Like Jonah, Paul is on a storm-tossed ship, but this time God uses the storm to carry his servant exactly where he is meant to go.

connections.title: Singing at Midnight
connections.why: Just as Paul kept his faith and courage in a prison, here he stays calm and full of hope in the middle of a deadly storm.

quiz.question: What happened to Paul's ship?
options: It was caught in a terrible storm
It sailed smoothly
It stayed in port

quiz.question: What did God promise Paul?
options: Everyone would be kept safe
The storm would last forever
He would be alone

quiz.question: What happened to the people in the end?
options: They all reached land safely
They were lost
They stayed on the ship
